title: 6000: Sin and salvation
content:
• 6010: Sin
  • 6100: Aspects of sin
  • 6500: Salvation
  • 6600: Aspects of salvation

title: 6010: Sin
content:
• 6020: sin
  • 6040: sinners

title: 6020: sin
content: Primarily a wrong relationship with God, which may express itself in wrong attitudes or actions towards God himself, other human beings, possessions or the environment. Scripture stresses that this condition is deeply rooted in human nature, and that only God is able to break its penalty, power and presence.

title: 6021: sin, nature of
content:
Scripture portrays sin in terms of wrongness before God in a variety of different ways, such as uncleanness, guilt or rebellion.

The basic nature of sin

All sin is directed against God

PSA 51:4 See also GEN 13:13; EXO 10:16; JDG 10:10; PSA 41:4; LUK 15:18

Sin is essentially a lack of faith in God

ROM 14:23 See also HEB 11:6

Descriptions of sin

Corruption of God's good purposes

DEU 32:5 See also GEN 6:11–6:12; DEU 9:12, DEU 31:29; JDG 2:19

Doing evil in God's sight

JHN 3:19–3:20 See also JDG 2:11; PSA 34:12–34:16; PRO 8:13; ISA 59:6–59:7; MAT 12:35 parallel LUK 6:45

Ungodliness

JUD 1:14–1:15 See also ISA 9:17, ISA 32:6; 1TI 1:9

Rebellion against God's authority

ISA 30:9 See also DEU 9:7; 1SA 15:23; PSA 78:40, PSA 78:56; JER 3:13; HOS 7:13

The breaking or transgression of God's laws

1JN 3:4 See also 1SA 13:13–13:14; 1CH 10:13; NEH 9:29; MIC 1:5, MIC 7:18; ROM 2:23, ROM 4:15, ROM 5:14–5:17; JAS 2:10–2:11

Straying from the right path

ISA 53:6 See also PSA 58:3, PSA 95:10, PSA 119:10, PSA 119:21, PSA 119:118

Incurring a debt

MAT 6:12 See also MAT 18:21–18:35

Falling short of a standard

ROM 3:23 The words most commonly used for “sin” in the OT and NT originally signified “missing the mark”.

Uncleanness

PSA 51:2; ISA 1:16 See also PSA 51:7; HEB 9:14

Kinds of sin

Sins of omission

JAS 4:17 See also MAT 23:23 parallel LUK 11:42; MAT 25:45

Deliberate sins

LUK 12:47 See also NUM 15:30–15:31; DEU 1:42–1:43, DEU 17:12; PSA 19:13; ISA 57:17; ROM 1:32

Unintentional sins

LUK 12:48 See also LEV 4:1–4:5; NUM 15:22–15:29; DEU 4:41–4:42; ACT 3:17; 1TI 1:13

Sin against the Holy Spirit

MRK 3:29–3:30 parallels MAT 12:31–12:32, LUK 12:10 See also 1JN 5:16

resource.label: 4811: darkness, as a symbol of sin

title: 6022: sin, causes of
content:
Sin is the result of the fall, at which the creation rebels against God its Creator.

Sin as a result of the devil's activity

The devil's instigation of the first sin

GEN 3:13 See also GEN 3:1–3:6; 2CO 11:3; REV 12:9

The devil's role as tempter

1TH 3:5 See also MAT 4:1–4:11 parallels MRK 1:12–1:13, LUK 4:1–4:13; MAT 6:13

The devil as the source of sinful behaviour

JHN 8:44 See also JHN 8:38, JHN 8:41; 1JN 3:8, 1JN 3:10

The sins of angels

2PE 2:4 See also JUD 1:6

Sin as a power in the world

Sin's reign from the time of Adam

ROM 5:12 See also GEN 4:7; JHN 8:34; ROM 5:21, ROM 6:16

Sin uses the law to provoke sinful desires

ROM 7:5 See also ROM 7:7–7:12; 1CO 15:56

The world is under sin's dominion

1JN 2:16 See also LUK 21:34; TIT 2:12

Sin is rooted in human nature

The human heart is dominated by sin

JER 17:9 See also MAT 15:19 parallel MRK 7:21–7:22; ROM 1:24; HEB 3:12

Human nature is fundamentally opposed to God

ROM 8:5–8:8 See also ROM 7:14–7:25; GAL 5:17–5:21; EPH 2:3; 2PE 2:10, 2PE 2:18

The act of sinning

Sin results from giving in to evil desires

JAS 1:14–1:15 See also ROM 13:13–13:14; JAS 4:1; JUD 1:16

Sin results from the human desire to be like God

GEN 3:5 See also ISA 14:12–14:14; EZK 28:2

Sin results when the body is placed at sin's disposal

ROM 6:13 See also MAT 5:29–5:30, MAT 18:8–18:9 parallel MRK 9:43–9:47

Sin results from the influence of others

1CO 15:33 See also EXO 23:33; 1KI 11:3; PRO 1:10, PRO 22:24–22:25

The seriousness of leading others into sin

MRK 9:42 parallels MAT 18:6, LUK 17:1–17:2 See also 1CO 8:9–8:13

title: 6023: sin, universality of
content:
All human beings sin and are guilty in the sight of God on account of an inherently sinful disposition, which can be traced back to Adam. Acts of sin thus arise from a sinful human heart. The basis of cleansing and cancellation of “original sin” is the atoning death of Jesus Christ.

Adam, the cause of universal sin

ROM 5:19 See also GEN 3:1–3:24; ROM 5:12; 1CO 15:22

The universe is in subjection to sin

GAL 3:22 See also ROM 5:21, ROM 11:32

Sin is inherent in human nature

Sinfulness from birth

PSA 51:5 See also JOB 25:4; PSA 58:3

The sinful heart

GEN 6:5 See also GEN 8:21; ECC 9:3; JER 17:9; MAT 15:19 parallel MRK 7:21

The sinful nature

EPH 2:3 See also ROM 8:6–8:8; GAL 5:19–5:21

The universality of sin

No-one is righteous

ROM 3:9–3:19 See also PSA 5:9, PSA 10:7, PSA 14:1–14:3 parallel PSA 53:1–53:3; PSA 36:1, PSA 140:3; ISA 59:7–59:8

All have sinned

ROM 3:23 See also GEN 6:11–6:12; 1KI 8:46 parallel 2CH 6:36; ECC 7:20; ISA 53:6; ROM 1:18–1:32; JAS 3:2

Everyone is sinful in God's sight

1JN 1:8 See also PSA 130:3, PSA 143:2; PRO 20:9; ISA 64:6–64:7; MAT 19:17 parallels MRK 10:18, LUK 18:19; 1JN 1:10

Some characters in Scripture are described as being blameless in comparison with their contemporaries

GEN 5:24 Enoch; ; GEN 6:9 Noah; ; 1KI 15:5 David

Jesus Christ alone is sinless

HEB 4:15; 1PE 2:22 See also ISA 53:9; LUK 23:47; 2CO 5:21; HEB 7:26; 1JN 3:5

title: 6024: sin, effects of
content:
Sin affects every level of human existence, including the sinner's relationship with God, with other human beings and with the environment.

The effects of sin on individuals

Lack of peace of mind

ISA 57:20–57:21 See also JOB 15:20–15:35; PSA 38:5–38:8; PRO 13:15–13:22; LAM 1:20–1:21

Bondage to a continuing habit of sin

JHN 8:34 See also PRO 5:22; ROM 6:16; 2TI 2:16

Physical death

1CO 15:56 See also GEN 2:17, GEN 3:19; PRO 21:16; ROM 5:12–5:14, ROM 6:21–6:23; 1CO 15:22; JAS 1:15

The sinful life is equivalent to death

EPH 2:1 See also ROM 7:9, ROM 7:13, ROM 8:10; COL 2:13

The effects of sin on the sinner before God

Uncleanness

ISA 64:6 See also PSA 106:39; ISA 6:5; JER 2:22; LAM 1:8; MAT 15:18–15:20 parallel MRK 7:20–7:23

Guilt

EZR 9:6 The prayer of Ezra. See also GEN 3:10; PSA 38:3–38:4, PSA 44:15; ISA 59:12–59:13; JER 3:25, JER 14:20

Separation from God

ISA 59:2 See also DEU 31:18; ISA 1:15, ISA 64:7; EZK 8:6; HOS 5:6; MIC 3:4; EPH 2:12

The effects of sin on Israel

PRO 14:34 See also JOS 7:1–7:16 Achan's sin; 1KI 8:33–8:40 the consequences of sin for Israel; ISA 1:4–1:9 the plight of Judah; ROM 1:21–1:32 the condition of humanity

The effects of sin on the world

The ground cursed

GEN 3:17–3:18 See also JER 12:13; ROM 8:20–8:22

The land polluted

LEV 18:25 See also GEN 4:10–4:12; NUM 35:33–35:34; PSA 106:38; ISA 24:4–24:6; JER 3:1

title: 6025: sin, and God's character
content:
In his righteousness and holiness, God detests sin and its effects upon humanity. In his mercy and grace, he makes available a means of atonement, by the death and resurrection of Jesus Christ.

God's character and sin

God himself is perfect

HAB 1:13; 1JN 1:5 See also DEU 32:4; JOS 24:19; PSA 97:2; ISA 6:3

Jesus Christ is sinless

1PE 2:22 See also ISA 53:9; 2CO 5:21; HEB 1:9, HEB 7:26–7:27; 1JN 3:5

God's people are to be holy

1PE 1:15–1:16 See also LEV 11:44, LEV 11:45, LEV 19:2, LEV 20:7; MAT 5:48; 1TH 4:7; 1JN 3:3

God's attitude to sin

God knows all sin

JER 16:17 See also JOB 10:14; PSA 139:1–139:4; JER 2:22; HOS 7:2; AMO 5:12; HEB 4:13

God grieves over sin

GEN 6:6 See also ISA 63:10; EPH 4:30

God hates sin

PSA 11:5 See also DEU 25:16; 2SA 11:27; PSA 5:5; PRO 6:16–6:19; ZEC 8:17; LUK 16:15

Sin provokes God's anger

ROM 1:18 See also 2CH 36:16; EZK 20:8; AMO 1:3; JHN 3:36; EPH 5:5–5:6; COL 3:5–3:6

God is also merciful and gracious

EXO 34:6–34:7 See also NEH 9:17, NEH 9:31; PSA 78:38, PSA 103:8–103:14; LAM 3:22–3:23; MIC 7:18–7:19; ROM 11:32

God's patience with sinners

2PE 3:9 See also ROM 2:4, ROM 9:22; 1TI 1:16

Jesus Christ is the supreme revelation of God's love for sinners

Jesus Christ's ministry of forgiveness

1TI 1:15 See also MAT 9:2 parallels MRK 2:5, LUK 5:20–5:21; LUK 7:36–7:50, LUK 15:1–15:10, LUK 19:5–19:10; JHN 8:1–8:11

God's love shown in Jesus Christ's death

ROM 5:8 See also JHN 3:16–3:17; EPH 2:4; 1PE 3:18; 1JN 4:9–4:10

The risen Christ gives grace to sinful people

1JN 2:1 See also ROM 8:34; HEB 2:17–2:18, HEB 4:15–5:2

title: 6026: sin, God's judgment on
content:
Sin comes under the judgment of God, in that it contradicts his nature and opposes his purposes.

God's judgment on sin

God is the judge of sin

ISA 26:21 See also PSA 99:8; ISA 13:11; AMO 3:14; ZEP 1:12; 1TH 4:6

God's judgment is certain

ROM 2:12 See also PSA 37:38; PRO 11:21; HEB 9:27

God's judgment is in proportion to the seriousness of the sin

JER 21:14 See also DEU 25:2; ISA 59:18; MAT 7:1–7:5; LUK 12:47–12:48; ROM 2:5–2:6

God's judgment under the Sinaitic covenant

EXO 20:5 parallel DEU 5:9 The Sinaitic covenant makes references to the judgment of future generations for the sins of their parents but, as a principle, this is abolished by later prophets. LEV 26:14–26:39; ; DEU 28:15–28:68; JER 31:29–31:30; ; EZK 18:1–18:32

John the Baptist and Jesus Christ warn of judgment

MAT 3:10 parallel LUK 3:9 See also MAT 3:12, MAT 7:19, MAT 12:36–12:37; MRK 9:42–9:49

Ways in which God judges sin

God causes sin to bring evil to the sinner

2CH 6:23 See also DEU 19:19; EST 7:10; PSA 7:15–7:16; ISA 30:13

God abandons people

ISA 64:7 See also DEU 31:17–31:18; JOS 7:11–7:12; LAM 4:16; EZK 39:23–39:24

Unrepentant sinners to be expelled from the church

MAT 18:17 See also 1CO 5:1–5:13

God exercises judgment through a country's legal system

1PE 2:14 See also ROM 13:3–13:4

Untimely death is God's judgment on all sin

GEN 2:17 See also LEV 20:1–20:17; 1CH 10:13; PRO 11:19; ROM 1:32; 1CO 11:29–11:30

Sinners are excluded from the kingdom of God

REV 22:15 See also 1CO 6:9–6:10; GAL 5:9–5:21; REV 21:27

Occasions of God's judgment on sin

OT examples

GEN 3:16–3:24 expulsion from Eden; ; GEN 6:1–7:24 the flood; ; GEN 18:20–19:29 Sodom and Gomorrah; ; DEU 9:1–9:5 The Canaanites (the Anakites were earlier inhabitants of Canaan).; 2KI 17:7–17:23, 2KI 24:10–24:20

The destruction of Jerusalem

LUK 11:47–11:51, LUK 13:34–13:35

God's final judgment of the world

REV 21:8 See also ISA 66:24; MAT 25:31–25:46; 2TH 1:8–1:9; 2PE 3:7; JUD 1:7

title: 6027: sin, God's remedy for
content:
Under the old covenant, sin was forgiven through sacrifice, prefiguring the atoning death of Jesus Christ, which brings forgiveness of sins under the new covenant.

Atonement for sins in the OT was through the shedding of blood

LEV 17:11

Kinds of sin offering in the OT

The sin offering was for unintentional sins

LEV 4:1–5:13 See also NUM 15:22–15:31

The guilt offering was for unintentional sins, where restitution was required

LEV 5:14–6:7 See also NUM 5:5–5:10

The annual Day of Atonement cleansed the nation of sin

LEV 16:1–16:34 See also EXO 30:11–30:16; LEV 23:26–23:32; HEB 9:7

The priests made atonement on occasions of deliberate national sin

NUM 16:46–16:48 Aaron stops the plague after Korah's sin; ; NUM 25:13 Phinehas stops the plague after Moab caused Israel to sin.

Sacrifices of atonement needed to be accompanied by repentance and a willingness to obey

Worshippers recognised the need for repentance and obedience

PRO 21:3 See also PSA 40:6–40:8, PSA 51:16–51:17

The prophets declared the need for obedience

1SA 15:22 See also ISA 1:11–1:17; JER 7:21–7:23; HOS 6:6; MIC 6:6–6:8

The prophets warned of judgment to bring people to repentance

2KI 17:13 See also ISA 31:6–31:7; JER 4:1–4:4, JER 35:15; EZK 3:16–3:19; JON 3:4–3:10

The death of Jesus Christ brings forgiveness for sin

Jesus Christ died on behalf of sinful humanity

ROM 5:6 See also MAT 26:26–26:28; JHN 10:11, JHN 15:13; GAL 2:20

The early church proclaimed that Christ died for the sins of others

1CO 15:3 See also ROM 4:25; GAL 1:4; 1PE 3:18

Jesus Christ bore sin on the cross

2CO 5:21 See also ISA 53:10–53:12; HEB 9:28; 1PE 2:24

Jesus Christ has redeemed people by taking their place

MAT 20:28 parallel MRK 10:45 See also GAL 3:13; 1TI 2:6; TIT 2:14

Jesus Christ's death is sacrificial

ROM 3:25 See also ROM 8:3; 1CO 5:7; EPH 5:2; HEB 7:27, HEB 10:5–10:13; 1JN 2:2, 1JN 4:10

The shedding of Jesus Christ's blood brings forgiveness

1JN 1:7 See also JHN 1:29; EPH 1:7; HEB 9:12–9:22, HEB 13:12; 1PE 1:18–1:19; REV 7:14

title: 6028: sin, God's deliverance from
content:
The gospel reveals the purpose and power of God to deal with sin and all of its effects. Scripture uses a range of images to express the comprehensiveness of salvation.

God's removal of sin

Atonement for sin

ISA 6:7 See also EXO 32:30; LEV 4:27–4:31; PRO 16:6; ROM 3:25; HEB 2:17

Forgiveness of sin

MIC 7:18; ACT 13:38 See also 1KI 8:35–8:36; 2CH 30:18–30:20; PSA 103:2–103:3; ISA 33:24, ISA 55:7; JOL 3:21; MAT 26:27–26:28; LUK 24:46–24:47; EPH 1:7; 1JN 1:9

Cancellation of a debt

MAT 6:12 See also MAT 18:21–18:35; LUK 7:41–7:50

A covering over of sin

1PE 4:8 There is a close relation between “covering over sin” and “atoning for sin”. See also PSA 32:1, PSA 85:2; JAS 5:20

The taking away of sin

PSA 103:12 See also 2SA 12:13; ISA 6:6–6:7; ZEC 3:4; JHN 1:29; HEB 9:28; 1JN 3:5

Remembering sin no more

ISA 43:25 See also PSA 25:7; JER 31:33–31:34; 2CO 5:19

God's deliverance for the sinner

The salvation of the sinner

1TI 1:15 See also PSA 28:8–28:9; MAT 1:21; LUK 19:9–19:10; JHN 3:17; HEB 7:25

The image of healing

LUK 5:31–5:32 parallels MAT 9:12, MRK 2:17 See also 2CH 7:14; ISA 53:5, ISA 57:18–57:19; HOS 14:4; 1PE 2:24

The image of cleansing

PSA 51:2 See also LEV 16:30; EZK 36:25; JHN 13:1–13:11; ACT 22:16; HEB 10:22; 1JN 1:9

Redemption by God

PSA 130:8 See also ISA 44:22; TIT 2:14; 1PE 1:18–1:19

Justification before God

GAL 2:16 See also ISA 53:11; ROM 3:24–3:26, ROM 4:5, ROM 4:25, ROM 5:16–5:19, ROM 8:33

Freedom from condemnation

ROM 8:1 See also JHN 3:18, JHN 8:3–8:11; ROM 8:34

Peace with God

ROM 5:1 See also ISA 53:5; LUK 2:14; EPH 2:17

Reconciliation with God

2CO 5:18 See also ROM 5:9–5:11; COL 1:19–1:20

Sanctification to God

HEB 10:10 See also 1CO 6:11; EPH 5:25–5:26; COL 1:22

Freedom from sin and the sinful nature

ROM 7:24; 1PE 2:24 See also ROM 6:1–6:18, ROM 8:1–8:9; GAL 5:24

A transition from death to life

COL 2:13 See also LUK 15:22–15:24; EPH 2:4–2:5

Receiving eternal life

ROM 6:23 See also JHN 3:16, JHN 3:36, JHN 5:24

title: 6029: sin, accepting forgiveness of
content:
Sinners must respond to God's offer of forgiveness through faith in Jesus Christ.

The conviction of sin

LAM 1:20; JHN 16:8–16:9 See also 1KI 8:38–8:40; ISA 6:5; EZK 33:10–33:11; ACT 16:29

The inward response of faith

ACT 16:31 See also MRK 1:14–1:15; JHN 3:36, JHN 5:24; ACT 13:38–13:39, ACT 16:25–16:34; ROM 3:22–3:26, ROM 10:8–10:10; EPH 2:8

The outward response of baptism

ACT 22:16 See also MRK 1:4–1:5 parallels MAT 3:1–3:6, LUK 3:2–3:6; ACT 2:38, ACT 8:36; COL 2:11–2:12; 1PE 3:21

Confession of sin

PRO 28:13; 1JN 1:9 See also LEV 16:20–16:22, LEV 26:40–26:42; 2SA 12:13; PSA 32:3–32:5; LAM 3:40; LUK 15:17–15:20; ACT 19:18

Repentance

Turning towards God

ACT 3:19 See also 2CH 6:36–6:39; ISA 55:7; EZK 18:21; MAT 3:1–3:2 parallels MRK 1:4, LUK 3:2; ACT 17:30; 2CO 7:10; 1TH 1:9

Turning away from sin

JHN 8:11 See also JER 4:3–4:4; LUK 19:1–19:10; JHN 5:14; ROM 6:11–6:14; 1PE 2:11

The making of restitution

LUK 19:8 See also LEV 6:1–6:7; NUM 5:6–5:8; PRO 6:30–6:31; EZK 33:12–33:16

The forgiveness of others

LUK 11:4 parallel MAT 6:12 See also MAT 6:14–6:15, MAT 18:21–18:35; MRK 11:25; EPH 4:32; COL 3:13

title: 6030: sin, avoidance of
content:
God calls his people to avoid sin, and through Jesus Christ gives them the inner power to be victorious over it.

God's people are to resist sin

1PE 2:11 See also PSA 97:10; PRO 4:23–4:27; 1CO 15:34; EPH 4:25–5:20; JAS 1:21

The Christian life is a constant struggle against sin

HEB 3:13 See also ACT 20:28; ROM 7:14–7:25; EPH 6:10–6:18; 1PE 5:8–5:9

God helps his people to resist sin

Release from sin through Jesus Christ's death

1PE 2:24 See also ROM 6:1–6:7; GAL 2:20, GAL 5:24; COL 2:11–2:12

The avoidance of sin through new life in Jesus Christ

1JN 3:9 See also 2CO 5:17; 1PE 1:23; 1JN 3:6, 1JN 5:18

Believers co-operate with Jesus Christ to avoid sin

PHP 2:12–2:13

Believers are to put to death what is sinful in them

COL 3:5 See also ROM 6:11–6:14, ROM 8:13

Believers are to exchange sinful for righteous behaviour

ROM 13:12–13:14 The words used here, and in similar passages, are those used for “taking off” old clothes and “putting on” new ones. See also EPH 4:22–4:24; COL 3:7–3:10; 1TI 6:11; 2TI 2:22

Believers are to allow the Spirit to inform and direct their conduct

ROM 12:2 See also ROM 8:5–8:8; GAL 5:16–5:25

Practical steps for overcoming sin and temptation

Meditation on Scripture

PSA 119:11 See also PSA 18:22–18:23; MAT 4:1–4:11 parallel LUK 4:1–4:13; 2TI 3:16–3:17

Prayerful dependence upon God

MAT 6:13 parallel LUK 11:4 See also PSA 19:13; MAT 26:41; 1CO 10:13; HEB 4:15–4:16

Active seeking of the good

ROM 6:19 See also PSA 34:14; ISA 1:16–1:17; AMO 5:14–5:15; 1TH 5:22; 3JN 1:11

Incentives for avoiding sin

The fear of God

PRO 16:6 See also EXO 20:20; PRO 3:7, PRO 8:13

The holiness of God

1PE 1:15–1:16 See also LEV 11:44–11:45, LEV 19:2, LEV 20:7; 1CO 6:18–6:20; 1TH 4:7

The expectation of Jesus Christ's return

1PE 4:7 See also 2CO 5:9–5:10; 1TH 5:4–5:6; 2PE 3:10–3:14

A consideration of the consequences of sin

GAL 6:7–6:8 See also MRK 9:42–9:48; ROM 6:21–6:23; HEB 6:7–6:8, HEB 10:26–10:31

The need to be a good witness to unbelievers

1PE 2:15 See also 1PE 3:1–3:2, 1PE 3:15–3:16

The role of others in avoiding sin

Bad company is to be avoided

1CO 15:33 See also DEU 7:1–7:4; PSA 1:1; PRO 1:10; 1CO 5:1–5:13

A good example is to be followed

1CO 11:1 See also PHP 3:17, PHP 4:9; HEB 12:1–12:3; 1PE 4:1–4:3

Believers are to support one another

JAS 5:19–5:20 See also MAT 18:15–18:17; GAL 6:1–6:2; 1TI 5:20; HEB 3:12

title: 6040: sinners
content:
All who have sinned. The term tends to be used in particular of those regarded as outside God's covenant of grace, whose sins are therefore not forgiven.

The universality of sinful behaviour

Only Jesus Christ is sinless

1PE 2:22 See also ISA 53:9; 2CO 5:21; HEB 4:15, HEB 7:26; 1JN 3:5

Everyone else has sinned

GAL 3:22 See also 1KI 8:46; ROM 3:9–3:19, ROM 3:23, ROM 5:12, ROM 5:19

God's mercy towards sinners

God graciously extends mercy to sinners within the covenant

PSA 25:8–25:10 See also 1KI 8:30; PSA 86:5, PSA 130:8; DAN 9:9; 1JN 1:9

Jesus Christ died for sinners

ROM 5:8; 1TI 1:15 See also ROM 8:3

Sinners as those whose behaviour excludes them from God's covenant

Prostitutes, tax collectors, idolatrous Gentiles and the ungodly

LUK 15:1 See also DEU 23:17–23:18; 1SA 15:18; LUK 3:12, LUK 7:37–7:38; GAL 2:15; 1TH 4:5; 1PE 4:18; PRO 11:31; 2PE 2:5–2:6

Such sinners shunned by the Jews

LUK 7:39 See also PSA 1:1; PRO 1:10–1:16

The judgment of sinners

Prayers seeking their judgment

PSA 104:35 See also PSA 109:6–109:20, PSA 137:7–137:9, PSA 139:19

They are cut off from God

ISA 59:2 See also PSA 66:18; PRO 15:29; ISA 1:15; JHN 9:31; 1CO 6:9–6:10

They receive their just reward

PRO 13:21–13:22 See also PSA 1:5, PSA 34:16, PSA 37:38; ECC 2:26

Prophecies of their judgment

ISA 1:28 See also ISA 13:9, ISA 33:14; JER 4:18; AMO 9:10

Their final judgment

2PE 3:7 See also ISA 66:24; 2TH 1:8–1:9; 2PE 2:9; JUD 1:15

Jesus Christ's attitude towards sinners

Jesus Christ's association with sinners

MAT 9:9–9:13 parallels MRK 2:14–2:17, LUK 5:27–5:32 Jesus Christ's attitude and behaviour were radically different from that of his contemporaries. See also LUK 15:1–15:10, LUK 19:5–19:10; ROM 5:8; 1TI 1:15–1:16

Jesus Christ extended God's forgiveness to sinners and welcomed them into his kingdom

MAT 21:31–21:32 See also MAT 9:2–9:7 parallels MRK 2:3–2:12, LUK 5:18–5:25; LUK 7:40–7:50, LUK 15:11–15:32, LUK 18:9–18:14, LUK 19:9; JHN 8:3–8:11

Jesus Christ's behaviour led to accusations that he himself was a sinner

JHN 9:16, JHN 9:24

Jesus Christ's refusal to accept sinful behaviour

JHN 8:10–8:11 See also MAT 4:17, MAT 18:8–18:9 parallel MRK 9:43–9:48; JHN 5:14

Jesus Christ's use of the example of sinners to teach his disciples

LUK 6:32–6:34 parallel MAT 5:46–5:47 See also LUK 16:1–16:9

Sinners in the church

Those who fall into sin should be restored

JAS 5:19–5:20 See also PSA 51:13; MAT 18:12–18:15; GAL 6:1

Unrepentant sinners should be expelled

MAT 18:15–18:17 See also 1CO 5:9–5:11

title: 6103: abomination
content:
That which is utterly detestable in the sight of God, particularly sin and idolatry. Daniel predicts the establishment of an “abomination that causes desolation” on the temple mount and Jesus Christ reiterates this prophecy.

Sin is an abomination

God detests it

LEV 20:13; PRO 26:24–26:26 See also LEV 18:26–18:30; PRO 6:16–6:19, PRO 28:9; ISA 66:3–66:4

The abomination of Babylon the Great

REV 17:3–17:6 See also JER 51:6–51:8; REV 14:8, REV 19:1–19:2

Idolatry is an abomination

JER 32:32–32:35 DEU 7:25–7:26, DEU 12:31, DEU 18:9–18:13, DEU 27:15, DEU 29:17–29:18; DEU 32:15–32:16; 1KI 11:4–11:8; ; 2KI 23:13; ISA 44:19; ; JER 4:1–4:2, JER 7:30, JER 16:18; ; EZK 5:9, EZK 7:20, EZK 8:5–8:18 idolatry in the temple; ; EZK 11:17–11:21, EZK 20:30; ; HOS 9:10; ; 1PE 4:3

Unclean animals are an abomination

ISA 66:17 See also LEV 7:21, LEV 11:13–11:20, LEV 11:41–11:42; ISA 65:2–65:5; REV 18:2

The abomination that causes desolation

DAN 9:25–9:27 See also DAN 11:29–11:32, DAN 12:11–12:12; MAT 24:15–24:21 parallel MRK 13:14–13:19; REV 13:14–13:15

title: 6106: addiction
content:
The devotion of a person to something, whether sin in general or food, alcohol or wealth in particular, so as to become dependent upon it. A person will never be satisfied in such a state of slavery. The answer to addiction is self-control through the resources of the new life in Christ.

Addiction is the consequence of sin

2PE 2:19 See also JHN 8:34

Addiction to food, alcohol or drugs is to become dependent upon them

PHP 3:18–3:19 See also ISA 5:11, ISA 28:7–28:8, ISA 56:12; TIT 2:3

Addiction to wealth is to be controlled by it

MRK 10:21–10:22 parallel LUK 18:22–18:23 See also MAT 26:14–26:16 parallels MRK 14:10–14:11, LUK 22:3–22:6; ACT 16:19, ACT 24:26; 2PE 2:15

Addiction never satisfies

Addiction to sinful habits does not satisfy

ROM 6:20–6:21

Addiction to food, alcohol or drugs does not satisfy

ECC 6:7 See also PRO 20:1, PRO 21:17, PRO 23:20–23:21, PRO 23:29–23:35; HOS 4:10–4:11 parallel MAT 19:21–19:22

Addiction to wealth does not satisfy

ECC 5:10 See also 1TI 6:9–6:10; JAS 5:1–5:3

The answer to addiction: self-control through the new life in Christ

PRO 23:1–23:4; ROM 6:6–6:7, ROM 6:11–6:14 See also PRO 25:16; ROM 6:16–6:19, ROM 8:12–8:15; 1CO 6:12, 1CO 9:27; GAL 5:22–5:23

title: 6109: alienation
content:
A state of being separate or apart from something or someone, often accompanied by an awareness of the separation. Human beings are alienated from God on account of sin. Alienation is also encountered in other areas of life, including relations between believers, and between believers and secular society.

Alienation from God because of sin

Sin causes separation from God

GAL 5:4 Those seeking to justify themselves cut themselves off from Jesus Christ's saving work; COL 1:21 See also ISA 59:1–59:2; EZK 39:23–39:24

Seen in discomfort in God's presence

GEN 3:8–3:10 See also ISA 6:5; LUK 5:8

Seen in defeat in battle

DEU 1:42 See also NUM 14:41–14:45; JOS 7:11–7:12; JDG 2:11–2:15

Seen in unanswered prayer

ISA 59:1–59:2 See also DEU 1:45; PSA 66:18; PRO 1:28–1:30; ISA 1:15

Seen in unaccepted worship

ISA 1:11–1:14; ; AMO 5:21–5:23; ; MAL 1:10

Seen in lost closeness with God

GEN 3:23–3:24; ; EXO 33:3; ; ISA 64:7; ; EZK 8:6; ; HOS 5:6; ; JON 1:3

A sense of alienation from God

Feelings of alienation

PSA 22:1; ISA 49:14 See also PSA 39:12, PSA 42:3–42:4, PSA 42:9–42:10, PSA 44:23–44:24; ISA 40:27; LAM 2:1; MAT 27:46 parallel MRK 15:34 Jesus Christ's alienation from God as he bore the consequences of human sin was felt and actual.

The reassurance of God's concern

ISA 49:15–49:16 See also ISA 43:1–43:5, ISA 44:21, ISA 54:7; JER 31:20

Alienation from others

Alienation caused by a third party

PRO 16:28 See also PRO 17:9; GAL 4:17 False teachers in Galatia tried to drive a wedge between Paul and the church.

Examples of alienation

GEN 3:7 Man and woman; ; GEN 4:3–4:8 Cain and Abel; ; GEN 27:41 Jacob and Esau; ; 1SA 18:8–18:9 Saul and David; ; JOB 19:13–19:19 Job and his family and friends; ; PSA 69:8 the psalmist and his family; ; JER 12:6 Jeremiah and his family; ; ACT 15:39 Paul and Barnabas; ; EPH 2:12 Jew and Gentile

Overcoming alienation

Being reconciled to God

COL 1:22 See also ROM 5:11; 2CO 5:18–5:20; 1PE 2:25

Reconciliation with others

COL 3:13 See also LEV 19:18; MAT 5:23–5:24

Examples of reconciliation

GEN 33:4 Jacob and Esau; ; GEN 50:19–50:21 Joseph and his brothers; ; EPH 2:14–2:16 Jew and Gentile

God's people are aliens in the world

JHN 15:18–15:19 See also JHN 17:16; PHP 3:20; HEB 11:13–11:16; 1PE 2:11

Responsibility to the alien

God's concern for the outsider

PSA 146:9 See also DEU 10:18; JER 30:17

The example of Jesus Christ

MAT 9:10–9:11 parallels MRK 2:15–2:16, LUK 5:29–5:30 Jesus Christ welcomed those who had been alienated by the rest of society. See also MAT 8:2–8:3 parallels MRK 1:40–1:42, LUK 5:12–5:13 Jesus Christ touched the “untouchable” man with leprosy; MAT 11:19 parallel LUK 7:34

Equal treatment for the alien

LEV 19:34 See also EXO 12:48–12:49; LEV 24:22

Ensuring justice for the alien

DEU 24:17 The weaker members of Israelite society are of special concern. See also EXO 22:21; JER 22:3; ZEC 7:9–7:10

Special provision for the alien

LEV 23:22 God makes special provision for the poor so that they will have a share in the blessings of the land. See also LEV 19:10; DEU 24:20, DEU 14:28–14:29 The third year's tithe is shared with those without inheritance in the land.

resource.label: 8269: holiness, as separation from the worldly

title: 6112: banishment
content:
A form of punishment in which the guilty party is expelled from a region and forbidden to return. An important example is the banishment of Adam and Eve from Eden as a result of their disobedience to God.

Banishment is a punishment for disobedience

EZR 7:26 See also EXO 12:15, EXO 31:14; LEV 7:20–7:21; NUM 15:30

Banishment by God

Adam and Eve were banished from Eden because of their disobedience

GEN 3:22–3:24

God banished kings as a punishment

Nebuchadnezzar was banished from every human being because of his sin: DAN 4:25, DAN 4:32–4:33; EZK 28:16–28:17 The king of Tyre was banished from God's presence because of his sin.

God punished the people of Judah by banishing them from their land

JER 23:39 God also promised their return (Jer 32:37). See also JER 8:3, JER 24:9, JER 25:10, JER 27:10

At the final judgment, God will banish all evildoers

MAT 7:23 See also MAT 8:12, MAT 22:13, MAT 25:46

Kings used banishment as a punishment

Saul expelled all mediums and spiritists from the land: 1SA 28:3, 1SA 28:9; 1KI 15:12 Asa banished all the shrine-prostitutes from the land.

The psalmists recognised banishment as the severest of punishments

PSA 5:10, PSA 31:22, PSA 37:22, PSA 37:28, PSA 37:34, PSA 37:38, PSA 51:11, PSA 71:9, PSA 125:5; ; JON 2:4 This portion of the book of Jonah is written in the same manner as the Psalms, i.e., as Hebrew poetry.

The church is to use banishment as a means of discipline

1CO 5:12–5:13 See also DEU 17:7, DEU 19:19, DEU 21:21, DEU 22:21, DEU 22:24, DEU 24:7

title: 6115: blame
content:
The state of having been accused, correctly or incorrectly, of doing something wrong. Guilt is felt as a result of having been justly blamed for a crime or wrong against a person. Scripture portrays blaming others as a natural human reaction to events. Blame may often be apportioned by people on the basis of a misunderstanding, whereas God's blame of people is just. Christians should seek to lead blameless lives.

Blaming others: a basic human characteristic since the fall

GEN 3:12–3:13 NUM 20:5, NUM 21:5; ; JOS 22:15–22:18; ; 1SA 22:13–22:15; ; 2SA 3:8; ; 1KI 18:17–18:18; ; NEH 5:4–5:5; ; JOB 4:5–4:7, JOB 8:6, JOB 11:14–11:15; ; JER 38:4; ; JHN 9:1–9:2, JHN 11:21; ; ACT 6:1

Blaming God

JER 15:16–15:18 See also JOB 2:9, JOB 3:23; PSA 13:1–13:2, PSA 22:1–22:2, PSA 44:9–44:26, PSA 60:1–60:3, PSA 74:1–74:23, PSA 79:1–79:5; ISA 40:27

God justly blames his people for their sins

HEB 8:8 See also ROM 9:19

Warnings about falsely attributing blame

PRO 3:30 See also PSA 71:13, PSA 109:18–109:20, PSA 109:29; ROM 14:1–14:4, ROM 15:7

Dealing with blame

By confession

PSA 32:5 See also PSA 51:3–51:7

By forgiveness

LUK 23:34 See also GEN 50:19–50:21; EPH 4:31–4:32

By correcting misunderstandings

JOS 22:13–22:34

God's people should deal gently with those who deserve blame

MAT 18:15 See also JAS 5:19–5:20

God's people should aim to live free from blame

PHP 1:9–1:10 See also GEN 17:1; PSA 19:13; 1CO 1:8; PHP 2:15; 1TH 3:13, 1TH 5:23

title: 6118: blemish
content:
A mark or stain which spoils something or lessens its value. The term is often used symbolically to refer to sin. The OT sacrificial system specified that only unblemished animals should be sacrificed.

A blemish is ritually unacceptable

In animals to be sacrified

DEU 15:21; MAL 1:13–1:14 See also EXO 12:5; LEV 22:20–22:25; DEU 17:1

In priests offering sacrifices

LEV 21:17–21:23

A blemish or stain as a symbol of sin

JER 2:22 See also ISA 59:3; JUD 1:23

Sinful people represented as blemishes

2PE 2:13 See also JUD 1:12

Without blemish: physical or moral perfection

Absalom

2SA 14:25

Daniel and his companions

DAN 1:3–1:4

Jesus Christ

1PE 1:18–1:19 See also HEB 9:12–9:14

The church

EPH 5:27 See also COL 1:22

title: 6121: boasting
content:
Negatively, conceitedly praising oneself. Positively, trusting and exulting in who God is, and what he has done.

Boasting condemned

1JN 2:16 See also PSA 10:2–10:6, PSA 12:3–12:4, PSA 52:1, PSA 94:4; HAB 1:10–1:11; ROM 1:29–1:30; 2TI 3:1–3:2

Boasting exposed as folly

PRO 27:1 See also JDG 9:38–9:40; PRO 25:14; 1CO 3:18–3:23; JAS 4:13–4:16

Salvation by faith means no-one can boast about human merit

EPH 2:8–2:9 See also LUK 18:9–18:14; ROM 2:17–2:24, ROM 3:27, ROM 11:17–11:21; GAL 6:14

Examples of foolish boasting

ISA 10:12–10:14 See also ISA 37:24–37:25; DAN 4:28–4:30 Nebuchadnezzar; DAN 7:8, DAN 7:11, DAN 11:36; ACT 8:9–8:10 Simon the sorcerer; 2TH 2:3–2:4 the antichrist; 2PE 2:17–2:18

Legitimate boasting

Boasting in God

JER 9:23–9:24 See also 1SA 2:1–2:3; PSA 34:2, PSA 44:8; 1CO 1:27–1:31; JER 9:24; 2CO 10:7–10:18; HEB 3:6

Boasting in the results of ministry

1TH 2:19–2:20 See also ROM 15:17–15:19; 1CO 15:31; 2CO 8:24–9:3

Boasting in the conduct of ministry

2CO 1:12–1:14 See also 1CO 9:15; 2CO 11:7–11:12; 2TI 3:10–3:11

title: 6124: condemnation
content: An act of judgment, in which someone rebukes and denounces the actions and motivations of others. Scripture stresses the righteousness and trustworthiness of divine condemnation but notes how human judgments are often tainted and distorted by sin.

title: 6125: condemnation, divine
content:
God's righteous condemnation is directed against human sin and wickedness.

God's wrath causes divine condemnation

ROM 1:18–1:20 See also REV 16:1–16:7

The reality and certainty of divine condemnation

MRK 16:15–16:16; JHN 5:28–5:29 See also MAT 12:36–12:37, MAT 25:31–25:33, MAT 25:41–25:45; 2PE 2:4–2:9

People's awareness of being under divine condemnation

2SA 24:10 See also PSA 32:3–32:5, PSA 51:3–51:4; DAN 9:7–9:14

Examples of divine condemnation

Condemnation of individuals

GEN 3:16–3:19; ; ACT 5:1–5:11; ; REV 20:11–20:15

Condemnation of groups, cities and nations

GEN 11:4–11:9, GEN 19:23–19:25; ; JOL 3:1–3:8, JOL 3:12–3:13

Divine condemnation averted through the work of Jesus Christ

JHN 3:16–3:18, JHN 5:24; ROM 8:1–8:4 See also ROM 8:31–8:34

resource.label: 6717: reconciliation, of the world to God

title: 6126: condemnation, human
content:
The passing of judgment on others. This is valid when authorised by God, but otherwise it is frequently partial, godless and self-protecting, and therefore unacceptable.

Condemnation and the legal process: impartial judgment demanded

EXO 23:6–23:8 See also DEU 1:15–1:17, DEU 17:2–17:7, DEU 19:15; 2CH 19:4–19:7; PRO 17:15

The perversity and injustice of human condemnation

ROM 2:1–2:5 See also 2SA 12:1–12:10; DAN 6:1–6:16

People's false condemnation of Jesus Christ

ACT 13:27–13:28 See also MAT 26:59–26:63 parallel MRK 14:55–14:61

NT warnings against judgment and condemnation

MAT 7:1–7:5 parallels LUK 6:37–6:38, LUK 6:41–6:42 See also LUK 18:9–18:14; JHN 8:3–8:11; ROM 14:1–14:4, ROM 14:9–14:13

God's protection of his people from false condemnation

ISA 50:7–50:9; ; ROM 8:31–8:39

resource.label: 2027: Jesus Christ, grace and mercy of

title: 6130: corruption
content:
A state of spiritual decay and moral dishonesty, arising from the effects of sin, which expresses itself in disobedience towards God.

Corruption of nature as a result of the fall

GEN 6:11

Sinful humanity is corrupt

ISA 1:4; ACT 2:40 See also DEU 32:20; JOB 15:16; PSA 14:1–14:3 parallel PSA 53:1–53:3; PRO 4:24, PRO 6:12; HOS 9:9; PHP 2:15; TIT 1:15–1:16

The gospel breaks the power of corruption

2PE 1:4 See also 2CO 7:1–7:2; 2PE 2:20; JUD 1:23

Examples and causes of corruption

Corruption as injustice

PSA 55:23 See also 2CH 19:7; JOB 5:16; ISA 58:6; EZK 9:9

Disobedience towards God

DEU 31:29 See also DEU 32:5; JDG 2:19

Denying the truth

1TI 6:5

Paganism

EZR 9:11

Mocking justice

PRO 19:28

Taking bribes

See also ECC 7:7

Bad company

1CO 15:33

Careless talk

JAS 3:5–3:6 See also PRO 4:24, PRO 6:12

title: 6133: coveting
content: Desiring to possess something at the expense of the legitimate owner.

title: 6134: coveting, prohibition of
content:
Coveting is forbidden by God and can lead to terrible consequences. It occasionally refers to a commendable earnest desiring.

Coveting is forbidden by God

In the Ten Commandments

EXO 20:17 parallel DEU 5:21ROM 7:7–7:8 Coveting is provoked by the law that forbids it. See also ROM 13:9–13:10

Through the prophets

ISA 5:8, ISA 57:17; ; EZK 33:31; ; AMO 2:6, AMO 5:11–5:12, AMO 8:5–8:6; ; MIC 2:2

In Jesus Christ's teaching

MAT 6:19–6:21 See also LUK 12:15

Coveting has some terrible consequences

2SA 11:2–11:4 adultery; ; 1KI 21:18–21:19 murders; MAT 18:1–18:3; ; MRK 9:33–9:35 parallel LUK 9:46–9:48; ; 1CO 6:9–6:10; ACT 5:3–5:10 God's judgment

Further examples of coveting

JOS 7:21 See also DEU 7:25; JOS 22:20 Achan; 1SA 2:12–2:15 Eli's sons; 1SA 8:3 Samuel's sons; 1SA 15:9–15:10 Saul; PRO 30:16; JER 6:13, JER 8:10; HAB 2:5 Israel's enemies are likened to the grave, always swallowing people and never satisfied; ACT 8:13–8:23 Simon the sorcerer

Believers may pray to be free from coveting

PSA 119:36

Godly people claim not to have coveted

Samuel

1SA 12:3–12:5

Paul

ACT 20:33 See also 1CO 9:12; 2CO 7:2, 2CO 11:9, 2CO 12:14–12:18; 1TH 2:5

Coveting as a commendable earnest desiring

A desire for the greater spiritual gifts: 1CO 12:31, 1CO 14:1, 1CO 14:39; 1TI 3:1 a desire to serve in a leadership role in the church

title: 6135: coveting, and other sins
content:
Covetousness is associated with other sins and brings trouble with it.

Coveting is linked with other sins

Idolatry

EPH 5:5 See also 1CO 10:6–10:7; COL 3:5

Pride

PSA 10:3

Sexual sin

MAT 5:28 See also 1TH 4:5

Laziness

PRO 21:25–21:26

Covetousness is one of many sins which pollute a person

MRK 7:21–7:23

Love of money is covetousness

It is a source of evil

1TI 6:9–6:10 See also PRO 10:2, PRO 22:16, PRO 28:20, PRO 28:22, PRO 30:8–30:9

Examples of those who love money

LUK 16:14; JHN 12:5–12:6 See also MAT 23:25 parallel LUK 11:39; 2PE 2:3, 2PE 2:14 False teachers are “experts in greed”.

Believers must not be lovers of money

HEB 13:5; 1PE 5:2 Elders must not be greedy for money. See also EXO 18:21; EPH 5:3; 1TI 3:1–3:3

Love of money characterises the last days

2TI 3:1–3:2

Coveting brings trouble

JAS 4:1–4:2 See also PRO 15:27, PRO 28:25, PRO 29:4

Even a gift can be given covetously

2CO 9:5 The gift translated as “one grudgingly given” literally means a “covetous gift”.

title: 6139: deadness, spiritual
content:
Being morally and spiritually without life, therefore without a relationship with God. This condition, being the result of original sin, brings wrath and judgment and can be changed only by the sovereign, intervening grace of God.

Spiritual deadness is humanity's condition by nature

It is linked to physical death

GEN 2:17 See also GEN 3:3, GEN 3:19; PRO 11:19; EZK 18:20–18:31; JHN 8:21–8:24; ROM 1:32, ROM 6:23

It is the universal consequence of sin

EPH 2:1 See also GEN 8:21; 1CH 10:13; PSA 51:5, PSA 58:3; PRO 21:16; JHN 6:53; ROM 3:23, ROM 5:12–5:13, ROM 5:17; COL 2:13

It is confirmed in the law

ROM 5:20 See also ROM 7:4–7:12, ROM 8:2–8:4; GAL 2:19

It is evidenced in the human mind

ROM 8:6; ; 2CO 4:4; ; COL 1:21; ; TIT 1:15

It is evidenced in the human body

ROM 7:24 See also ROM 6:12–6:14, ROM 6:16, ROM 8:10; 1CO 15:50; 2CO 5:10; GAL 6:7–6:8; EPH 4:22; 1TI 5:6; JAS 3:6

Spiritual darkness brings judgment

God's judgment is final

LUK 16:26 See also ISA 66:24; MAT 8:12, MAT 25:30–25:32; MRK 9:43–9:49; LUK 13:28; HEB 9:27; 1PE 1:17

The second death

REV 20:14–20:15 See also MAT 25:41; 1JN 5:16–5:17; JUD 1:12; REV 2:11, REV 21:8

Rescue from spiritual deadness is initiated by God

He brings salvation

EPH 2:4–2:5 See also PSA 103:12; ISA 26:19; EZK 37:1–37:14

He brings union with the death of Jesus Christ

ROM 6:4 See also ROM 6:5–6:10; 2CO 4:10, 2CO 5:15–5:17; GAL 2:19–2:20, GAL 5:24, GAL 6:14; PHP 3:10; COL 2:12

He brings union with the resurrection of Jesus Christ

COL 3:1 See also ROM 5:21, ROM 6:11; REV 20:4–20:5

He brings ultimate physical resurrection

ROM 8:10–8:11 See also 1CO 15:42–15:49

Concern for those who are spiritually sleepy

EPH 5:14 See also ROM 13:11; 1TH 5:5–5:7; REV 3:1–3:2

title: 6142: decay
content:
As a consequence of the fall, all created things have a tendency to waste away and spoil. Jesus Christ brings deliverance from this.

Decay was decreed as a result of the fall

In human beings

GEN 2:17

In the world

GEN 3:17–3:19 See also ROM 8:20

Decay is the common experience of humanity

PSA 90:3–90:10 See also PSA 49:8–49:9

A decaying world is not worth clinging to

MAT 6:19–6:20 See also PSA 102:26; LUK 12:33; 1JN 2:15–2:17

Jesus Christ is free from decay

ACT 2:24–2:31 See also PSA 16:8–16:11; ACT 13:34–13:37; HEB 7:26, HEB 13:8

Believers are redeemed from decay

2CO 4:16 See also JOB 19:26; JHN 3:16

Christians are ``salt'' in a decaying world

MAT 5:13 See also MRK 9:50; LUK 14:34

The world to come is free from decay

1PE 1:3–1:5 See also ROM 8:21; 1CO 15:42; HEB 12:28, HEB 13:14; REV 21:4

Examples of the link between sin and decay

LEV 26:14–26:16, LEV 26:39; NUM 5:20–5:22, NUM 5:27; DEU 28:22, DEU 32:24; JOB 18:13; ; PSA 32:3, PSA 49:13–49:14, PSA 106:15, PSA 106:43, PSA 112:10; ; ISA 5:24, ISA 10:16, ISA 17:4, ISA 24:16, ISA 64:7; ; JER 49:7; LAM 2:6, LAM 2:8; EZK 4:17, EZK 24:23, EZK 33:10; HOS 4:3, HOS 8:10; HAB 3:16; ; JAS 5:2

title: 6145: deceit
content: Behaviour, in words or actions, which is deliberately intended to make people believe things that are not true.

title: 6146: deceit, and God's nature
content:
Deceit is totally contrary to the nature and purposes of God. The gospel is grounded in the complete truthfulness and trustworthiness of God, whereas sin is based upon self-deception.

God's nature and purposes are not based on deception

Deceit is contrary to the nature of God

TIT 1:2 See also JHN 16:13; 1PE 2:22

God cannot be deceived

1CH 28:9 See also PSA 139:1; ACT 5:3; 1CO 4:5

Jesus Christ does not deceive

ISA 53:9

Satan's power rests on deception

JHN 8:44 See also GEN 3:1–3:4; 2CO 11:3, 2CO 11:14; 2TH 2:9–2:10

The coming antichrist will rely on deception

2JN 1:7 See also MAT 24:24 parallel MRK 13:22; REV 13:14

False prophets and false teachers rely on deception

2CO 11:13 See also JER 14:14, JER 23:26; EZK 13:6; ACT 20:30; ROM 16:18; EPH 4:14; 1TI 4:2; TIT 1:10–1:11; 2PE 2:1–2:3

Deception is at the heart of human sin

The fall took place through deception

GEN 3:1–3:4, GEN 3:13 See also 1TI 2:14

Deception is a characteristic of fallen human nature

JER 17:9 See also JOB 15:35PSA 5:9, PSA 10:7, PSA 35:20, PSA 36:3; MIC 6:12; ROM 3:13 PSA 38:12, PSA 116:11; PRO 12:5; JER 9:4–9:5 deceptive friends and brothers; HOS 11:12; NAM 3:1; MAT 15:19 parallel MRK 7:21–7:22; ROM 1:29; 2TI 3:13; TIT 1:12

Examples of deceit

GEN 4:9 Cain; GEN 12:13, GEN 20:2; GEN 26:7 Isaac; ; GEN 27:19 Jacob; ; GEN 31:7 Laban; ; GEN 37:31 Joseph's brothers; ; EXO 8:29 Pharaoh; ; JOS 9:4 the Gibeonites; 1SA 21:2, 1SA 21:13; 2SA 13:6 Amnon; ; 1KI 13:18 the old prophet; ; 2KI 5:22 Gehazi; ; MAT 2:8 Herod; MAT 22:18 parallels MRK 12:15, LUK 20:23

title: 6147: deceit, practice of
content:
Deceit is condemned by God as an attempt to deny his sovereignty and his revelation of himself.

Deceit is forbidden by God

Condemnation of deception in general

LEV 19:11 See also PRO 12:22, PRO 24:28

False witnesses condemned

EXO 20:16 parallel DEU 5:20 See also PRO 12:17, PRO 14:5, PRO 19:9

False measures prohibited

LEV 19:35 See also DEU 25:13; PRO 11:1, PRO 20:10; MIC 6:11

Spiritual deception condemned

TIT 1:16 See also MAL 1:14; MAT 23:27–23:28; LUK 6:46; ROM 2:21

Deception must not be found in the people of God

God's people must not practise deceit

1PE 2:1 See also JOB 27:4; PSA 24:3–24:4, PSA 32:2; ZEP 3:13; JHN 4:24; 1CO 5:8; EPH 4:25; COL 3:9; 1PE 3:10; REV 14:5

Christian leaders in particular must not deceive

2CO 4:2 See also 2CO 1:12, 2CO 2:17; 1TH 2:3–2:5; 1TI 3:8; TIT 1:7

God's people warned against being deceived

EPH 5:6 See also JER 29:8; 1CO 6:9–6:10, 1CO 15:33; GAL 6:7; COL 2:8; 2TH 2:3; 1JN 3:7

Deceit results in God's judgment

PSA 5:6 See also DEU 25:16; PSA 55:23, PSA 119:118; PRO 20:17, PRO 21:6; JER 9:8–9:9; EZK 13:9; ACT 5:9–5:10; REV 21:8, REV 21:27, REV 22:15

title: 6151: dirt
content:
Throwing dirt to signify contempt

2SA 16:13 See also NAM 3:6

Bathing to remove dirt

1PE 3:21

Comparative use of dirt to emphasise abundance

ZEC 9:3

Metaphorical use of filth to represent sin

ISA 4:4 See also PRO 30:11–30:14; ISA 28:8; JAS 1:21; REV 17:4

Refuse indicating worthlessness

1CO 4:13 See also 2KI 9:37; PSA 83:10; ISA 5:25; JER 8:2, JER 9:22, JER 16:4, JER 25:33; LAM 3:45; PHP 3:8–3:9

title: 6154: fall, the
content: The departure by creation, including human beings, from the patterns and standards set for it by God. Creation now exists at a lower level of integrity and fulfilment than that which God originally intended for it.

resource.label: 6157: fall, of Satan and his angels

title: 6155: fall, of Adam and Eve
content:
The first human beings abused their God-given freedom. Their original disobedience and resultant expulsion from the Garden of Eden is usually referred to as “the fall”.

The first sin was the disobedience of Adam and Eve to God's command

GEN 2:17, GEN 3:1–3:7 See also HOS 6:7; ROM 5:12, ROM 5:14; 2CO 11:3; 1TI 2:14

Causes of the fall

Disobedience

GEN 2:16–2:17, GEN 3:1–3:3; ; ISA 43:27; ; HOS 6:7

Through satanic deception

GEN 3:4, GEN 3:13; ; 2CO 11:3; ; 1TI 2:14

Evil desire

GEN 3:6

Adam and Eve tried to hide their disobedience

GEN 3:8–3:13 See also JOB 31:33

The disobedience of Adam and Eve affected every relationship in life

Relationship with God

GEN 3:22–3:23 See also GEN 4:14; PSA 90:3–90:10; ROM 1:20–1:21

Relationships among human beings

GEN 3:16 See also GEN 4:1–4:8; ROM 1:18–1:32; EPH 2:1–2:3, EPH 2:11–2:12

The relationship between human beings and the creation

GEN 3:17–3:19 See also GEN 3:14–3:15, GEN 4:10–4:12; ROM 8:19

The consequences of the disobedience of Adam and Eve

It leads to death and punishment

ROM 5:12 See also ROM 1:18; 1CO 15:21–15:22; 1TH 1:10, 1TH 2:14–2:16

Sin springs from within the human mind, will and emotions

JAS 1:13–1:15 See also JAS 4:6–4:7; 1JN 2:15–2:17

title: 6156: fall, of humanity
content:
On account of their relation to Adam, all human beings have an inherent bias towards evil due to sin. Individual acts of sin reflect this bias, often referred to as “original sin”.

All human beings, except for Jesus Christ, are sinful

ROM 3:23; HEB 4:15 See also GEN 6:5, GEN 6:11–6:12; PSA 14:1–14:3, PSA 51:5; ISA 64:6–64:7; JHN 8:7; ROM 3:9–3:20, ROM 5:12–5:19; EPH 2:1–2:3

Every part of a person is affected by sin

The heart

JER 17:9 See also GEN 6:5, GEN 8:21; 1SA 17:28; 2CH 12:14; PSA 66:18; JER 5:23; MAT 5:28, MAT 15:8, MAT 15:19

The body

ROM 6:6 See also ROM 6:12–6:13; 1CO 6:13, 1CO 6:19–6:20

Human nature

ROM 8:5–8:8 See also ROM 7:5; 1CO 5:5; GAL 5:19–5:21, GAL 6:8; EPH 2:3

Believers are warned not to fall from grace

HEB 3:12–3:14 See also MAT 24:12–24:13; 1CO 15:2; GAL 5:4; HEB 4:11, HEB 12:15; 2PE 3:17; REV 2:4–2:5

God is able to keep believers from falling

JUD 1:24 See also JOB 33:14–33:18; ROM 16:25; EPH 6:13; 1JN 5:18

title: 6157: fall, of Satan and his angels
content:
Satan and his angels rebelled against God and were removed from their positions of authority. They are now destined for judgment.

The rebellion of Satan and his angels

ISA 14:12–14:15 Written about a king in Isaiah's own time, this passage has been used by both Jews and Christians as a description of the fall of Satan; JUD 1:6 See also JOB 4:18; EZK 28:15; LUK 10:18; REV 12:7–12:9, REV 12:12

The results of the rebellion of Satan and his angels

Constant spiritual warfare on the earth

REV 12:17 See also MAT 16:23 parallel MRK 8:33; ACT 13:10; 2CO 11:13–11:15; GAL 5:16–5:18; 1TI 3:6–3:7; JAS 4:7; 1PE 5:8–5:9

God's punishment

2PE 2:4 See also MAT 25:41; JUD 1:6; REV 19:20, REV 20:10

title: 6160: fathers, sin of the
content:
Children may suffer on account of the sins committed by their forebears, just as they may also be blessed for their sake. Nevertheless, Scripture teaches that each individual must take responsibility for his or her own sin.

Children suffer the ill-effects of their fathers' sins

EXO 34:6–34:7 See also EXO 20:4–20:6 parallel DEU 5:8–5:10; LEV 26:39; NUM 14:18, NUM 14:31–14:33; JOS 7:24–7:25; 1KI 16:34; JOS 6:26; JOB 21:19; ISA 14:21; JER 32:18

The exilic prophets emphasised individual responsibility for sin

EZK 18:20 See also JER 31:29–31:30; EZK 18:1–18:20

Children share in the blessing of their fathers

GEN 17:4–17:7, GEN 26:24; DEU 7:9 See also GEN 6:17–6:18, GEN 15:18–15:19; EXO 2:23–2:25, EXO 20:4–20:6 parallel DEU 5:8–5:10; NUM 25:10–25:13; 2SA 7:11–7:16; 1KI 11:12–11:13, 1KI 11:31–11:34; LUK 1:50; ROM 11:28–11:29

title: 6163: faults
content:
Human weaknesses and failings which are sinful in themselves, and may lead to further sin. It is common, often as a means of averting blame, to put undue focus on the faults of others. However, where necessary, faults should be exposed and responsibility properly attributed. Jesus Christ lived a faultless life and God's people are challenged to live lives that are above reproach. The church will one day be presented without fault before God.

The variety of human faults

A flawed human nature

ROM 7:14–7:23 See also GAL 5:17; EPH 2:3; 2PE 2:18

Human weaknesses and failings

ROM 15:1 See also ROM 8:28, ROM 14:1–14:2

Faults in thought, word and deed

PSA 78:57 disloyalty and unfaithfulness to God; ; PRO 14:21 not showing concern for others; ; ISA 53:6 selfishness in going one's own way; ; HOS 7:16 unwillingness to turn to God; ; JAS 3:2 failure to control the tongue; ; JAS 4:17 failure to do what is right

Finding fault with others

Passing blame to others

GEN 3:12 Adam blames Eve for his disobedience. See also EXO 32:22–32:24 Aaron blames the Israelites for the golden calf; 1SA 13:11–13:12 Saul blames his troops' impatience and Samuel's late arrival for his disobedience; 1SA 15:20–15:21 Saul blames his soldiers for the failure to carry out God's instructions; MAT 25:24–25:25 The man who buried his talent blames his action on his master's hardness.

Criticising and judging others

MAT 7:1–7:5 See also JAS 4:11–4:12, JAS 5:9; JUD 1:16

Finding fault with God's appointed leaders

EXO 17:3 See also EXO 15:24; NUM 14:2; JOS 9:18; ACT 11:2–11:3; 2CO 10:1

Finding fault with God's people

MAT 5:11 parallel LUK 6:22 See also EXO 5:16–5:17; PSA 41:6, PSA 71:10; DAN 6:24

Finding fault with God

PSA 78:19 See also EXO 16:7; NUM 14:27, NUM 21:5; EZK 35:13

Finding fault with Jesus Christ

LUK 6:7 parallels MAT 12:10, MRK 3:4 See also MAT 9:3 parallels MRK 2:7, LUK 5:21 Jesus Christ is accused of blasphemy because he claims to forgive sin; MAT 9:11 parallels MRK 2:16, LUK 5:30 Jesus Christ is criticised for his association with “sinners”; MAT 15:2 parallel MRK 7:5 Jesus Christ is criticised for failure to conform to ceremonial cleansing traditions; MAT 26:59–26:61 parallel MRK 14:55–14:59 false charges brought against Jesus Christ at his trial; JHN 9:16 Jesus Christ is accused of breaking the Sabbath.

Confronting human faults

Accepting blame for one's own faults

PSA 51:3–51:4 See also JON 1:12; LUK 15:18

God holds his people at fault

JER 17:4 See also HEB 8:8–8:9; JER 31:31–31:32

Faults cannot be hidden from God

PSA 19:12, PSA 44:21, PSA 90:8; ; ECC 12:14; ; 1CO 14:25

Confronting others with their faults

MAT 18:15–18:17; GAL 6:1 See also LUK 17:3; JAS 5:19–5:20

Examples of those confronted with their faults

2SA 12:7 David; ; 1KI 14:7–14:9 Jeroboam; ; 2CH 16:9 Asa; ; DAN 5:23 Belshazzar; ; MAT 14:3 parallel MRK 6:18 Herod; ; ACT 5:3 Ananias and Sapphira; ; GAL 2:11–2:12 Peter

God can deal with human faults

Freedom from the power of sin

ROM 6:11–6:12, ROM 8:3–8:4; ; 2CO 5:17; ; GAL 5:16

Strength for service

2CO 12:9–12:10; ; PHP 4:13

Forgiveness

PSA 65:3; ; EPH 4:32; ; COL 2:13; ; 1JN 1:9

Faultlessness

Faultlessness shown by Jesus Christ

1PE 2:22 See also ISA 53:9; JHN 8:46; HEB 4:15; 1PE 1:19; 1JN 3:5

God's people are to be above reproach

1PE 2:12 See also ROM 12:17–12:18; 2CO 8:20–8:21 Paul ensures that his handling of money is above criticism; PHP 2:14–2:151TI 3:2; TIT 1:6 2PE 3:14

The church will be presented faultless before God

JUD 1:24–1:25 See also EPH 5:27; COL 1:22; 1TH 3:13, 1TH 5:23

title: 6166: flesh, sinful nature
content:
The physical aspect of human beings, which distinguishes them from God and is therefore frequently used in the NT as a symbol of human sinful nature in contrast with God's perfection. (The Greek word for “flesh” is sometimes translated by other words and phrases in the passages cited in this theme.).

Flesh as the bodily substance of human beings

As individuals or in relation to others

PSA 84:2 See also GEN 2:23–2:24, GEN 29:14; 1CO 15:39GAL 2:20; PHP 1:22–1:24

As the means by which Jesus Christ identified with the human race to bring salvation

JHN 1:14 See also EPH 2:15; HEB 10:20; 1JN 4:2

As subject to mortality

ISA 40:6–40:7 See also PSA 78:39; ACT 2:31; 1CO 15:50

As subject to weakness

2CH 32:8 See also PSA 73:26; MAT 26:41 parallel MRK 14:38

Flesh as contrasting human nature with God's perfection

The powerlessness of human beings contrasted with God's eternal power

ISA 31:3 See also JHN 3:6, JHN 6:63

Human or worldly standards contrasted with God's standards

JHN 8:15 See also 1CO 1:26; 2CO 5:16, 2CO 10:3–10:4

Flesh as denoting the sinful nature of human beings

The tendency to sin

ROM 7:18 Paul does not mean that no goodness at all exists in people; nor that the physical aspect of human beings is inherently evil. He means that humans are invariably infected by evil and subject to its power. See also JER 17:5

The conflict in human experience between the sinful nature and the Spirit of God

GAL 5:17 See also ROM 8:4–8:9; GAL 5:19–5:25

The sinful nature is opposed to God and his will

This opposition finds expression in a range of acts and attitudes

GAL 5:19–5:21 See also ROM 7:14–7:25, ROM 8:7, ROM 13:13–13:14; 1CO 6:9–6:11; EPH 5:5; JAS 1:14–1:15; 1PE 2:11; 2PE 2:10, 2PE 2:18; 1JN 2:16

Confidence in the law is futile

ROM 8:3 Because of the sinfulness of human nature, God's law is powerless to bring people into relationship with God; GAL 3:3 Even the attempt to find acceptance with God through keeping his law is an act of the sinful nature because it involves rejecting his offer of salvation through his grace. See also ROM 7:25; PHP 3:3–3:9

The sinful nature controls human behaviour in ways which run counter to God's purpose

ROM 8:8 See also ROM 7:5

The sinful nature therefore makes people subject to God's judgment and to death

ROM 8:13; EPH 2:3 See also GAL 6:8

Believers are not controlled by the sinful nature

Through Jesus Christ's entering into human flesh, God delivers from the power and consequences of human sinfulness

ROM 8:3 See also EPH 2:15

Believers have crucified the sinful nature

ROM 7:5–7:6 See also ROM 8:8–8:9; GAL 5:24; COL 2:11

The power of God's Spirit enables believers to continue to resist the sinful nature

ROM 8:13 See also ROM 13:14; GAL 5:13; COL 3:5–3:6; 1PE 2:11

God's provision of church discipline in eliminating the sinful nature

1CO 5:5 The discipline of excluding a sinner from the church community is intended to bring him to repentance and so abandon his sinful course of action. Repentance provoked by physical suffering is possibly also in mind.

title: 6169: godlessness
content:
An outlook and lifestyle which does not acknowledge God. God frustrates the evil purposes of the godless and judges them. Believers are to guard against godlessness.

Godlessness is an outlook and lifestyle which does not acknowledge God

PSA 14:1–14:3 parallel PSA 53:1–53:3 See also JOB 35:9–35:10; PSA 10:4–10:11, PSA 36:1–36:4; PRO 11:9, PRO 30:8–30:9

Examples of godlessness

Judah's religious leaders: JER 23:11–23:12, JER 23:15–23:17; HEB 12:16 Esau

God frustrates the evil purposes of the godless

JOB 8:11–8:19, JOB 34:29–34:30; ; PSA 9:17–9:20

God judges godless people and nations

ROM 1:18 JOB 13:15–13:16, JOB 15:34, JOB 20:4–20:5, JOB 27:8–27:10, JOB 36:8–36:13; PSA 50:16–50:22; ; ISA 10:6, ISA 33:13–33:14

Believers are to guard against godlessness

JUD 1:3–1:23 See also 1TI 4:7, 1TI 6:20–6:21

Jesus Christ will turn godlessness away from Israel

ROM 11:26–11:27

title: 6172: guilt
content: Primarily a state of being at fault, often accompanied by a feeling of being in the wrong. Scripture affirms that human guilt arises from sin before God, and can only be purged through accepting the forgiveness offered through the death of Jesus Christ.

title: 6173: guilt, and God
content:
All human beings are guilty in the sight of God, and all require forgiveness and expiation of their sin. Guilt can arise through failing to know what is right, through failing to do what is right and through giving in to temptation to do wrong.

Guilt as the objective condition of sinners

Guilt results from intentional sin

LEV 6:1–6:7; JAS 2:10 See also NUM 5:5–5:7; DEU 24:14–24:15; EZR 9:13–9:15; EZK 22:1–22:4; JHN 16:7–16:8

Guilt results from unintentional sin

LEV 4:13 See also LEV 4:22, LEV 4:27; ACT 3:17–3:20

Guilt as an accumulation of sin

HOS 13:12 See also EZR 9:6; ISA 1:4, ISA 24:20

God's knowledge of human guilt

PSA 69:5 See also GEN 4:8–4:10; JOS 7:1–7:26; JER 2:22; 1CO 4:4–4:5

God's anger at human guilt

2CH 24:18 See also NUM 32:10–32:13; 2KI 22:11–22:13; HOS 12:13–12:14; ROM 1:18, ROM 2:5–2:8; EPH 5:6–5:7

God's determination to punish the guilty

God himself punishes the guilty

NUM 14:18 See also EXO 20:5; JER 25:12; NAM 1:2–1:3

Human judges are to punish the guilty

DEU 25:1–25:3 See also PRO 17:15, PRO 24:23–24:25; ROM 13:1–13:5; 1PE 2:13–2:14

title: 6174: guilt, human aspects of
content:
Scripture affirms that all are guilty in the sight of God, and explores the various ways in which guilt affects human life, before offering forgiveness of guilt through the death of Jesus Christ.

Human awareness of guilt

PSA 51:3–51:5 See also GEN 42:21; PSA 32:1–32:4; JHN 8:3–8:11; HEB 9:9; 1JN 3:19–3:20

Human beings are held responsible for their guilt

LEV 5:17 See also ISA 24:5–24:6; EZK 18:19–18:20; HOS 10:1–10:2, HOS 13:16; MAT 12:35–12:36; ROM 14:12; 2CO 5:10; REV 20:12

Guilt defiles human life

ISA 59:1–59:3 See also PSA 51:1–51:7; JER 2:22; HEB 10:22

Human experience of guilt

Feelings of guilt in general

HEB 10:1–10:2 See also 2SA 24:10 parallel 1CH 21:8; 2CO 7:10; HEB 10:22; 1JN 3:19–3:20

Guilt produces feelings of anguish and despair

MAT 27:3–27:5 See also EZR 9:1–9:7, EZR 10:1; PSA 40:11–40:12; PRO 28:17; LAM 1:20

Guilt produces feelings of shame and disgrace

EZR 9:6 See also JER 23:38–23:40

Guilt produces feelings of fear and terror

ISA 6:1–6:7 See also GEN 3:8–3:10; ISA 33:14

Guilt produces feelings of unworthiness

LUK 15:21 See also EZR 9:13–9:15; PSA 51:11 The fear of being cast out of God's presence is the result of the psalmist's sense of guilt; LUK 5:8; 1CO 15:9

title: 6175: guilt, removal of
content:
Through the death of Jesus Christ, God has provided a means of removing guilt, thus opening the way for believers to know the blessings of a right relationship with him.

The removal of guilt is God's work

JER 2:22, JER 50:20; ROM 8:33 “Justify” is a legal term which includes the meaning “to declare not guilty”. See also 2SA 24:10 parallel 1CH 21:8; PSA 32:1–32:5; ISA 6:1–6:6

The removal of guilt requires confession

PSA 32:5 See also 2SA 24:10 parallel 1CH 21:8; PSA 51:14; JER 3:12–3:13; ACT 11:21; ROM 2:4; 2TI 2:25; 1JN 1:8–1:9

The removal of guilt by the OT sacrificial system

Guilt removed by the guilt offering

LEV 6:6–6:7 See also LEV 19:20–19:22; EZR 10:18–10:19

Guilt removed by the sin offering

NUM 15:27–15:28 See also LEV 5:5–5:6; NUM 6:9–6:11

Guilt removed by the Day of Atonement

LEV 16:20–16:34

Animal sacrifices are ultimately ineffective in removing guilt

HEB 10:1–10:4 See also HEB 9:9

The removal of guilt by Jesus Christ's sacrificial death

ISA 53:10–53:12 This prophecy finds its ultimate fulfilment in the person of Jesus Christ; HEB 9:14 See also JHN 1:29 The point of John's image is that Jesus Christ will be sacrificed, as lambs were, to remove people's guilt; ROM 5:9 The blood of Jesus Christ referred to here is his shed blood, and therefore speaks of his (sacrificial) death; 2CO 5:21 Human guilt is transferred to Jesus Christ, in order that believers might become righteous before God; TIT 2:14; HEB 9:28, HEB 10:11–10:14; 1JN 1:7

The results of guilt being removed

A clean conscience

HEB 9:14, HEB 10:22

Peace with God

ISA 53:5; ; ROM 5:1; ; COL 1:19–1:20

Access into God's presence

ROM 5:1–5:2; ; EPH 2:18

Joy

PSA 32:1–32:11; ; ROM 5:1–5:2

Hope

ROM 5:1–5:2

A desire to worship God

PSA 51:14–51:15

A desire to serve God

ISA 6:6–6:8; ; JHN 21:15–21:20

title: 6178: hardness of heart
content:
Primarily, in Scripture, a persistent inner refusal to hear and obey the word of God. Also, in a more general sense, an uncaring or unsympathetic attitude towards other people.

Kinds of hardness of heart

As disobedience towards God

PSA 95:8–95:9 Referring to the disobedience of the Israelites in their wilderness wanderings. See also HEB 3:7–3:9EPH 4:18 See also PRO 28:14; ZEC 7:12; ROM 2:5; HEB 3:12–3:15, HEB 4:5–4:7

As the work of God

EXO 10:20 God's judgment on Pharaoh was to confirm him in his stubbornness so that he might feel the full weight of his wrath; ISA 6:10 See also JHN 12:40

As an uncaring attitude towards other people

DEU 15:7 See also PSA 17:10, PSA 73:7, PSA 119:70; MAT 19:8 parallel MRK 10:5; JAS 2:15–2:16; 1JN 3:17

Examples of hardness of heart

Pharaoh

EXO 7:13, EXO 7:22, EXO 8:19, EXO 8:32, EXO 9:7, EXO 9:34–9:35

Israel

1SA 6:6; ; JER 5:23, JER 9:14, JER 11:8, JER 13:10, JER 16:12, JER 18:12, JER 23:26

Zedekiah

2CH 36:12–36:13

Jesus Christ's disciples

MRK 6:51–6:52, MRK 8:17

Jesus Christ's generation

MAT 13:15, MAT 19:8 parallel MRK 10:5; ; MRK 3:5

Paul's hearers

ACT 28:25–28:27

God's remedies for hardness of heart

Repentance

PSA 51:17 See also 1KI 8:46–8:49; 2CH 34:27; JER 3:17; EZK 18:31

A work of grace

EZK 11:19 God's promise to Israel. See also 1KI 8:58; JER 31:33; EZK 36:26

title: 6181: ignorance
content: A state of lack of knowledge or information, including a failure to know the person, will and ways of God. Scripture emphasises the importance of a true knowledge of God and makes such knowledge available.

title: 6182: ignorance, and human situation
content:
Ignorance is part of the human condition: as finite creatures, human knowledge is incomplete, but human beings can trust God, whose knowledge is complete, to guide their steps.

Ignorance of God

Finite human beings cannot know God completely

ECC 11:5 See also JOB 38:1–38:7; ECC 3:11

This ignorance will be dispelled at Jesus Christ's return

1CO 13:9–13:12

Ignorance of the future

Human beings are ignorant of future events

ECC 8:7–8:8 See also ECC 3:22, ECC 9:12, ECC 10:14, ECC 11:2, ECC 11:6; ACT 20:22

Human beings are ignorant of the time of Jesus Christ's return

MRK 13:32–13:36 parallel MAT 24:36–24:44

Presumptious plans should therefore be avoided

JAS 4:13–4:16 See also PSA 39:6; PRO 27:1; LUK 12:16–12:21

God's guidance is essential

PRO 20:24 See also PRO 19:21; JER 10:23

Ignorance of doctrinal matters

Within the church

1TH 4:13–4:18 See also 1CO 10:1–10:10, 1CO 12:1–12:11

God has established teachers to combat ignorance

1CO 12:28 See also EPH 4:11–4:13; 1TI 4:13, 1TI 5:17; 2TI 3:16–3:17

Ignorance may lead to death

PRO 9:17–9:18 See also PRO 4:19, PRO 7:21–7:23

title: 6183: ignorance, of God
content:
A lack of knowledge of God, regarded as inexcusable by Scripture. God has made his existence, nature and power known to all through creation. God himself is not ignorant of human sin, but is able to discern the real intentions of human minds and wills.

Ignorance of God is inexcusable

God is made known through creation

ROM 1:18–1:20 As God is made known to all through nature, human ignorance of God is inexcusable. See also PSA 19:1–19:4; ACT 14:17, ACT 17:24–17:28; ROM 2:14–2:15

God revealed himself to Israel

NEH 9:13–9:14 See also EXO 19:10–19:11, EXO 19:16–19:19; PSA 103:7, PSA 147:19–147:20; EZK 20:5

Ignorance of God is linked to sin and rebellion

Among the Gentiles

EPH 4:18–4:19 See also EXO 5:2; JHN 4:22; ACT 17:23; 1TH 4:3–4:5

In Israel

ISA 1:2–1:3 See also ISA 56:10; JER 2:8–2:9, JER 4:22, JER 5:4–5:5, JER 8:7–8:12, JER 9:6, JER 10:21; HOS 4:1, HOS 4:6; AMO 3:10

Ignorance of God in the ministry of Jesus Christ

Among the religious leaders

JHN 8:13–8:19 See also MAT 22:23–22:32 parallels MRK 12:18–12:27, LUK 20:27–20:38; JHN 8:54–8:55, JHN 16:2–16:3

Among his disciples

JHN 14:8–14:11 See also LUK 18:31–18:34; JHN 14:5–14:7

God is not ignorant of human sin and motives

GEN 6:5; HEB 4:13 See also EXO 3:7; JOB 28:12–28:28; PSA 139:1–139:6, PSA 147:5; EZK 11:5; MAT 6:8; ACT 1:24

Human ignorance of sin and the revelation of God

The fall brought knowledge of sin

GEN 3:22 See also GEN 3:4–3:7

The OT law exposed the nature of sin

ROM 7:7 See also ROM 3:20, ROM 4:15, ROM 5:13

Human ignorance does not excuse sin

By individuals

LEV 5:17–5:19 The law made provision for atonement of sins committed as a result of ignorance. See also LEV 4:27–4:35; NUM 15:27–15:29; EZK 45:20

By the nation

LEV 4:13–4:21 See also NUM 15:22–15:26; HEB 9:7

By leaders

LEV 4:22–4:26 See also LEV 4:3–4:12

Sin committed in ignorance incurs a lesser judgment than other sin

LUK 12:47–12:48 See also PRO 24:12; ROM 2:14–2:15

Repentance of sin committed in ignorance brings forgiveness

GEN 20:1–20:18 See also ACT 3:17–3:20; 1TI 1:13

Ignorance of God and salvation

There is no salvation outside Jesus Christ

ACT 4:12 See also JHN 14:6; 1TI 2:5–2:6

Believers have a responsibility to remove ignorance of the gospel

ROM 10:14–10:17 See also MAT 28:19–28:20

Ignorance and God's judgment

The justice of God in salvation

GEN 18:25 See also DEU 32:4; NEH 9:33; PRO 24:12; DAN 4:37; LUK 12:47–12:48

God's judgment falls on all who do not know him

2TH 1:8–1:10 See also PSA 79:6; JER 10:25; ROM 2:8

God's judgment falls severely on those who choose to disobey him

PRO 1:28–1:32 on Israel; ACT 17:29–17:31; ; HEB 2:2–2:3; ; 2PE 2:20–2:22

title: 6184: imagination
content: The human ability to form mental pictures, often used to refer to human desires or schemes which are inclined towards evil. Scripture often treats the human heart as the focus of the imagination.

title: 6185: imagination, inward desires
content:
The usually sinful inclinations of a heart which is by nature corrupt and stubborn, but which may be renewed by God, resulting in purified desires.

The heart is the source of human desires

PRO 4:23 See also PSA 10:3; MAT 12:34–12:35 parallel LUK 6:45

God knows the human heart

1KI 8:39; 1CH 28:9 See also 1SA 16:7 God's choice of David; PSA 7:9, PSA 19:14, PSA 26:2, PSA 38:9, PSA 44:21, PSA 139:2, PSA 139:23; PRO 15:26, PRO 21:2; JER 11:20 The plot of Jeremiah's enemies is revealed to him by the LORD; JER 20:12; EZK 11:5; REV 2:23; MAT 9:4 parallels MRK 2:8, LUK 5:22 Jesus Christ knows the thoughts of others.

The corruption of the heart

Its inclination towards evil

GEN 6:5 See also GEN 8:21; PSA 14:1–14:3 parallel PSA 53:1–53:3; PSA 94:11; ECC 9:3; JER 17:9–17:10; MAT 15:18–15:19 parallel MRK 7:20–7:23

Corruption finds particular expression in sexual sin

ROM 1:24 See also MAT 5:28; ROM 1:26–1:27; GAL 5:19

The stubbornness of the heart

It leads to disregard for God

ISA 65:2

It produces false confidence

DEU 29:18–29:19 See also JER 23:17

It leads to idolatry

JER 9:14 See also JER 13:10

It leads to disobedience

JER 7:24 See also JER 16:12

It brings God's judgment

JER 11:8 See also PRO 28:14, PRO 29:1; JER 18:12–18:13; ROM 2:5; HEB 3:7–3:11; PSA 95:7–95:11

God allows people to continue in their stubbornness

God hardens Pharaoh's heart: EXO 4:21, EXO 7:3, EXO 14:4, EXO 14:17; PSA 81:11–81:12; ; ROM 1:28

God's renewal of the heart

It results in purified desires

JER 24:7; EZK 36:26 See also DEU 6:6; EZK 11:19; ROM 12:2; 2CO 4:6; EPH 4:23; PHP 4:8–4:9; HEB 8:10; JER 31:33

Examples of renewed hearts

2CH 11:16; ; NEH 9:8; ; PSA 51:10, PSA 57:7, PSA 86:11; ; JER 3:17

God delights in a pure heart

PSA 24:3–24:5 See also DEU 5:29; PSA 73:1, PSA 125:4; MAT 5:8

God fulfils the desires of hearts devoted to him

PSA 37:4 See also PSA 21:2, PSA 145:19; MAT 6:21 parallel LUK 12:34; MAT 6:33 parallel LUK 12:31

God gives the desire to follow him

1CH 29:18

The human ability to visualise in parables

See also MAT 13:11–13:17 parallels MAT 4:10–4:12, LUK 8:9–8:10; LUK 10:23–10:24

title: 6186: imagination, evil scheming
content:
Human thinking, darkened by sin, expressed in plans which are in opposition to God and which seek to take advantage of others.

Human thought is darkened by sin

As a result of rejecting God's revelation

ROM 1:21 See also PSA 10:2–10:4; 2CO 3:14; EPH 4:17–4:18; TIT 1:15

As a result of excessive alcohol

PRO 23:33

As a result of Satan's activity

2CO 4:4; ; EPH 2:1–2:3

Sinful human thinking finds expression in evil ways

In evil schemes and plans

PRO 6:16–6:18 See also GEN 11:6; PSA 36:4, PSA 64:5–64:6; PRO 6:14, PRO 12:20, PRO 14:22, PRO 24:8–24:9; ECC 8:11; ROM 1:30

In scheming against God

2CO 10:5 See also DEU 31:21; PSA 2:1–2:2; HOS 7:15; NAM 1:11

In scheming against others

LAM 3:60–3:61 See also DEU 15:9 warnings against evil thinking as the year for cancelling debts approaches; JOB 21:27; PSA 10:2–10:3, PSA 37:7, PSA 38:12, PSA 41:7–41:8, PSA 140:1–140:2; ISA 32:7; ZEC 8:16–8:17; MAT 26:3–26:4 parallels MRK 14:1–14:2, LUK 22:1–22:2, JHN 11:53 Jewish religious leaders plot against Jesus Christ.

Human plans are an expression of pride

JAS 4:13–4:16 See also PRO 18:11; ISA 10:7–10:11, ISA 30:1; LUK 12:16–12:20

Evil plans will fail

LUK 1:51 See also EST 9:25; PSA 21:11–21:12, PSA 33:10; PRO 19:21; ISA 8:10, ISA 19:3, ISA 66:18; MIC 2:1; NAM 1:9

False prophecy comes from evil thinking

EZK 13:2–13:3 See also DEU 13:1–13:3; JER 23:16–23:17; EZK 13:17

resource.label: 4811: darkness, as a symbol of sin

title: 6187: immorality
content: Behaviour which is contrary to the will of God for his people. Although the term may be used to refer to a wide range of immoral actions, it is used especially in relation to unacceptable sexual behaviour.

title: 6188: immorality, nature of sexual
content:
Sexual behaviour which is contrary to God's law.

Sexual immorality is widespread in the world

1CO 5:9–5:10 See also 1CO 7:1–7:2; REV 9:21

The cause of sexual immorality

GAL 5:19 See also MAT 15:19–15:20 parallel MRK 7:21–7:23; EPH 4:17–4:19

The folly of sexual immorality

PRO 6:32 See also PRO 5:3–5:5, PRO 5:20, PRO 6:26

Sexual immorality brings punishment

HEB 13:4 See also LEV 20:10–20:21; PRO 2:16–2:19, PRO 22:14; EZK 16:38; ROM 1:24–1:27; EPH 5:5; COL 3:5–3:6; 1TH 4:3–4:6; JUD 1:7; REV 21:8, REV 22:15

Sexual immorality has no place in the Christian life

1TH 4:3, 1TH 4:7 See also ACT 15:20, ACT 15:29, ACT 21:25; ROM 13:13; 1CO 6:9–6:11, 1CO 6:13–6:20, 1CO 10:8; EPH 5:3; COL 3:5; HEB 12:16

Forgiveness for sexual immorality

1CO 6:11 See also LUK 7:36–7:39; JHN 8:3–8:11

title: 6189: immorality, examples of sexual
content:
Prohibited sexual relationships

Incest

LEV 18:6 See also GEN 19:33–19:36 Lot's daughters; GEN 35:22, GEN 38:13–38:18; LEV 18:7–18:20; 2SA 16:22; 1CO 5:1 more likely referring to his stepmother

Adultery

2SA 11:4; ; JER 23:14, JER 29:23; ; HOS 1:2; ; JHN 4:17–4:18

Prostitution

1CO 6:15–6:16 See also JDG 16:1; 1KI 3:16; HOS 4:13–4:15

Fornication

NUM 25:1, NUM 25:6; ; 1SA 2:22

Rape

GEN 34:1–34:2; ; 2SA 13:10–13:14

Homosexuality

GEN 19:5 the men of Sodom; ; JDG 19:22 the men of Gibeah

Sexual immorality among Christians

1CO 5:1; ; 2PE 2:13–2:14; ; JUD 1:4; ; REV 2:14, REV 2:20

Sexual immorality as a picture of spiritual unfaithfulness

Among God's people

JER 3:20 See also JER 13:26–13:27 reflecting the immorality of the pagan religions in which Israel had indulged; EZK 6:9, EZK 16:15–16:17; HOS 2:1–2:10, HOS 3:1, HOS 4:10–4:12, HOS 5:4; MAT 12:39, MAT 16:4

In the world

REV 14:8, REV 17:1–17:2, REV 17:4, REV 18:2, REV 19:2

title: 6193: impenitence
content: A deliberate and persistent refusal to repent or to submit to God's will.

title: 6194: impenitence, warnings against
content:
Scripture stresses the seriousness of impenitence. Despite the goodness of God, some sinners refuse to repent and turn to him in faith.

Impenitence is a refusal to turn from sin

HOS 7:10; ACT 7:51 See also JOB 34:33; ISA 1:4; JER 9:6; HOS 11:5

Impenitence in spite of God's invitations and and warnings

ISA 66:4; ZEC 7:11–7:12 See also 2KI 17:14; ZEC 1:4JER 7:25–7:26, JER 11:7–11:8, JER 11:10, JER 13:10, JER 17:23, JER 25:3–25:4, JER 32:33, JER 44:4–44:5, JER 44:16

Impenitence in spite of God's dealings

NUM 14:11 See also NEH 9:17; MAT 11:20–11:24 parallel LUK 10:13–10:15

Impenitence in spite of God's punishment

PSA 78:31–78:32; AMO 4:6 See also ISA 9:13; JER 2:30, JER 3:2–3:3, JER 5:3, JER 30:15; ZEP 3:2; HAG 2:17

Impenitence in spite of God's goodness

ROM 2:4 See also JOB 15:11 Although inappropriate for Job, the advice is still valid; ROM 10:21

There is no remedy for persistent impenitence

2CH 36:16; 2TH 2:10 See also HEB 6:4–6:6, HEB 10:26–10:27

title: 6195: impenitence, results of
content:
The seriousness of the failure to repent is frequently underlined in Scripture, which provides numerous examples of impenitence and its consequences.

Warnings against impenitence

HEB 12:25 See also 2CH 30:6–30:9; JER 4:1, JER 18:9–18:10; EZK 14:6; HOS 14:1–14:2; MAL 3:7; LUK 13:1–13:5; HEB 3:7–3:19; PSA 95:7–95:8; REV 2:5, REV 2:16

Results of impenitence

Storing up wrath for the day of judgment

ROM 2:5 See also 2CH 24:20, 2CH 36:16–36:17; JOB 36:12; PRO 1:24–1:26; AMO 4:9; ROM 1:24; 2PE 2:21

Condemnation

MAT 11:20

Hardness of heart

MAT 13:13–13:15 See also HEB 3:7–3:11

Spiritual blindness

JHN 12:40 See also ROM 11:8; REV 3:17

Being forsaken by God

2KI 17:13–17:20; ; 2CH 29:6–29:9

Examples of impenitence

EXO 8:15 Pharaoh; JDG 2:19; ; 1SA 8:19; ; NEH 9:26–9:29; ; DAN 9:13–9:14; 2CH 33:23 Amon; ; 2CH 36:13 Zedekiah; ; JER 6:15 inhabitants of Jerusalem; ; MAT 21:31 the elders and chief priests; ; LUK 18:18 the hard-hearted; ; ROM 1:18–1:23 the pagans; ; REV 2:21–2:27 Jezebel, the false prophetess; REV 9:20–9:21, REV 16:8–16:11

title: 6199: imperfection
content: The state of incompletion or decay that characterises the fallen world. Although God created the world perfect, it lapsed into its present state of imperfection through human sin.

title: 6200: imperfection, influence of
content:
Scripture indicates that the world, including human beings and institutions, are imperfect because of the contaminating influence of sin. This influence extends to the household of faith: both Israel and the Christian church are portrayed as prone to sin and weakness.

The creation is imperfect because of sin

GEN 3:17–3:19 See also GEN 5:29; ROM 8:20–8:22

Imperfection is expressed in corruption and death

PSA 103:15–103:16; 2PE 1:4 See also 2SA 14:14; PSA 90:3–90:10; ECC 12:1–12:7; JAS 1:10; 1PE 1:24

The imperfection of the spiritual creation

JOB 4:18; JUD 1:6 See also 2PE 2:4

The universal imperfection of human beings

ROM 3:23 See also GEN 6:5; 1KI 8:46 parallel 2CH 6:36; PSA 14:3, PSA 130:3; PRO 20:9; ECC 7:20; ISA 53:6, ISA 64:6; GAL 3:22; JAS 3:2

The imperfection of the household of faith

Israel and her leaders

NEH 9:34; JER 2:21 See also JDG 2:11–2:13; NEH 9:16; PSA 106:39; ISA 3:14; JER 50:6; EZK 20:30; MIC 3:1–3:3

The Christian church and its leaders

2CO 12:20; PHP 3:12 See also 1CO 3:1–3:3, 1CO 13:12; COL 2:20; HEB 5:12; 1JN 1:8; REV 3:2, REV 3:17

title: 6201: imperfection, and God's purposes
content:
The unacceptable falling short of the God-given ideal must be resisted and overcome.

Imperfection is contrary to God's purpose

In creation

GEN 1:31

For individuals

EPH 1:4

For the church

EPH 5:25–5:27

Imperfection is unacceptable in God's presence

Sacrificial animals must be unblemished

DEU 15:21; MAL 1:8 See also EXO 12:5; LEV 22:20–22:25; NUM 28:31; DEU 17:1

The priests must be physically perfect

LEV 21:17–21:23

Moral imperfection separates from God

ISA 59:2 See also PSA 66:18; ISA 50:1, ISA 64:7; 1CO 6:9–6:10; EPH 5:5; HEB 10:26–10:27; REV 21:27, REV 22:15

God's people should strive against imperfection

MAT 5:48 See also GEN 17:1; DEU 18:13; PSA 34:14; ROM 12:9; 2CO 13:11; COL 1:28, COL 3:5; 1TH 5:22; 2TI 2:19; HEB 6:1, HEB 12:14; 2PE 3:14

Imperfection will be dealt with at Jesus Christ's return

Creation will be remade

ISA 65:17 See also ISA 66:22; MAT 19:28; ROM 8:19–8:21; 2PE 3:13; REV 21:1–21:5

God's people will be perfected

1JN 3:2 See also PHP 3:20–3:21

Evil will be removed

REV 21:4 See also ISA 25:8, ISA 65:19; 2TH 2:8; REV 20:10, REV 21:8

title: 6203: mortality
content:
The human state of being subject to death and decay. God did not create human beings to be like this; mortality is the consequence of sin and is countered by the gospel promise of eternal life in Jesus Christ.

Mortality originated in the fall

GEN 2:16–2:17

It is the decree of God

PSA 90:3, PSA 90:5 See also GEN 3:19, GEN 6:3

It is universal

ECC 3:20 See also 1CO 15:22

It is inevitable

2SA 14:14; ; JOB 30:23; ; ECC 3:2; ; ROM 6:23

It is a judgment from God

ROM 5:12 See also ROM 5:15–5:19

It leads to judgment

HEB 9:27 See also 2CO 5:10

Mortality extends to the creation

ROM 8:20–8:21

Human beings likened to the animals

ECC 3:19

Human beings likened to the grass

PSA 90:5–90:6, PSA 103:15–103:16; ; ISA 40:6–40:7; ; 1PE 1:24; ; JAS 1:10

The natural reaction to mortality

A sense of oppression

JOB 10:8–10:9

Carefree abandon

1CO 15:32 See also ISA 22:13

The godly reaction to mortality

Humility

JOB 20:6–20:11; ; PSA 22:29, PSA 89:48; ; ECC 3:21–3:22

Trust

LUK 12:25

The pursuit of wisdom

PSA 90:12

Saving faith

ISA 55:6 See also PSA 39:4–39:8; 2CO 6:2

The struggle with sin

ROM 6:12, ROM 7:14–7:25

Death is not the end

The spirit returns to God

ECC 12:7 See also PHP 1:23

The body will be raised

DAN 12:2; 1CO 15:53–15:54 See also ISA 25:8; ROM 8:11; 1CO 15:42–15:44; 2CO 5:4

Eternal life through Jesus Christ

JHN 11:25–11:26 See also MAT 25:46; 2TI 1:9–1:10; 1JN 5:11–5:12; REV 22:3–22:5

Eternal punishment for the wicked

MAT 25:46; ; REV 14:11, REV 20:10, REV 20:15

title: 6206: offence
content:
A transgression or affront against the law or an individual. The gospel itself is seen as offensive by unbelievers, who are often unable to cope with its radical challenge to their unbelief.

Legal offence

DEU 21:22–21:23 See also EXO 21:15–21:25, EXO 22:1–22:5; DEU 19:15–19:21

Moral offence

EZK 22:11 See also EXO 22:16–22:17; LEV 18:6–18:23; DEU 22:13–22:30; 2SA 3:8; 1CO 6:9–6:10

An offence to others

On a national level

1SA 13:4 See also 2SA 10:6 parallel 1CH 19:6; JER 24:9

On a personal level

2SA 16:21 See also GEN 20:1–20:16, GEN 40:1; 1SA 25:14–25:28 Abigail seeks David's forgiveness for Nabal's offence; JOB 19:17; PRO 17:9, PRO 18:19, PRO 19:11; MAT 13:54–13:57 parallel MRK 6:1–6:4 The people of Nazareth take offence at Jesus Christ; MAT 15:1–15:12, MAT 17:24–17:27; JHN 6:53–6:66

Offence against God

1KI 8:50–8:51 Offence against God is synonymous with sin. See also JOB 7:21, JOB 10:14, JOB 13:23 “wrongs”, “sins” and “offence” are three important OT terms for sin; JOB 14:16–14:17, JOB 34:31–34:33; PSA 59:3, PSA 139:24; ISA 43:24, ISA 44:22, ISA 59:12; EZK 18:1–18:32 Ezekiel warns that the soul who sins will die; EZK 33:10, EZK 37:23, EZK 39:24; AMO 5:12

Offence against God's house

NUM 18:1 See also NUM 18:23

The things of God are an offence to sinful people

JER 6:10; 1CO 1:22–1:24 See also GAL 5:11

A stumbling-block as an object of offence

A stumbling-block for others

ROM 14:13 The concept behind the English word “offence” is one of striking against something or someone. The image of a stumbling-block conveys the idea of tripping or falling as a result of striking against such an object. See also LEV 19:14; MAT 16:23; ROM 11:9–11:10; PSA 69:22–69:23; 1CO 8:9; 2CO 6:3

A stumbling-block for oneself

EZK 14:7 See also EZK 14:3–14:4

A stumbling-block set up by God

EZK 3:20

title: 6209: pagans
content:
OT use of the term ``pagan''

Used of foreign adherents of heathen religions

ISA 2:6 See also 2KI 23:4–23:7; ZEP 1:4–1:6

Used of heathen religions, peoples and countries

ISA 57:8 See also LAM 1:10; AMO 7:17

In the Gospels pagans'' is synonymous withGentiles''

MAT 6:7 See also MAT 5:47, MAT 6:32 parallel LUK 12:30; MAT 18:17

The term ``pagans'' is used to refer to people who were neither Jews nor Christians

Pagans as idolaters

1CO 12:2 See also 1CO 10:7; EXO 32:6; 1CO 10:20

Pagans as immoral

1PE 4:3 See also 1CO 5:1

Many believers lived among the pagans

1PE 2:12 See also 3JN 1:7

title: 6212: participation
content: Sharing in actions or identifying with actions or people. All human beings share in Adam's fall and its consequences; believers share in Christ and his life.

title: 6213: participation, in sin
content:
The natural condition of all human beings as a result of sharing in Adam's fall. Believers are called not to associate with, nor give approval to wickedness but to stand apart from evil as God's holy people.
