Participation in Adam

In Adam's humanity

GEN 3:20, GEN 5:1–5:3; ; JHN 3:6; ; ACT 17:26; ; 1CO 15:48–15:49

In Adam's sin

ROM 5:12 See also HOS 6:7; ROM 5:15–5:19; 1CO 15:21–15:22GEN 3:17; ROM 8:20–8:21

In a sinful nature

EPH 2:3 See also ROM 7:5, ROM 7:14–7:20

Ways of participating in evil

Associating with evildoers

PSA 50:18 See also GEN 49:6; LEV 15:31; JOS 23:12–23:13; JOB 34:8; PSA 141:4; PRO 22:24–22:25; 2JN 1:10–1:11 giving hospitality to evildoers

Giving approval to evil

LEV 19:17; PSA 64:5; ROM 1:32 See also 2SA 3:28–3:29, 2SA 3:37; PSA 49:13; EZK 13:22; LUK 11:48; ACT 7:60, ACT 22:20; ROM 14:22; EPH 5:11

Sacrifice and idolatry

NUM 25:1–25:3 See also 2KI 17:33; HOS 4:17; 1CO 10:18–10:20

God's people are not to participate in evil

God's people are called to be holy

LEV 20:26 See also LEV 11:44–11:45, LEV 19:2; 1PE 1:15–1:16

Separation from foreign gods

JOS 23:7 See also DEU 12:30, DEU 29:18; 1KI 11:2

Separation from the godless

EZR 10:11 Too close an association with the nations round about, particularly in marriage, would cause Israel to compromise her unique relationship with God; 2CO 6:14 Often related to marriage, this applies to all close alliances with unbelievers. See also EZR 4:1–4:3, EZR 9:1–9:2REV 18:4 See also EZR 10:2; NEH 2:20, NEH 10:30, NEH 13:26–13:27; JER 51:45; 1CO 7:39; 2CO 6:17; ISA 52:11

Separation from evildoers

1TI 5:22 See also ROM 16:17; 1CO 5:9–5:11; 2TH 3:14

title: 6214: participation, in Christ
content:
The joining of believers to Jesus Christ, through faith, whereby they are freed from participation in Adam by sharing in Christ's death and resurrection, his nature, sufferings and glory and whereby, too, they become part of his body. Believers therefore share in the fellowship, ministry and hope of God's people, which is anticipated by the OT and fulfilled in the church.

Believers are united with Christ

EPH 1:13 See also JHN 13:8; 1CO 1:9, 1CO 6:17; HEB 3:14

Faith liberates from participation in sin

ROM 5:15; GAL 5:24 See also ROM 5:16–5:21; 1CO 15:21–15:22; COL 2:11

Participation in Christ

In Christ's death and resurrection

ROM 6:3–6:4; GAL 2:20 See also ROM 6:5–6:8, ROM 7:4; GAL 3:27; COL 2:12–2:13; 2TI 2:11

In Christ's nature

2PE 1:4 See also ROM 8:29; 1CO 15:49; 2CO 3:18, 2CO 5:17; COL 3:10; HEB 12:10

In Christ's sufferings

PHP 3:10 See also MAT 16:24 parallels MRK 8:34, LUK 9:23; 2CO 1:5; 1PE 4:13

In Christ's glory

ROM 8:17 See also JHN 17:22; EPH 2:6; 2TH 2:14; 2TI 2:12

Participation in Christ's body

1CO 10:16–10:17 See also ROM 12:5; 1CO 12:27

Sharing unity through Christ

GAL 3:28 See also 1CO 1:2; EPH 2:21–2:22; COL 3:11

Sharing a source of life

EPH 4:16 See also JHN 15:4–15:5; ROM 11:17; COL 2:19

Sharing an inheritance

GAL 3:29 See also 1SA 26:19; PSA 106:5; ACT 3:25, ACT 20:32; COL 1:12; HEB 6:17

Sharing spiritual blessings

EPH 1:3 See also ROM 15:27; PHP 1:7; HEB 6:4; JUD 1:3

Sharing a hope

1PE 5:1 See also LUK 20:35; 2TH 1:10; REV 20:6

Sharing in worship

1CO 14:26 See also 2CH 5:12–5:13; NEH 12:24

Accepting responsibility towards others

1CO 12:26 See also GAL 6:1–6:2; PHP 2:1–2:4

Sharing one another's sufferings

2CO 1:4, 2CO 1:7; ; PHP 4:14; ; 2TI 1:8; ; HEB 10:33–10:34; ; REV 1:9

Sharing with those in need

EZK 45:16; ; ACT 4:32; ; 2CO 8:4, 2CO 9:13; ; 1TI 6:18; ; HEB 13:16

Sharing in God's work

2CO 6:1; PHP 1:5 Paul thanks the Philippians for their active support of his ministry. See also NUM 8:24, NUM 18:2; 1CH 16:38; EZR 3:2; ROM 15:30; 1CO 9:10–9:13; 2CO 5:20; GAL 2:7–2:9; PHP 4:15–4:16

Participation in Christ demands holiness

1CO 10:21 See also 1CO 6:15; EPH 5:6–5:7; COL 3:1–3:5

Participation of the nations in God's salvation

Gentiles are included among God's people

EPH 3:6 See also ROM 11:24; GAL 3:8–3:9; GEN 12:3, GEN 18:18, GEN 22:18; GAL 3:14

God's promise to include the nations

ZEC 2:11 See also ISA 2:3 parallel MIC 4:2; ISA 14:1, ISA 19:23–19:25; ZEP 2:11; ZEC 14:16

title: 6218: provoking God
content:
The arousing of God's righteous anger in response to the sin of his people, particularly their idolatry.

God is provoked by sin and disobedience

DEU 9:7–9:8 See also NUM 32:9–32:13; DEU 31:29; JOS 7:1; 2SA 6:6–6:7 parallel 1CH 13:9–13:10 Uzzah's disobedience in touching the ark cost him his life; 2SA 24:1; 1KI 15:30, 1KI 16:2–16:3, 1KI 16:7, 1KI 21:21–21:22; 2KI 21:14–21:15; ISA 65:1–65:5; JER 32:30–32:33

God is provoked by the rejection of his chosen leaders and prophets

2CH 36:15–36:16 See also NUM 12:1, NUM 12:9

God is provoked by injustice

EXO 22:22–22:24 See also ISA 59:15–59:18; EZK 8:17; HOS 12:14

God is provoked by complaining

NUM 11:1 See also EXO 4:10–4:14; NUM 11:4–11:10, NUM 11:33

God is provoked by idolatry

DEU 4:25–4:26; EZK 16:26 See also DEU 9:16–9:19; JDG 2:12–2:13, JDG 3:5–3:8; 1KI 14:9, 1KI 14:15, 1KI 16:13, 1KI 16:26, 1KI 16:33, 1KI 22:53; 2KI 17:11, 2KI 22:17, 2KI 23:25–23:27; 2CH 28:25; PSA 78:58–78:59, PSA 106:28–106:29; JER 7:17–7:19, JER 8:19, JER 25:6–25:7, JER 44:3, JER 44:8; EZK 20:28

God is provoked by human sacrifice

2KI 17:17, 2KI 21:6 parallel 2CH 33:6

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 6221: rebellion
content: A state of revolt against established divine or human authority. Scripture condemns rebellion against God and indicates the futility of Israel's frequent rebellions against him.

title: 6222: rebellion, against God
content:
The human race is in rebellion against God through its disobedience and unbelief.

Examples of rebellion against God

PSA 2:2 See also NUM 20:24, NUM 27:14; 1SA 15:22–15:23; PSA 66:7

Warnings not to rebel

1SA 12:14–12:15 See also NUM 14:9, NUM 17:10; PSA 2:10–2:12; EZK 2:8

Indignation expressed over rebellion against God

PSA 5:10

The fate of the rebellious

ISA 66:24 See also DEU 13:5; PSA 68:6; JER 28:16, JER 29:32; LAM 1:18, LAM 1:20, LAM 3:42; HOS 13:16

God is ready to forgive those who rebel against him

JER 33:8 See also DAN 9:5–9:9

The end times will be characterised by open rebellion against God

2TH 2:3 See also 1JN 2:18

resource.label: 6157: fall, of Satan and his angels

title: 6223: rebellion, of Israel
content:
Despite God's repeated mercy, Israel's history is seen as a history of rebellion against God.

The history of Israel is seen as a history of rebellion

PSA 78:40 See also PSA 78:8, PSA 78:17, PSA 78:56; DEU 31:27; PSA 105:28, PSA 106:7, PSA 107:11; ISA 63:10; EZK 20:8, EZK 20:13, EZK 20:21

God's compassion and readiness to deliver his people

NEH 9:28 See also NEH 9:16–9:31; PSA 106:43

Examples of Israel's rebellion

DEU 1:26, DEU 1:43, DEU 9:7, DEU 9:23

Prophetic condemnation of Israel's rebellion

HOS 7:13 See also ZEP 3:1

Isaiah depicts Israel as a rebellious nation

ISA 1:2–1:5 See also ISA 1:20, ISA 1:23, ISA 1:28, ISA 30:1, ISA 30:9, ISA 48:8, ISA 65:2–65:3

Jeremiah denounces the Israelites as rebels

JER 6:28 See also JER 2:29, JER 3:13, JER 4:17, JER 5:23

Ezekiel condemns Israel as a rebellious house

EZK 2:5–2:8 See also EZK 3:9, EZK 3:26–3:27, EZK 12:2–12:3, EZK 12:9, EZK 12:25, EZK 17:12, EZK 24:3, EZK 44:6

The rebellion of Israel in the parables of Jesus Christ

MAT 21:33–21:44 parallels MRK 12:1–12:11, LUK 20:9–20:18; ; LUK 13:6–13:9

Israel's rebellion is a warning to believers

1CO 10:6 See also 1CO 10:1–10:12; HEB 3:7–4:2; PSA 95:7–95:11; JUD 1:5, JUD 1:7

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 6224: rebellion, against human authority
content:
Human authority is frequently defied and resisted. To rebel against God's chosen leaders is to defy God himself.

Examples of rebellion against human leaders

GEN 14:3–14:4 See also 2KI 18:7, 2KI 24:1, 2KI 24:20 parallels 2CH 36:13, JER 52:3; 2CH 13:6; MRK 15:7

Rebellion against parents

DEU 21:18–21:21

The rebellion of nations

1KI 12:19 parallel 2CH 10:19; ; 2KI 1:1

Accusations of rebellion

NEH 2:19, NEH 6:5–6:8

Rebellion against God's chosen leaders

EXO 23:21 See also NUM 16:1–16:3, NUM 20:2–20:5; JOS 1:18; 2SA 15:10

Rebellion against legitimate authority is condemned

ROM 13:2 See also 1PE 2:13

title: 6227: regret
content:
Deep feelings of sorrow arising from past events now seen as sins, mistakes or misfortunes. It can either lead to true repentance or destructive bitterness.

Examples and expressions of regret

1SA 26:21 See also GEN 27:33–27:35; NUM 12:10–12:12 Aaron and Miriam when they had spoken against Moses; NUM 14:1–14:2; JDG 11:34–11:35 Jephthah when he returned home from fighting the Ammonites2SA 24:10 parallel 1CH 21:8; 2SA 24:14 parallel 1CH 21:13 1KI 21:27 Ahab after he murdered Naboth and seized his vineyard; DAN 6:14; MAT 26:75 parallels MRK 14:72, LUK 22:61–22:62 Peter when he disowned Jesus Christ; MAT 27:3–27:4 Judas after betraying Jesus Christ

Divine expressions of regret

GEN 6:6 See also 1SA 15:10–15:11, 1SA 15:35; 2SA 24:16 parallel 1CH 21:15

Actions which may lead to regret

PRO 5:11–5:14; ; 1TI 6:9–6:10

The bitterness of vain regrets

There is no remedy for bitter regrets

HEB 12:16–12:17 See also GEN 27:41; 2SA 18:33; PRO 28:17; EZK 7:16–7:18; LUK 13:24–13:28; REV 1:7

The unending remorse of the lost

DAN 12:2 See also ISA 66:24; MAT 8:12, MAT 25:30; MRK 9:47–9:48; LUK 16:22–16:23; REV 14:11

Regret is an element of true repentance

2CO 7:8–7:10 See also NUM 14:39–14:40; 2SA 12:13–12:17; 2KI 22:18–22:20 parallel 2CH 34:26–34:28 Josiah's response on finding the Book of the Law; EZR 10:1, EZR 10:6; PSA 38:2–38:8, PSA 51:3; JER 3:21–3:25; JOL 2:12; JON 3:6–3:9 the repentance of Nineveh; MAT 5:4; LUK 15:17–15:19 the prodigal son; ACT 2:37

Absence of regret

Sinning without shame

JER 6:15; ; ZEP 3:5; ; 1CO 5:1–5:2

When the wicked die

2CH 21:20

At the end of life

2SA 23:5; ; 2TI 4:6–4:8

In God's new creation

ISA 65:17–65:19 See also ISA 35:10; REV 21:4

title: 6230: rejection
content: The deliberate and conscious act of turning away from someone, or refusing to accept something that is being offered.

title: 6231: rejection of God
content:
The deliberate rejection of God lies at the heart of human sin. It is seen in rebellion against God's authority, purpose and instruction and results in the rejection of God's servants and, ultimately, in the rejection of his Son.

Rejection of God

Rejection of God's authority

2KI 17:15 See also ISA 1:4

Rejection of God in favour of other gods

DEU 32:15–32:18 See also EXO 22:20; DEU 31:20; 1SA 8:8; ISA 1:28, ISA 65:11; JER 15:6; EZK 14:5

Rejection of God as king

1SA 8:7 See also 1SA 10:19

Rejection of God's ways

PSA 95:10; ISA 30:15 See also EXO 16:7–16:8; NUM 11:18–11:20, NUM 14:27; PSA 81:11–81:13; ISA 8:6, ISA 30:1–30:2; LUK 7:30

Rejection of the covenant

1KI 19:10 See also LEV 26:15; 1KI 19:14; HOS 8:1

Rejection of the promised land

NUM 14:1–14:4, NUM 14:31; ; NEH 9:30

Rejection of God's compassion

MAT 23:37 See also 2CH 36:15; NEH 9:30; HOS 11:1–11:4

Rejection of God's word

Rejection of God's instruction

1SA 15:10–15:11; 1TH 4:8 See also 1SA 15:23, 1SA 15:26; PSA 50:17; PRO 1:24–1:25, PRO 5:12

Rejection of God's law

LEV 26:43; HOS 4:6 See also 2KI 17:26; ISA 5:24; JER 6:19, JER 8:8–8:9; EZK 5:6, EZK 20:13, EZK 20:16, EZK 20:24; AMO 2:4; MRK 7:13 parallel MAT 15:6

Rejection of God's messages

ISA 30:12 See also ISA 65:12; JER 6:10, JER 7:13, JER 35:17, JER 36:23–36:24; ACT 13:46

Rejection of God's messengers

2CH 36:16; ACT 7:52 See also 2CH 30:10; JER 20:8; ZEC 7:12; MAT 21:33–21:39 parallels MRK 12:1–12:8, LUK 20:9–20:15; ACT 7:35, ACT 7:39

Rejection of Jesus Christ

Jesus Christ's rejection was prophesied

PSA 118:22; ISA 53:3 It is generally accepted that God's servant in Isaiah chapter 53 refers to Jesus Christ; MRK 8:31 parallels MAT 16:21, LUK 9:22 See also ISA 49:7, ISA 50:6, ISA 52:14, ISA 53:7–53:8MAT 21:42 parallels MRK 12:10, LUK 20:17; ACT 4:11; 1PE 2:7 MRK 9:12 parallel MAT 17:12; LUK 17:25; ROM 9:33; ISA 28:16

The rejection of his earthly ministry

JHN 1:11 MAT 13:54–13:58 parallel MRK 6:1–6:6; ; LUK 4:28–4:29; JHN 5:40, JHN 5:43, JHN 6:41–6:42, JHN 8:59, JHN 9:16, JHN 10:31; MAT 27:22–27:23 parallels MRK 15:12–15:14, LUK 23:20–23:23; ; JHN 11:53, JHN 19:6, JHN 19:15; ; ACT 2:23, ACT 4:10, ACT 7:52

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 6232: rejection of God, results of
content:
Rejecting God angers and grieves him. To reject God can be to reject all the benefits of the gospel, and can lead to judgment and punishment.

Rejection grieves God

God rejected as Creator

GEN 6:6

God rejected as a husband

EZK 6:9 See also JER 2:2, JER 3:20, JER 31:32; HOS 1:2

God rejected as a father

HOS 11:1–11:4 See also DEU 32:18; JER 3:19, JER 31:20

God rejected by Israel, his vineyard

ISA 5:4 See also JER 2:21

Rejection angers God and brings his wrath

EZR 8:22; JHN 3:36 See also LEV 26:27–26:28; 2CH 36:16; ZEC 7:12; ROM 2:8

The results of rejecting God

Judgment

JER 1:16; JHN 12:48 See also LEV 26:14–26:17; PSA 50:22; PRO 1:24–1:26; ISA 28:22; JER 6:19, JER 15:6, JER 26:4–26:6; AMO 2:4–2:5; JHN 3:18–3:19

God rejects his people

DEU 32:19; ; PSA 78:59, PSA 119:118; ; JER 6:30, JER 7:29; ; HOS 9:7

God rejects those who reject him

HOS 4:6; MAT 10:33 parallel LUK 12:9 See also 1CH 28:9; 2CH 15:2; LUK 9:26

God hides his face

DEU 31:17–31:18, DEU 32:20; ; PSA 88:14; ; ISA 1:15; ; MIC 3:4

God rejects the sanctuary and its worship

LEV 26:31; ; 1KI 9:6–9:7 parallel 2CH 7:19–7:20; ; LAM 2:7; ; EZK 7:24, EZK 10:18–10:19, EZK 11:22–11:23

God's people are removed from his presence into exile

2KI 17:18–17:20, 2KI 17:23, 2KI 23:27, 2KI 24:3, 2KI 24:20 parallel JER 52:3; ; JER 7:15, JER 15:1

Examples of others rejected by God

Esau: ROM 9:13; ; MAL 1:2–1:3; 1SA 15:23, 1SA 15:26, 1SA 16:1, 1SA 28:15–28:16; JER 22:24–22:25 Jehoiachin

God's rejection is not final

LEV 26:44; ROM 11:15 See also 1SA 12:22; 2CH 7:14; PSA 66:20; ISA 41:9, ISA 49:21, ISA 54:5–54:8; JER 31:37, JER 33:24–33:26; ZEC 10:6; ROM 11:1, ROM 11:25–11:29

resource.label: 9511: hell, as a place of punishment

title: 6233: rejection, human experience of
content:
Feelings of rejection by God may be a result of alienation caused directly or indirectly by sin. God's people also experience rejection by a hostile world. Experiences and feelings of rejection are to be met with prayer, forgiveness and perseverance, together with confidence in God's commitment to and compassion for, his people.

Feelings of rejection by God

Feelings of national rejection

PSA 74:1; ISA 49:14 See also PSA 44:9, PSA 108:11; ISA 40:27; JER 13:17, JER 14:9; LAM 1:2, LAM 5:20; EZK 37:11

Feelings of personal rejection

PSA 13:1; MAT 27:46 parallel MRK 15:34 Jesus Christ experiences separation from his Father as a consequence of bearing human sin. See also PSA 22:1–22:2, PSA 43:2, PSA 88:14

God's servants experience rejection

Samuel

1SA 8:5–8:7

Elijah

1KI 19:10 See also 1KI 18:22, 1KI 19:1–19:4

The psalmist

PSA 41:7 See also PSA 22:6–22:8, PSA 31:12–31:13, PSA 69:20, PSA 71:10

Jeremiah

JER 15:10 See also JER 20:7–20:9, JER 20:14–20:15, JER 20:18

Jesus Christ

ISA 53:5; JHN 1:11 See also LUK 23:18

Jesus Christ's disciples

JHN 15:18 See also MAT 5:10–5:11 parallel LUK 6:22; JHN 17:14; ACT 13:50, ACT 14:19, ACT 16:22; 2TI 3:12; 1PE 4:12

Responding to rejection

Appealing to God

2CH 6:42; PSA 27:9, PSA 71:12 See also PSA 22:9–22:11, PSA 22:19–22:21, PSA 44:23–44:24, PSA 51:11–51:12, PSA 77:7–77:12, PSA 132:10; JER 15:15, JER 20:12; LAM 5:21–5:22

Offering forgiveness

LUK 23:34 See also GEN 50:17; ACT 7:59–7:60; 2TI 4:16

Rejoicing in suffering

MAT 5:11–5:12 parallel LUK 6:22–6:23; ; ACT 5:41; ; ROM 5:3; ; JAS 1:2–1:3

Persevering

ACT 5:28–5:29, ACT 5:42

Encouragement for those experiencing rejection

God's commitment to his people

ISA 49:15–49:16 See also PSA 27:10, PSA 94:14; JER 31:20; LAM 3:21–3:24; HOS 11:1–11:4; MAT 10:30–10:31 parallel LUK 12:7

God's compassion and forgiveness

LAM 3:31–3:32 See also DEU 32:36; PSA 130:7–130:8; ISA 12:1–12:2, ISA 49:13; EZK 39:25; HOS 11:8–11:9; 2CO 2:7–2:8 God's readiness to forgive is to be reflected in the attitude of his people towards one another.

God's strengthening

ISA 40:28–40:31; ; 2TI 4:17

God's vindication of his people

PSA 135:14; ; ISA 50:6–50:9, ISA 54:17; ; ROM 8:18; ; 1PE 4:13; ; REV 2:10

Support from fellow believers

2CO 1:4 See also ACT 14:22; 2CO 6:6–6:7

title: 6236: sexual sin
content: Sexual relations are to be seen as an aspect of God's good creation which can be corrupted and devalued through human sin.

title: 6237: sexual sin, nature of
content:
Any transgression of the limits that God has set for the enjoyment of sex. Such sins are often life-dominating, but may be broken through the power and grace of Jesus Christ.

The origin of sexual sin

Fallen human nature

GAL 5:19 See also ROM 13:12–13:14; COL 3:5–3:6

The heart

MAT 15:19 parallel MRK 7:21–7:22

The offensiveness of sexual sin

It defiles the sinner

MAT 15:20 parallel MRK 7:23 See also ROM 1:24; 1CO 6:18

It defiles society

LEV 18:24–18:25 See also 1CO 5:6; REV 19:2

It offends other people

2PE 2:7–2:8 See also GEN 9:22–9:25, GEN 34:7, GEN 38:24; 2SA 13:21–13:22; 2CO 12:21

It offends God

2SA 11:27 See also JER 13:26–13:27; MAL 3:5

Sexual sin under the old covenant

Pre-marital sex

DEU 22:13–22:21

Adultery

EXO 20:14 parallel DEU 5:18 See also LEV 18:20; DEU 22:22–22:24

Prostitution

LEV 19:29; ; DEU 23:17–23:18

Rape

DEU 22:25–22:29

Homosexual intercourse

LEV 18:22 See also LEV 20:13

Bestiality

EXO 22:19; ; LEV 18:23, LEV 20:15–20:16; ; DEU 27:21

Incest

LEV 18:6–18:18, LEV 20:17, LEV 20:19; ; DEU 22:30, DEU 27:20, DEU 27:22–27:23; ; EZK 22:11

Intercourse during menstruation

LEV 18:19, LEV 20:18

Sexual sin under the new covenant

The OT laws are confirmed and strengthened

MAT 5:27–5:28 See also MAT 5:31–5:32; ROM 1:24–1:27; 1CO 6:15–6:16, 1CO 7:9; HEB 13:4

The need for holiness in the lives of believers

1TH 4:3–4:7 See also ACT 15:29; EPH 5:3; HEB 12:16; 1PE 1:15–1:16; LEV 11:44; 1PE 4:1–4:3

The church must be kept pure

REV 2:14–2:16 See also 1CO 5:1–5:5, 1CO 5:9–5:11; REV 2:20

God's judgment on sexual sin

In this life

REV 2:21–2:23 See also GEN 19:24; NUM 25:1–25:9; 2SA 12:11–12:12; PRO 7:26–7:27; 1CO 10:8

In the world to come

REV 21:8 See also EPH 5:5–5:6; 2PE 2:6; JUD 1:7; REV 22:14–22:15

God's power over sexual sin

It can be forgiven

JHN 8:10–8:11 See also 2SA 12:13; PSA 51:0; MAT 21:31–21:32; LUK 7:36–7:38, LUK 7:47–7:50; REV 2:21

Hearts can be changed

1CO 6:9–6:11

Resisting sexual sin

By remembering God's word

PSA 119:9 See also PRO 6:23–6:24, PRO 7:5

By resisting temptation

GEN 39:6–39:12; ; PRO 7:24–7:25

By mortifying lust

JOB 31:1 See also MAT 5:29–5:30

By living in the Spirit

GAL 5:16

By living for holiness

ROM 6:19

By enjoying sex within proper limits

PRO 5:18–5:20; ; 1CO 7:2, 1CO 7:9

title: 6238: homosexuality
content:
Sexual relations between people of the same sex. The biblical emphasis upon the loving union of male and female, as an integral part of God's creation ordinance, establishes the context against which the censure of homosexual practice is to be set.

God's creation order for sexuality

GEN 1:27–1:28, GEN 2:18–2:24; MRK 10:6–10:9 parallel MAT 19:4–19:5; ; EPH 5:31

OT prohibitions of homosexual practice

LEV 18:22 See also LEV 20:13

Homosexual practice is incompatible with the kingdom of God

1CO 6:9–6:10 The meanings of the Greek words for “male prostitutes” and “homosexual offenders” are disputed, but the terms are commonly associated with particular forms of homosexual behaviour. See also 1TI 1:9–1:11

Sexual disorder is one consequence of rejecting God

ROM 1:21–1:27 1KI 14:24, 1KI 15:12; ; 2KI 23:7

Examples of homosexual practice

In Sodom and Gomorrah: GEN 19:4–19:8; ; JUD 1:7; JDG 19:16–19:24 in Israel in a time of moral decadence

title: 6239: prostitution
content:
Scripture condemns the offering of sex for money or favours. However, it also stresses the prospect of new life in Christ for prostitutes, and points to some prostitutes as examples of faith.

Prostitution among the heathen

For money

GEN 38:15–38:16; ; JOS 2:1; ; JOL 3:3

Religious prostitution

GEN 38:21–38:22; ; JOB 36:14

Prostitution in Israel

It was forbidden under God's law

LEV 19:29; DEU 23:17–23:18

Priests were forbidden to marry prostitutes

See also LEV 21:7, LEV 21:14

The penalties for prostitution

GEN 38:24; ; LEV 21:9

Efforts to remove the shrine-prostitutes

1KI 15:11–15:12, 1KI 22:45–22:46

The lure of prostitution

PRO 7:10 See also 1CO 6:9

The prevalaence of prostitution

1KI 14:24 See also 1KI 3:16; JER 5:7; HOS 4:13–4:14, HOS 6:10; MIC 1:7

Warnings against prostitution

It has dire consequences

PRO 6:25–6:27 See also JDG 16:1–16:2; PRO 23:26–23:27, PRO 29:3; LUK 15:30

Christians must avoid it

1CO 6:15–6:16

Prostitutes can be redeemed

MAT 21:31–21:32 See also LUK 7:37–7:50; 1CO 6:9–6:11; HEB 11:31; JAS 2:25; JOS 6:22–6:25

Prostitution as a metaphor

For worldly corruption

REV 17:1–17:5 See also ISA 23:15–23:17; NAM 3:4

For apostasy

DEU 31:16 See also EXO 34:15–34:16; LEV 20:5; JDG 2:17, JDG 8:27, JDG 8:33; 1CH 5:25; PSA 106:39; ISA 1:21; EZK 16:16–16:17, EZK 16:26, EZK 16:28, EZK 16:33–16:34, EZK 16:41, EZK 23:1–23:35; HOS 4:10–4:12, HOS 5:4

title: 6240: rape
content:
The sexual violation of an individual (normally, though not necessarily, female). This is a deeply traumatic and degrading experience for the victim and is an evil that Scripture condemns.

Lust is the motivation for rape

2SA 13:1–13:15

Women and rape

Women are particularly vulnerable to male assault

2SA 13:14 See also ZEC 14:2

Women were protected under the law

DEU 22:25–22:29

Women are to be loved and cared for

EPH 5:25, EPH 5:28–5:29 See also GEN 12:18–12:20; 1PE 3:7

The trauma of rape

Its psychological and emotional effects

2SA 13:19–13:20 See also GEN 34:6–34:7

Its physical effects

JDG 19:26–19:28 See also JDG 20:4–20:5

Avenging the victim of rape

GEN 34:7–34:31; ; 2SA 13:22–13:32; ; JDG 20:6–20:11; ; ROM 12:19, ROM 13:3–13:4; ; JAS 1:20

Examples of rape

Female rape

GEN 34:1–34:3; ; JDG 19:24–19:25; ; 2SA 13:2–13:13; ; ZEC 14:2

Attempted male rape

GEN 19:4–19:9; ; JDG 19:22–19:23; ; LEV 18:22, LEV 20:13

title: 6241: seduction
content:
The deliberate enticement or persuasion of someone into the abandonment of godly principles. Scripture speaks of both sexual and spiritual seduction.

Sexual seduction

Warnings to avoid temptation

PRO 7:1–7:22; 2TI 2:22 See also PRO 1:10, PRO 2:16–2:19

Examples of enticement

GEN 39:6–39:12; NUM 25:1–25:3 See also NUM 31:15–31:16; JDG 16:1, JDG 16:4–16:5; 2SA 11:1–11:4; 1KI 11:1–11:6; REV 2:14–2:16, REV 2:20–2:22

The law as a deterrent

EXO 22:16–22:17 See also HEB 13:4

Resisting temptation

JOB 31:1 See also MAL 2:14; MAT 5:27–5:30; 1CO 7:2–7:3; PHP 4:8; 1TH 4:3–4:6

Spiritual seduction

Through Satan

GEN 3:1 See also JHN 8:44; 2CO 2:11, 2CO 11:3, 2CO 11:14; EPH 6:11

Through lying spirits and false prophets

1JN 4:1 See also 1KI 22:19–22:23 parallel 2CH 18:18–18:22; 1TI 4:1; MAT 24:24–24:25 parallel MRK 13:22–13:23

Through false teachers

2PE 2:1, 2PE 2:14–2:15 See also 2TI 3:6–3:7, 2TI 3:13; 1JN 2:18–2:19; 2JN 1:7–1:8; JUD 1:10–1:12

The need for vigilance

1TH 5:21–5:22 See also ACT 20:28–20:31

The process of seduction

A thought planted

GEN 3:2–3:3 See also MAT 4:3; JAS 1:14; 1JN 2:15

An advantage promised

GEN 3:4–3:5 See also MAT 4:6

Attraction

GEN 3:6 See also 1JN 2:16

Persistence on the part of the seducer

MAT 4:8–4:9 See also GEN 19:9; JDG 16:15–16:16

Yielding

GEN 3:6 See also JAS 1:15

title: 6242: adultery
content:
Sexual relations involving at least one partner who is married to someone else. Such relations break the marriage covenant and are condemned in Scripture. Jesus Christ extended the understanding of adultery to include inner lust.

Adultery is condemned by God

God's law forbids it

EXO 20:14 parallel DEU 5:18 See also LEV 18:20; MAT 19:18 parallels MRK 10:19, LUK 18:20; JAS 2:11

It is listed with other evil practices

PSA 50:18; ; JER 7:9–7:10; ; HOS 4:1–4:2; ; LUK 18:11; ; 1TI 1:9–1:10

The lure of adultery

It begins with lust

PRO 6:25; MAT 5:27–5:28 See also 2SA 11:2–11:3; JOB 31:1, JOB 31:9; JER 5:8; MAT 15:19

It is seductive

PRO 7:14–7:21 See also PRO 5:3, PRO 5:20

It is secretive

JOB 24:15; ; PRO 7:8–7:10, PRO 30:20

It is a snare to the unwary

PRO 7:7, PRO 22:14; ; ECC 7:26

It is resisted by the wise

PRO 2:16 See also GEN 39:7–39:12; PRO 7:4–7:5

Adultery breaks the marriage covenant

PRO 2:17 See also HOS 2:2; MAL 2:14

The consequences of adultery

It destroys individuals and society

PRO 6:32–6:34 See also PRO 5:4–5:5, PRO 5:9–5:11, PRO 6:26–6:29, PRO 7:22–7:27; JOB 31:11–31:12

It was punishable by death under OT law

LEV 20:10 See also DEU 22:22, DEU 22:23–22:24; JHN 8:4–8:5

Adulterers face God's judgment

HEB 13:4 See also 2SA 12:11–12:12; MAL 3:5; 1CO 6:9; REV 2:22

Remarriage after divorce may be adultery

MAT 19:9 parallel MRK 10:11 See also MAT 5:32; LUK 16:18; ROM 7:3

Hypocrisy in relation to adultery

ROM 2:22 See also 2SA 12:5–12:6; HOS 4:14; JHN 8:7–8:9

Examples of adultery

2SA 11:1–11:4 See also JER 5:7 the people of Jerusalem; JER 29:23 the exiles in Babylon; HOS 3:1–3:3 Hosea's wife, Gomer; JHN 8:3 the woman caught in adultery; 2PE 2:14 false teachers in the church

Children born of adultery

ISA 57:3 “offspring of adulterers” was a term of abuse. See also 2SA 12:15–12:18 David and Bathsheba's child died when he was seven days old; HOS 2:4–2:5, HOS 5:7

title: 6243: adultery, spiritual
content:
In Scripture there is a close link between sexual sin and spiritual corruption and immorality as promoted by false teachers. Scripture frequently uses the language of sexual sin to expose the infidelity of God's people.

The link between sexual sin and spiritual adultery

In practice

NUM 25:1–25:3 See also EXO 34:15; HOS 4:14

In false teaching

REV 2:20–2:25 See also NUM 31:15–31:16; 2TI 3:6; 2PE 2:13–2:15; JUD 1:4, JUD 1:8; REV 2:14–2:16

Spiritual adultery and God's covenant relationship with his people

God as a husband to his people

EZK 16:8 The spiritual relationship between God and his people is defined in terms of the intimate and exclusive bond of marriage. See also ISA 54:5; JER 3:14, JER 31:32; EPH 5:31–5:32

God is jealous for his people's affections

EXO 34:14 Just as husband and wife are jealous for their relationship in love, so God is jealous for the affections of his people. See also EXO 20:4–20:5; DEU 4:23–4:24, DEU 6:13–6:15

The history of spiritual adultery among God's people

Before Israel's kings

DEU 31:16 See also LEV 17:7–17:9, LEV 20:4–20:6; JDG 2:16–2:17, JDG 8:27, JDG 8:33–8:34

In the northern kingdom of Israel

HOS 1:2 See also 1CH 5:25; EZK 23:1–23:8; HOS 2:2–2:3, HOS 4:10–4:12, HOS 5:3–5:4

In the southern kingdom of Judah

JER 3:1–3:3 See also 2CH 21:10–21:11; ISA 57:3–57:8; JER 2:20, JER 5:7–5:8, JER 23:13–23:14; EZK 16:15–16:22, EZK 23:11–23:21

In Jesus Christ's generation

MAT 12:38–12:39 See also MAT 16:4

God's judgment on spiritual adultery

JER 19:3–19:5 See also JER 3:6–3:10, JER 16:10–16:13; EZK 23:36–23:49

Believers are not to be drawn into spiritual adultery

1JN 2:15–2:17 See also 1CO 10:6–10:11; COL 3:5; HEB 3:12–3:14, HEB 10:26–10:31; JAS 4:4–4:5; 1JN 5:21

resource.label: 5712: marriage, between God and his people

title: 6245: stubbornness
content:
Proud and disobedient resistance to God's will and rejection of his commands.

Stubbornness is an attitude of heart

JER 7:24 See also DEU 9:27; JDG 2:19; PSA 78:8; ISA 48:4; JER 3:17, JER 5:3, JER 9:14, JER 16:12, JER 18:12; HOS 4:16; ZEC 7:11

The practical consequences of stubbornness

EPH 4:18 See also EXO 13:15, EXO 33:3; LEV 26:19; 2CH 26:16; NEH 9:16–9:17; JOB 20:6–20:7; PSA 10:4; PRO 1:24–1:31, PRO 28:14, PRO 29:1 sudden destruction for the unrepentant; ISA 2:11 a humbling on judgment day; JER 5:3, JER 11:8, JER 13:10; DAN 5:20 King Nebuchadnezzar deposed; HOS 13:6 The people of Israel forget their God; ZEP 3:11; MRK 6:51–6:52 Jesus Christ's disciples amazed; MAT 18:15–18:17; ROM 2:5 The self-righteous store up wrath for the day of judgment.

Commands not to be stubborn

NEH 9:29 See also EXO 33:5; 2CH 30:8; PSA 95:8; MRK 16:14

Stubbornness can be God's judgment on sin

ISA 63:17 When people persistently refuse to obey God, he brings about a condition where they cannot obey him, and consequently cannot receive his blessing. See also EXO 4:21, EXO 14:17; DEU 2:30; JOS 11:20; PSA 81:11–81:12; ROM 9:18, ROM 11:7–11:8

God's appeal to stubborn people

ISA 65:2 See also ROM 10:21; 2CH 32:24–32:25; JOB 33:16–33:18; ISA 46:12; JER 7:22–7:24; EZK 2:4–2:5; ZEC 7:11–7:12; ACT 19:8–19:9

title: 6248: temptation
content: Pressure to yield to influences that can lead people away from God and into sin.

title: 6249: temptation, universality of
content:
Scripture makes clear that all human beings are subject to temptation. It provides examples of individuals who have faced and yielded to such influences.

The inevitability of temptation to do wrong

1CO 10:13 See also PRO 7:21–7:23; MAT 13:20–13:21 parallels MRK 4:16–4:17, LUK 8:13; LUK 17:1; ROM 7:15; 1CO 10:12; 2CO 11:3; 1PE 1:6

Examples of those who were tempted

Adam and Eve tempted

GEN 3:6–3:7 Sin enters the world through temptation, which leads to the fall of humanity.

Esau tempted

See also GEN 25:29–25:34

Achan tempted

JOS 7:21

Samson tempted

JDG 14:16–14:17

David tempted

2SA 11:2–11:4

Solomon tempted

1KI 11:1, 1KI 11:4

Gehazi tempted

2KI 5:20–5:23

Peter tempted

MAT 26:69–26:75 parallels LUK 22:55–22:62, JHN 18:16–18:18, JHN 18:25–18:27

Help for those facing temptation

ROM 8:31 See also JOS 1:5; ROM 8:37–8:39; HEB 4:15–4:16, HEB 13:5; 1JN 2:1, 1JN 4:4; REV 3:10

title: 6250: temptation, sources of
content:
Temptation comes from a number of sources but not from God.

Temptation arising from sinful human nature

EPH 2:1–2:3; JAS 1:14 See also MAT 5:29–5:30 parallel MRK 9:43–9:47; MAT 6:23; ROM 7:18–7:23

Temptation from other people

2PE 2:18 See also GEN 3:6; PRO 5:3–5:6

Temptation from Satan

GEN 3:1 See also 1CH 21:1; MAT 4:1 parallels MRK 1:13, LUK 4:2; MRK 4:15 parallel LUK 8:12; 1CO 7:5; 1TH 3:5

Temptation from the world

1JN 2:16 See also MAT 13:22 parallels MRK 4:19, LUK 8:14

Attractions which lead to temptation

Money

1TI 6:9–6:10

Power

See also DEU 8:17–8:18; 2CH 26:16

Lust

PRO 6:25, PRO 7:21

Pride

PRO 11:2

Temptation does not come from God

JAS 1:13 See also MAT 6:13 parallel LUK 11:4

title: 6251: temptation, resisting
content:
Being tempted is not in itself a sin. Sin arises when believers give in to temptation. Scripture urges believers to resist temptation, and gives them encouragement to face it.

Encouragement to those facing temptation

JAS 1:12 See also ROM 8:37; HEB 2:18, HEB 4:15–4:16; JAS 1:2–1:3; 1JN 4:4

Finding in God and his word resources to overcome temptation

DAN 11:32 See also PRO 2:1–2:2, PRO 2:12–2:15; MAT 6:13 parallel LUK 11:4; 1TI 6:11–6:12; JAS 4:7

Practical suggestions for overcoming temptation

Overcoming temptation through prayer

MAT 26:41 parallels MRK 14:38, LUK 22:40 See also MAT 18:8–18:9; 1CO 7:5

Overcoming temptation through personal discipline

HEB 12:4, HEB 12:7; ; 2PE 3:17

Encouragements to resist temptation

GAL 6:1; 1TH 5:22 See also PRO 1:10–1:15; ROM 6:12–6:14

Examples of those who did not give in to temptation

JOB 1:22 See also GEN 39:7–39:10; JOB 2:10; JER 35:5–35:6; DAN 1:8

Jesus Christ resisted temptation

MAT 4:4 parallel LUK 4:4 See also MAT 4:7 parallel LUK 4:12; MAT 4:10 parallel LUK 4:8

title: 6252: temptation, and Jesus Christ
content:
Jesus Christ, like every other human being, knew temptation. However, he never yielded to it. The sinlessness of Christ refers to his obedient refusal to give in to temptation. It does not mean that he did not experience temptation himself.

The temptation of Jesus Christ

MRK 1:13 parallels MAT 4:1–4:10, LUK 4:1–4:13

The temptation to avoid the cross

MAT 16:21–16:23 parallel MRK 8:31–8:33 See also MAT 26:36–26:44 parallels MRK 14:32–14:42, LUK 22:40–22:46; MAT 27:39–27:44 parallels MRK 15:31–15:32, LUK 23:36–23:37, LUK 23:39

The temptation to please the crowd

LUK 11:16 See also MAT 12:38–12:39 parallel LUK 11:29; JHN 2:18, JHN 6:30

The significance of the temptation of Jesus Christ

Believers can identify with Jesus Christ in his temptation

HEB 2:18

Believers can have confidence in the face of temptation

HEB 4:15–4:16

title: 6253: temptation, avoiding causing
content:
Any behaviour which might cause others to do wrong is to be avoided.

Warnings not to cause temptation

LUK 17:1

Special care is needed to avoid tempting those whose faith is young

MAT 18:6 See also MRK 9:42; LUK 17:2

Temptation is linked to passing judgment on others

ROM 14:13

Allowance must be made to avoid causing temptation to those whose faith is weak

ROM 14:15, ROM 14:21

Avoiding causing temptation by exercising Christian freedom responsibly

1CO 8:9–8:13

Examples of causing temptation

NUM 25:1–25:2; PRO 16:29 See also HAB 2:15; MAT 18:6

Examples of restraining sin or temptation

GEN 37:21–37:22; ; ACT 20:28–20:31; ; EPH 6:10–6:13; ; HEB 12:1–12:3; ; 1PE 5:8–5:9; ; 1JN 3:7

title: 6257: unbelievers
content:
Those who are unwilling to believe and trust in God, especially as he has made himself known in and through Jesus Christ.

Characteristics of unbelievers

A refusal to believe in God

NUM 14:11; ; PSA 78:32; ; JHN 5:38; ; ACT 14:2

A refusal to trust in God

PSA 78:22; ; HEB 4:2

A persistence in sin

PSA 78:32

Descriptions of unbelievers

They are blind

2CO 4:4 See also JHN 9:39–9:41

They are disobedient

HEB 3:12, HEB 3:18–3:19

Unbelievers can become believers

1TI 1:13 See also MRK 9:24

The effect of tongues and prophecy on unbelievers

1CO 14:22–14:25

Relationships between believers and unbelievers

Believers should not marry unbelievers

2CO 6:14–6:15

An unbelieving husband or wife

1CO 7:12–7:16 See also 1PE 3:1

Eating with unbelievers

1CO 10:27–10:29

The consequence of remaining an unbeliever

JHN 3:18 See also LUK 12:46; JHN 8:24, JHN 12:48; REV 21:8

Examples of unbelievers

Some Israelites: NUM 14:13–14:39; ; DEU 1:34–1:35; ; 1CO 10:10; MAT 17:20 the disciples; ; MRK 8:11–8:12 some Pharisees; ACT 14:2, ACT 17:5

title: 6260: uncircumcised
content:
Those who are outside the covenant of God.

Circumcision marked the covenant people of God

God's covenant with Abraham

GEN 17:10–17:12, GEN 17:14 See also JOS 5:6–5:7

Uncircumcised aliens participating in Jewish religious practices had first to be circumcised

EXO 12:48; ; EZK 44:6–44:7, EZK 44:9; ; ACT 11:3, ACT 21:21

The term ``uncircumcised''

Used to describe Gentiles

JDG 14:3, JDG 15:18; ; 2SA 1:20; ; EZK 28:10, EZK 31:18, EZK 32:19, EZK 32:21, EZK 32:24–32:32

As a term of derision

ISA 52:1 See also GEN 34:14; 1SA 14:6, 1SA 17:26, 1SA 17:36, 1SA 31:4 parallel 1CH 10:4

Uncircumcision represents a wrong inner spiritual attitude

JER 9:25–9:26 The tribes mentioned also practised male circumcision. See also ACT 7:51; ROM 2:25–2:27, ROM 3:29–3:30; 1CO 7:18–7:19; GAL 5:6, GAL 6:15; COL 2:13

In Christ the barrier between the circumcised and uncircumcised is overcome

EPH 2:11–2:13 See also ROM 4:9–4:12; 1CO 12:13; COL 3:11

title: 6500: Salvation
content: • 6510: salvation

title: 6510: salvation
content: The transformation of a person's individual nature and relationship with God as a result of repentance and faith in the atoning death of Jesus Christ on the cross. All humanity stands in need of salvation, which is only possible through faith in Jesus Christ.

title: 6511: salvation, nature of
content:
Salvation involves a change in the relationship between God and a person. Salvation includes God's adoption of believers into his family, his acceptance of them as righteous and his forgiveness of their sins. It also includes personal renewal and transformation through the work of the Holy Spirit.

Salvation as a change in status before God

Access to God

ROM 5:1–5:2 See also EPH 2:13; HEB 4:16

Adoption into the family of God

JHN 1:12; ; ROM 8:22–8:24; ; GAL 4:4–4:7

Forgiveness of sin

ACT 5:30–5:31 See also PSA 32:1–32:2; MAT 26:28; ACT 10:43, ACT 13:38; EPH 1:7; COL 2:13

Heavenly citizenship

PHP 3:20–3:21 See also EPH 2:19; COL 3:1–3:2; HEB 12:22–12:24

Inheritance from God

ROM 8:17 See also COL 1:12; REV 21:7

Peace with God

EPH 2:13–2:17 See also ISA 53:5; JHN 16:33; ROM 5:1–5:2; COL 3:15

Righteousness in the sight of God

ROM 1:17 See also ISA 61:10; ROM 3:22, ROM 4:3–4:13, ROM 4:25–5:1 The idea of being righteous in the sight of God lies at the heart of Paul's doctrine of justification by faith; 1CO 1:30; 2CO 5:21; PHP 3:8–3:9; 2TI 4:8; HEB 11:7

Salvation as a change in a person's nature

Becoming a new creation

2CO 5:17 See also ROM 6:4; GAL 6:14–6:15; EPH 2:15

Deliverance from God's righteous condemnation

ROM 8:1–8:2 See also ISA 50:8; ROM 5:15–5:17, ROM 8:33–8:39; COL 1:22

Deliverance from the power of sin and evil

GAL 1:3–1:4 See also ROM 6:14, ROM 7:21–7:25, ROM 8:2–8:4; 1PE 2:24; REV 1:5

Inner personal renewal

1JN 1:7 See also PSA 51:1–51:2, PSA 51:7; HEB 1:3, HEB 10:19–10:22

New birth

JHN 3:3–3:7 See also JAS 1:18; 1PE 1:23; 1JN 3:9

The presence of the Holy Spirit

ROM 8:10–8:11 See also GAL 5:2–5:25

title: 6512: salvation, necessity and basis of
content:
Scripture stresses that fallen human beings are cut off from God on account of their sin. All need to be saved, if they are to enter into a new relationship with God as their Creator and Redeemer. Salvation is not the result of human achievement, privilege or wisdom, but depends totally upon the graciousness of a loving God, supremely expressed in the cross of Jesus Christ. People must respond in repentance and faith if they are to benefit from God's offer of salvation in Christ.

The necessity of salvation

The universal rule of sin in human nature

ISA 64:6; ; ROM 3:19–3:23, ROM 5:12–5:18, ROM 7:24; ; EPH 2:3

Sin cuts humanity off from God

ISA 59:1–59:2 See also GEN 3:22–3:24; EPH 2:1–2:5, EPH 4:18

Sin enslaves humanity to evil

JER 13:23; ; HOS 5:4; ; ZEC 7:11–7:12; ; ROM 7:14–7:20; ; 2PE 2:13–2:19

Salvation is grounded in the love of God

Salvation is not based on human achievement

ROM 3:28; EPH 2:8–2:9 See also ACT 15:7–15:11; ROM 4:1–4:3, ROM 5:1–5:2; GAL 2:16, GAL 2:21; 2TI 1:9

Salvation is grounded in God's love for his people

EPH 2:4–2:5 See also DEU 7:1–7:8; JHN 3:16–3:17; ROM 5:8; 2TH 2:16; 1JN 4:9–4:19

Salvation is grounded in God's grace

ROM 3:22–3:24 See also JHN 1:16; ACT 15:11; ROM 5:15–5:17; 2CO 6:1–6:2; EPH 1:5–1:8, EPH 2:4–2:10; 1TI 1:14–1:15; TIT 2:11, TIT 3:4–3:7; HEB 2:9

Salvation and the work of Jesus Christ

Salvation is grounded in the work of Jesus Christ

ACT 5:30–5:31; 1TI 1:15 See also JHN 4:42; ACT 4:10–4:12; ROM 5:9–5:10; PHP 3:20–3:21; 2TI 1:9–1:10; TIT 3:5–3:7; HEB 7:24–7:25; 1JN 4:14

Jesus Christ's death was totally sufficient for salvation

1PE 3:18 See also JHN 17:1–17:4; ACT 4:10–4:12; GAL 1:3–1:4; EPH 1:5–1:10; 1TI 2:5–2:6; 2TI 1:9–1:10; HEB 10:10; 1JN 4:9–4:10; REV 7:9–7:10

Salvation demands a human decision

JHN 3:36; ACT 3:19 See also MRK 1:15; LUK 8:50; JHN 3:17–3:18; ACT 2:37–2:39; HEB 12:25; 1PE 2:4–2:8; 1JN 5:10

content: See 1320: God, as Saviour

content: See 2324: Jesus Christ, as Saviour

title: 6602: acceptance
content: The favourable reception of someone or something. God in his grace accepts human beings, their worship and their offerings. People are called to accept Jesus Christ and the message of the gospel and to respond to being accepted by accepting one another.

title: 6603: acceptance, divine
content:
God's gracious favour shown both to Israel and to the Gentiles. God accepts people who respond to him, and worship that is offered sincerely and is accompanied by wholehearted commitment.

God's acceptance of human beings

God's gracious acceptance of Israel

2KI 13:23 See also EZK 20:40–20:41; ROM 11:1

God's acceptance of those who respond to him

GEN 4:7; EZK 43:27; JHN 6:37 See also EXO 28:38; ROM 14:3; HEB 12:6; 2PE 1:10–1:11

God's acceptance of the Gentiles

ROM 15:16 See also ISA 56:6–56:7; ACT 10:34–10:35, ACT 15:7–15:8

Prayers for acceptance by God

DEU 21:8; ; 2SA 24:23

God's acceptance of human worship

Acceptable sacrifices

LEV 22:19–22:21; 1PE 2:5 See also LEV 1:3–1:4, LEV 19:5–19:7, LEV 22:27–22:29; MAL 3:3–3:4; JHN 4:23; ROM 12:1; PHP 4:18; HEB 12:28, HEB 13:16

Acceptance of gifts offered for the tabernacle

NUM 7:5 See also NUM 31:51–31:54

Unacceptable offerings

JER 14:10–14:12 See also LEV 7:18, LEV 22:25; MAL 1:8–1:10, MAL 2:13

Acceptable service

PRO 21:3; JAS 1:27 See also 1SA 15:22; PSA 51:6–51:7; JER 6:19–6:20; AMO 5:21–5:24; 2CO 6:17–6:18; ISA 52:11

God accepts prayer and praise

PSA 6:9; HEB 13:15 See also JOB 33:26, JOB 42:8–42:9; PSA 119:108; LUK 18:13–18:14

title: 6604: acceptance, human
content:
The response of human beings to the revelation and grace of God. It is seen in receiving Jesus Christ, his words and the message of the gospel, and in the acceptance of one another for his sake. Believers are called, too, to accept instruction and discipline and to submit to God's will for their lives.

Human beings are to accept Jesus Christ

Receiving Jesus Christ

JHN 1:12 See also COL 2:6

Accepting Jesus Christ by accepting others

JHN 13:20 See also MAT 10:40, MAT 18:5 parallels MRK 9:37, LUK 9:48; LUK 10:16; JHN 12:44

Accepting Jesus Christ's words

JHN 17:8 See also MAT 11:14, MAT 19:11–19:12; JHN 3:33–3:34

Refusing to accept Jesus Christ and his words

JHN 1:11, JHN 3:11, JHN 3:32, JHN 5:43–5:44, JHN 6:60

Acceptance of the gospel

The gospel is worthy of acceptance

1TI 1:15; JAS 1:21 See also MAT 13:23 parallels MRK 4:20, LUK 8:15; 1CO 15:3; 1TI 4:9–4:10

Examples of those accepting the gospel

ACT 2:41 3,000 on the day of Pentecost; ; ACT 8:14 the Samaritans; ; ACT 11:1 the Gentiles; 1CO 15:1; ; 2CO 11:4; GAL 1:9 believers in Galatia; 1TH 1:6; ; 2TH 2:13

Rejection of the gospel

ACT 22:18; ; ROM 11:15; ; 1CO 2:14

Acceptance of one another

A right self-acceptance

1CO 15:10 Paul accepts himself as a sinner saved by God's grace and entrusted with an important ministry; GAL 5:14 Loving others requires a proper self-love: the recognition that people are made in God's image and are the objects of his love. See also LEV 19:18; MAT 22:39 parallel MRK 12:31; MRK 12:33; ROM 13:9; 1TI 1:12–1:14; JAS 2:8

Acceptance of one another because of God's example

ROM 15:7 See also JHN 13:34, JHN 15:12

Acceptance of those whom God has accepted

ROM 14:1 See also DEU 10:18–10:19 God's people should reflect his concern for the weak and defenceless; MAT 19:13–19:14 parallels MRK 10:13–10:14, LUK 18:15–18:16 Jesus Christ rebukes his disciples for turning away children.ACT 9:11–9:17, ACT 22:12–22:13 ACT 9:27 Barnabas' acceptance of SaulACT 10:15, ACT 10:28, ACT 10:47

Acceptance of outsiders who join God's people

LEV 19:33–19:34 See also EZK 47:22–47:23

Acceptance of itinerant ministers

GAL 4:14 See also 1CO 16:10–16:11; PHP 2:29–2:30

Acceptance of things given

Acceptance of gifts

1SA 25:35 See also GEN 33:10–33:11; 1SA 10:4; 1TI 4:3–4:4

Refusal to accept gifts

GEN 14:23–14:24 Abraham refuses gifts offered by the king of Sodom; ; 2KI 5:15–5:16 Elisha refuses a gift offered by Naaman; ; EST 4:4 Mordecai refuses fine clothes from Esther.

Acceptance of instruction

JOB 22:22; ; PRO 4:10, PRO 10:8, PRO 19:20; ; ISA 29:24

Acceptance of discipline

JOB 5:17 See also DEU 21:18; PSA 94:12; PRO 3:11–3:12, PRO 10:17; HEB 12:5–12:7; REV 3:19

Acceptance of God's will

MAT 26:39 parallels MRK 14:36, LUK 22:42 See also JOB 2:10; PSA 40:8; ISA 50:5; ROM 8:28

Acceptance of bribes forbidden

EXO 23:8; DEU 10:17; ; 2CH 19:7; PSA 15:5; ; PRO 17:23; ; ISA 33:15

title: 6606: access to God
content:
The privilege of entering into the presence of God through the work of Jesus Christ.

Only the pure have access to God

PSA 24:3–24:4; 1JN 3:21–3:22 See also PSA 41:12, PSA 66:18–66:19, PSA 73:28; MAT 5:8; 1PE 3:12; PSA 34:15–34:16

The wicked do not have access to God

PSA 101:7; ISA 59:2 See also GEN 4:14, GEN 4:16; LEV 22:3; DEU 31:18; PSA 5:5; ISA 1:15, ISA 64:7; JER 7:15, JER 15:1, JER 23:39, JER 52:3; EZK 39:23; HOS 5:6; MIC 3:4

Access to God through the priests and sacrificial system

Access to God by this means in the OT

EXO 29:44–29:45 See also EXO 29:29–29:30, EXO 29:36, EXO 29:38–29:39

This OT access to God fulfilled in Jesus Christ

HEB 4:14–4:16, HEB 7:23–7:25 See also HEB 2:17, HEB 7:18–7:19, HEB 10:10–10:12, HEB 12:18–12:24

Access to God by the priesthood of all believers

1PE 2:5 See also 1PE 2:9; REV 1:6

Access to God through the Tent of Meeting and the tabernacle

Access to God by this means in the OT

EXO 33:7–33:11 See also EXO 40:1–40:2, EXO 40:34–40:35; NUM 1:51, NUM 3:10, NUM 3:38, NUM 18:7, NUM 18:22; 2CH 1:3

This OT access to God fulfilled in Jesus Christ

HEB 9:23–9:24 See also ACT 7:44; HEB 8:1–8:2, HEB 8:5–8:6, HEB 9:11

Access to God through the Most Holy Place

Access to God by this means in the OT

HEB 9:7–9:8 See also LEV 16:2, LEV 16:12–16:17, LEV 16:32–16:33; 1KI 6:16, 1KI 8:6 parallel 2CH 5:7; 1KI 8:10–8:11 parallel 2CH 5:13–5:14; 2CH 3:8

Access to God by this means fulfilled in Jesus Christ

HEB 9:3–9:5HEB 9:12, HEB 10:19–10:22 See also MAT 27:51 parallel MRK 15:38; HEB 6:19–6:20

Believers have access to God by grace

PSA 21:6 See also PSA 51:10–51:11, PSA 145:18; HOS 6:1–6:2; ACT 17:27; 2CO 4:14; JAS 4:8; JUD 1:24

Believers have access to God through Jesus Christ

ROM 5:1–5:2; EPH 2:18, EPH 3:12 See also JHN 10:9, JHN 14:6

title: 6608: adoption
content: The deliberate action by which a family gives to a person all the privileges of being a member of that family.

title: 6609: adoption, nature of
content:
The giving by God of the status and privileges of being his children. God adopts those who believe in him and grants them the benefits of his salvation.

A family adopts a child

GEN 15:3 See also GEN 48:5; EXO 2:10; EST 2:7

God adopts the nation of Israel

DEU 14:2 See also DEU 7:7; ISA 63:16; AMO 3:1–3:2; ROM 9:4

God is Israel's father

JER 31:9 See also MAL 1:6

Israel is God's son

HOS 11:1 See also DEU 14:1

God adopts believers as his children

JHN 1:12–1:13

God is the Father of believers

2CO 6:18 See also MAT 6:9 parallel LUK 11:2

Believers are adopted as an outcome of predestination

EPH 1:5

Believers are adopted as an outcome of redemption

GAL 4:5

Believers are adopted as an outcome of justification

GAL 3:24–3:26 See also JHN 1:12–1:13

Believers are adopted by grace

EPH 1:3–1:6 See also EZK 16:3–16:6; ROM 4:16; EPH 1:11

The final adoption of believers will occur at the resurrection

ROM 8:23 See also EPH 1:13–1:14; 1JN 3:2

title: 6610: adoption, descriptions of
content:
The words used by the NT to describe believers point to their status and privileges as the adopted children of God.

Believers are no longer slaves but sons

GAL 4:7–4:9 See also JHN 8:34–8:36; ROM 8:15; PHM 1:16

Believers are children of God

1JN 3:1–3:2 The title “child of God” is thought by some to be distinct from that of “son of God” (see below). “child” is thought to emphasise God's fatherhood, whilst “son” is thought to emphasise the Christian's privileges. These terms are however, sometimes used interchangeably. See also LUK 20:36; ROM 8:21; PHP 2:15; 1JN 3:2, 1JN 5:2

Believers are sons of God

ROM 8:14 See also ROM 9:26; GAL 3:26

Believers are children of the resurrection

LUK 20:36

Believers are children of light, sons of light

JHN 12:36; ; EPH 5:8; ; 1TH 5:5

Believers are heirs of God

ROM 8:17 See also GAL 3:29, GAL 4:7; TIT 3:7; HEB 6:17

Believers are brothers of Jesus Christ

HEB 2:11–2:12 See also MAT 12:48–12:50; ROM 8:29; HEB 2:17

Believers are brothers of other believers

1JN 3:14 See also 1JN 4:19–4:20; REV 12:10, REV 19:10

Believers are members of God's household, his family

EPH 2:19 See also GAL 6:10; HEB 3:2–3:6

title: 6611: adoption, privileges and duties of
content:
As adopted members of the family of God, believers receive both the privileges and responsibilities of being children of God.

The privileges received by believers through adoption

Believers are given the Spirit of adoption

GAL 4:6 See also ROM 8:15

Believers have access to their heavenly Father

EPH 2:18 See also EPH 3:12; HEB 4:16

Believers become heirs with Christ of heaven

ROM 8:17 See also GAL 3:29, GAL 4:7; COL 1:12; 1PE 1:4

The benefits God gives to those he adopts

Believers are pitied by him

PSA 103:13

Believers are protected

PRO 14:26

Believers are provided for

MAT 6:31–6:33

Believers receive loving discipline

HEB 12:6

Believers are never forsaken

PSA 94:14

Believers are assured by the Spirit

ROM 8:16

The responsibilities of God's adopted children

Believers are to walk in the light

See also JHN 12:35–12:36; EPH 5:8; 1TH 5:4–5:5

Believers are to shun evil

2CO 6:17–6:18; ; PHP 2:15

Believers are to purify themselves

2CO 7:1; ; 1JN 3:2–3:3

Believers are to live obediently

MAT 12:50; ; 1PE 1:14; ; 1JN 5:2–5:3

Believers are to live in peace

MAT 5:9; ; ROM 14:19

Believers are to live in love

GAL 5:13; ; 1PE 4:8; ; 1JN 3:18

Believers are to be watchful

1TH 5:5–5:6

title: 6614: atonement
content: Reconciliation; sin has alienated humanity from God and provoked God's anger. God has responded by providing the means of restoring this broken relationship, bringing both sides to a place where they are at one again (“at-one-ment”).

title: 6615: atonement, necessity and nature of
content:
Scripture stresses the seriousness and reality of human sin, and that human beings are unable to atone for their own sins. In his grace, God provides a means by which the situation can be remedied.

Atonement is necessary because of human sinfulness

Atonement is necessary because sin cuts people off from God

ISA 59:2 See also ISA 64:7; EZK 39:23; HAB 1:13; JHN 9:31

Atonement is necessary because sin provokes God's wrath

EPH 2:1–2:3 See also GEN 6:5–6:7; EXO 32:30–32:35; ROM 1:18–1:20, ROM 2:8

God's gracious nature is the basis for atonement

Atonement is grounded in God's reluctance to punish sinners

EZK 18:32 See also EZK 33:11; 1TI 2:1–2:4; 2PE 3:9

Atonement is grounded in God's readiness to forgive sin

EXO 34:6–34:7 The punishment of subsequent generations makes God's abhorrence of sin quite clear, but the extension of his love and forgiveness “to thousands” puts the emphasis in these verses on God's grace and compassion. See also PSA 145:8; DAN 9:9; JON 4:2

Atonement is grounded in God's covenant love

NUM 14:19 The Hebrew word for “great love” means God's loving faithfulness to those within the covenant. See also PSA 25:6–25:7, PSA 103:8–103:12; JOL 2:13

God's provision of atonement is a means of dealing with sin

Atonement through sacrifice

LEV 9:7 See also EXO 30:10; NUM 15:22–15:26

God's promised new covenant of forgiveness was fulfilled in Jesus Christ's atoning death

HEB 10:16–10:17 See also JER 31:33–31:34; MAT 26:28; HEB 9:15, HEB 12:24

Images used to portray the at-one-ment, or restored relationship, between God and humanity

Atonement as forgiveness of sins

EPH 1:7–1:8 See also LEV 19:22; ACT 13:38; COL 2:13–2:14

Atonement as cleansing and purification

LEV 16:30 See also ISA 6:6–6:7; TIT 2:14; 1JN 1:7; REV 7:14

Atonement as reconciliation

2CO 5:19 See also ROM 5:9–5:11; EPH 2:14–2:16

Atonement as healing

1PE 2:24 See also 2CH 7:14; PSA 103:2–103:3; ISA 53:5

Atonement as God buying people back for himself

REV 5:9 See also MAT 20:28 parallel MRK 10:45; ACT 20:28; 1PE 1:18–1:19

Atonement as making holy: creating a relationship of consecrated nearness to God

HEB 10:10 See also COL 1:22; HEB 13:12

resource.label: 6717: reconciliation, of the world to God

title: 6616: atonement, in OT
content:
The OT laid down complex regulations by which the guilt of sin could be removed through the sacrificial system. Particular emphasis was placed upon the role of the high priest, who was required to make annual atonement for the sins of the people.

The covenantal framework of atonement

As God's covenant partners, the Israelites undertook to keep his laws

EXO 24:3 See also DEU 26:17; JOS 24:24

The sin offering made atonement for unintentional sins under the covenant

LEV 9:7 See also LEV 4:13–4:14; NUM 15:22–15:26

The guilt offering atoned for sins where reparation was required

LEV 19:20–19:22 See also LEV 6:1–6:7

Deliberate flouting of God's law could not be atoned for

NUM 15:30–15:31 See also NUM 35:33; 1SA 3:14

The Day of Atonement provided for the removal of the nation's sin

LEV 16:34 See also EXO 30:10; LEV 16:1–16:33 The nation's sin was atoned for by sprinkling the atonement cover in the Most Holy Place with the blood of the sacrificial goat; the removal of the people's sin was symbolised by the driving of the scapegoat into the wilderness; HEB 9:7

The atonement cover

EXO 25:17–25:22 The locating of God's presence above the atonement cover in the tabernacle demonstrated that it was only on the basis of atonement that God could accompany his people. See also EXO 30:6; LEV 16:2; NUM 7:89

Atonement was effected by the blood of the sacrifice

LEV 17:11 The blood signified that the life of the animal had been given in place of that of the worshipper. See also HEB 9:22

The role of priests in making atonement

Priests were dedicated to God in order to make atonement for others

EXO 29:44 See also LEV 8:22–8:30

The priests had to make atonement for their own sins

HEB 5:1–5:3 See also LEV 9:8–9:11

The priests represented the people before God to atone for their sin

HEB 5:1 See also EXO 28:36–28:38; LEV 10:16–10:17

The people had constant reminders of the need for atonement

Rituals of cleansing included an atoning offering

LEV 12:7–12:8, LEV 14:18–14:22, LEV 14:53, LEV 15:15

Atonement was a feature of Israel's festivals

NUM 28:22, NUM 28:30, NUM 29:5, NUM 29:11

Abuses of the system of atonement

The sinful conduct of the priests who made atonement

HOS 4:7–4:8 See also 1SA 2:12–2:17; JER 6:13–6:14; EZK 22:26; MAL 1:6–1:8

The sinful conduct of the people who sought atonement without repenting of their sins

HOS 8:11–8:13 See also ISA 1:10–1:17, ISA 66:3; JER 7:21–7:24; AMO 4:4

The need for repentance for a relationship of atonement

PRO 16:6 See also 1SA 15:22; PSA 51:16–51:17; MIC 6:6–6:8

The prophets foretold a renewing of God's relationship with Israel, involving atonement for sin

Isaiah's message about the obedient servant

ISA 53:4–53:12

Jeremiah's prophecy of a new covenant

JER 31:31–31:34

Ezekiel's vision of a new temple

EZK 43:18–43:27

title: 6617: atonement, in NT
content:
In dying for the sins of the world, Jesus Christ fulfilled and replaced the OT sacrificial system, so that all who believe in him are restored to fellowship with God. Christ is the true high priest, who finally liberates his people from the guilt of sin, by offering himself as the supreme sacrifice.

The atoning purpose of Jesus Christ's death

Jesus Christ's death on behalf of others

JHN 10:11 See also JHN 10:14–10:18; 2CO 5:15; HEB 2:9; 1JN 3:16

Jesus Christ's atoning death for sin

1CO 15:3 See also ROM 4:25, ROM 8:3; GAL 1:4; 1PE 3:18

The atoning significance of Jesus Christ's death is expressed by references to his blood

ROM 5:9; REV 5:9 See also EPH 2:13; 1PE 1:18–1:19; 1JN 1:7; REV 7:14

Jesus Christ's atoning death is commemorated in the Lord's Supper

1CO 11:23–11:25 See also MAT 26:26–26:28 parallels MRK 14:22–14:24, LUK 22:19–22:20

Explanations of the atonement

Jesus Christ's death as an atoning sacrifice

ROM 3:25 See also 1CO 5:7; EPH 5:2; 1JN 4:10; REV 5:6

Jesus Christ's atoning death as redemption

MRK 10:45 parallel MAT 20:28 See also ACT 20:28; GAL 3:13–3:14; EPH 1:7; COL 1:13–1:14

The atonement is effective because of Jesus Christ's sinlessness

2CO 5:21 See also HEB 4:15; 1PE 2:22–2:24; 1JN 3:5

Jesus Christ's death fulfils and replaces the Day of Atonement

Jesus Christ makes atonement as the new high priest

HEB 7:26–7:28

Jesus Christ is the mediator of the new and better covenant

HEB 8:6–8:7, HEB 9:15

Jesus Christ has made atonement in the true heavenly sanctuary

HEB 8:1–8:2, HEB 9:24

Jesus Christ's atoning blood brings effective cleansing

HEB 9:12–9:14

Jesus Christ's single sacrifice replaces the many required under the old covenant

HEB 10:11–10:14

Access to the heavenly sanctuary is now open

HEB 10:19–10:20

By dying with Christ, believers are released from this age into the life of the age to come

ROM 6:1–6:7 See also ROM 7:4–7:6; GAL 2:19–2:20, GAL 6:14; EPH 2:6–2:7; COL 2:11–2:13

God the Father and the atoning death of his Son

God's sending of his Son to make atonement

1JN 4:14 See also JHN 3:16; ROM 8:32; 2CO 5:18; GAL 4:4–4:5

God's grace displayed in making atonement for the ungodly

EPH 2:4–2:5 See also ROM 5:6–5:8; EPH 2:8–2:9; TIT 3:4–3:5

The worldwide scope of Jesus Christ's atoning death

1JN 2:2 See also JHN 1:29; 2CO 5:19; 1TI 2:5

The appropriate response to the atonement

The response of repentance

ACT 3:19 See also ACT 2:38, ACT 17:30, ACT 20:21

The response of faith

ACT 10:43 See also JHN 3:14–3:15; ACT 16:31; ROM 3:22; GAL 2:16

The response of baptism

ACT 22:16 See also ACT 2:38; 1PE 3:21

title: 6620: calling
content:
God's summoning of individuals and people to himself, so that they will belong to him and serve him in his world. The calling of a believer may involve a specific place, task or vocation in life.

God calls individuals to belong to him and serve him

God calls all people everywhere

ACT 17:30 See also PRO 8:4; ISA 45:22, ISA 55:1; MAT 9:13, MAT 11:28–11:30, MAT 22:9; JHN 7:37; ACT 2:39; ROM 10:18; PSA 19:4

Few respond positively to God's call

MAT 22:14 See also ISA 65:12, ISA 66:4; JER 7:13, JER 35:17; MAT 22:2–22:10 parallel LUK 14:16–14:24

Responding to God's call results in salvation

HEB 9:15; JUD 1:1 See also JHN 5:25, JHN 10:3; ROM 1:6, ROM 8:28–8:30, ROM 11:29; 1CO 1:9, 1CO 1:24–1:29, 1CO 7:18–7:22; GAL 1:6; EPH 4:1; PHP 3:14; 2TH 1:11, 2TH 2:14; 1TI 6:12; HEB 3:1; 1PE 5:10; 2PE 1:3, 2PE 1:10; REV 17:14

God calls Christians to live differently

2TI 1:9; 1PE 2:9 1CO 7:15; ; COL 3:15; GAL 5:13 serving one another; EPH 1:18, EPH 4:4; ; 1PE 2:20–2:21, 1PE 3:9; 1TH 4:7

God called particular individuals to do specific tasks at specific times

Abraham: GEN 12:1–12:3; ; ISA 51:2; ; HEB 11:8; EXO 3:4 Moses; ; 1SA 3:4–3:10 Samuel; ; ISA 6:8 Isaiah; ISA 41:2, ISA 45:4; ISA 42:6, ISA 49:1; JER 1:4–1:5 Jeremiah; ; HOS 1:2 Hosea; ; AMO 7:15 Amos; JON 1:1–1:2, JON 3:1–3:2; MAT 4:18–4:22 parallel MRK 1:16–1:20; ; LUK 5:2–5:11; ; JHN 1:35–1:42; ACT 13:2 Barnabas and Paul; ROM 1:1; ; 1CO 1:1; ; GAL 1:15

God called a people, Israel, to serve him in a special way

ISA 43:1 See also HOS 11:1–11:2

The believer's place in life is understood to be a calling

1CO 7:17 See also 1CO 7:20, 1CO 7:24

title: 6622: choice
content:
A deliberate act of will or decision, in which God chooses individuals or peoples to be his or calls them to perform certain tasks or responsibilities. Human beings are also required to make choices, particularly between good and evil.

God's sovereign choice

MAL 1:2–1:3 See also ROM 9:10–9:15; EXO 33:19; 1CO 1:27–1:28; JAS 2:5

God's chosen people

Israel

DEU 7:6 See also EXO 19:4–19:6; DEU 14:2; PSA 65:4; ISA 14:1, ISA 41:9; JER 3:14

NT believers

EPH 1:4 See also ROM 8:28–8:33; 1PE 2:9

God chooses people for special tasks

EXO 3:10 See also 1SA 9:17, 1SA 16:1–16:13; HAG 2:20–2:23; MRK 3:13–3:19 parallel LUK 6:13–6:16; ACT 9:10–9:15; GAL 1:15–1:16

God chose where he was to be worshipped

DEU 12:5 See also DEU 12:11, DEU 12:14, DEU 12:18, DEU 12:26, DEU 14:23, DEU 16:2, DEU 16:6–16:7, DEU 16:11, DEU 16:15–16:16, DEU 26:2, DEU 31:11

People must choose between good and evil

JOS 24:15; PRO 16:16 See also EXO 32:26; DEU 30:19; 1KI 18:21; PRO 3:31, PRO 8:10; JHN 7:17

Examples of bad choices

JAS 4:4 See also PRO 1:28–1:33; ISA 65:12, ISA 66:3–66:4; MAT 27:21 parallel MRK 15:9–15:11

Examples of wise choices

HEB 11:24–11:26 See also JOS 24:21–24:24; RUT 1:16; 1KI 3:5–3:14

title: 6624: confession, of sin
content:
An admission of sin on the part of individuals or groups of people. Genuine repentance and confession herald divine forgiveness.

Confession of sin leads to divine forgiveness

Under the old covenant

PRO 28:13 See also LEV 16:21 Part of the ritual of the Day of Atonement involved the high priest confessing the sins of the nation, while laying his hands on the head of a sacrificial goat; LEV 26:40–26:42; 1KI 8:33–8:36 parallel 2CH 6:24–6:27; 1KI 8:46–8:52 parallel 2CH 6:36–6:39; PSA 32:3–32:5

Under the new covenant

1JN 1:8–1:10 See also JAS 5:13–5:16

Confession of sin accompanied by acts appropriate to repentance

NUM 5:5–5:8 See also LEV 5:5–5:6; EZR 10:10–10:11; NEH 9:1–9:3; LUK 19:8; ACT 19:18–19:19

Examples of confession of sin

On behalf of the nation

NEH 1:5–1:7 See also EZR 9:5–9:15; ISA 59:12–59:13, ISA 64:6–64:7; JER 14:7, JER 14:20; DAN 9:4–9:14

By the people as a whole

1SA 7:5–7:6 See also EZR 9:4–9:7, EZR 9:10–9:15; NEH 9:33–9:35; PSA 106:6; JER 3:24–3:25

By individuals

PSA 51:1–51:5 See also 2SA 24:10 parallel 1CH 21:10; 2SA 24:17 parallel 1CH 21:17; JOB 7:20, JOB 42:5–42:6; ISA 6:5

Confession of sin to other people

JOS 7:20–7:21 See also LUK 15:17–15:24; JAS 5:16

title: 6626: conversion
content: Turning or returning to God in repentance, faith and obedience by those who do not know God or who have turned from him. Although conversion can be seen as a human act or decision, Scripture stresses that the work of God lies behind this human decision, guiding and motivating it.

title: 6627: conversion, nature of
content:
True repentance results in a turning from sin and an inner renewal which can only be brought about by God, who draws people to himself and who, through Jesus Christ, gives forgiveness and new life.

Conversion as turning to God

Turning back to God

DEU 4:30–4:31; LUK 1:16–1:17 See also DEU 30:2–30:3, DEU 30:10; LUK 22:32; JAS 5:19–5:20

Turning from idolatry

ACT 14:15 See also 1SA 7:3; JER 3:12–3:13, JER 4:1–4:2; 1TH 1:9–1:10

Turning from sinful ways

2KI 17:13–17:14; ISA 55:6–55:7 See also 2KI 13:11, 2KI 14:24, 2KI 15:9; JER 18:11, JER 25:5; EZK 18:23; DAN 9:13

Conversion as a turning away from unbelief to faith

It is linked to repentance

ACT 3:19 See also EZK 14:6, EZK 18:30; ACT 26:20

It is linked to coming to faith

ACT 11:21

Conversion brings new life

It results in a transformed life

2CO 5:17 See also ROM 12:2; 2CO 3:18; GAL 6:14–6:15

It is symbolised in baptism

ROM 6:3–6:4 See also COL 2:12, COL 3:1–3:3

It demands a new lifestyle

HOS 12:6; MAT 18:3–18:4 See also GAL 5:22–5:24; EPH 4:1, EPH 5:8–5:11; 1PE 2:11–2:12

Conversion brings a new relationship with God

It brings a new status

GAL 4:7 See also GAL 3:26–3:29; 1JN 3:1; 1PE 2:9–2:10

It brings a new understanding

2CO 3:15–3:16 See also JER 31:34; HEB 8:11

Conversion is a work of God

God turns people to himself

JER 24:7; LAM 5:21 See also 1KI 8:58; JER 31:18; EZK 36:26–36:27; JHN 6:44, JHN 15:16; EPH 2:12–2:13

God gives new birth

JAS 1:17–1:18 See also JHN 3:3–3:6; TIT 3:4–3:5; 1PE 1:23

title: 6628: conversion, God's demands for
content:
Through his servants, God calls people to turn to him. He wants to save the world which is alienated from him, and to restore those of his backslidden people who return to him and renew their commitment to the covenant.

The need for conversion

The world is alienated from God

ROM 1:21–1:23 See also ROM 1:28, ROM 3:11–3:12; PSA 14:3

The world does not know God

GAL 4:8 See also 1CO 1:21, 1CO 15:34; 1TH 4:5; 2TH 1:8

God's people have turned away from him

MAL 3:7 See also NUM 14:43; DEU 9:12; 1SA 15:11 Saul; 1KI 11:9 Solomon; 2CH 25:27 Amaziah; JER 8:5; EZK 6:9; DAN 9:11; ACT 7:39

God's demands for the conversion of all people

ISA 45:22; ACT 17:30 See also PSA 65:5; JON 4:11; ZEC 9:10

God's demands for the conversion of his own people

God's desire to save his people

EZK 33:11 See also JER 3:22; JOL 2:12–2:13; ZEC 1:3

God's faithfulness to his people

JER 3:14 See also LEV 26:40–26:42; 2CH 30:9; HOS 3:5, HOS 6:1–6:3

God's promise of restoration

2CH 7:13–7:14 See also 1KI 8:48–8:49 parallel 2CH 6:38–6:39; NEH 1:9

The call to conversion through God's servants

The encouragement of his leaders

JOS 24:14–24:15 Joshua; ; 2KI 11:17 Jehoida; 2KI 23:24–23:25; ; 2CH 34:31–34:33; 2CH 15:11–15:15 Asa; ; 2CH 19:4 Jehoshaphat; ; 2CH 29:6–29:10 Hezekiah; ; PSA 51:13 David

The ministry of his prophets

JER 35:15 See also 2CH 24:19; JER 25:4; HOS 14:1–14:2; JON 3:2

The preaching of believers

ACT 26:16–26:18 See also ACT 2:38; ROM 10:14–10:15; ISA 52:7

Rejection of God's call to conversion

ISA 9:13 See also JER 3:10; HOS 5:4, HOS 7:10; AMO 4:6–4:11

resource.label: 4811: darkness, as a symbol of sin

title: 6629: conversion, examples of
content:
Scripture gives examples of individuals and peoples who have turned to God in conversion.

Examples of conversion in the OT

Peoples who turned to God

ISA 19:22 Egypt; ; JON 3:5–3:10 Nineveh

Individuals who turned to God

2KI 5:15 Naaman; ; 2CH 33:12–33:13 Manasseh; ; DAN 4:34–4:37 Nebuchadnezzar; ; JON 1:16 sailors on board ship with Jonah

Examples of conversion in the NT

Examples of people in general

JHN 4:42 people of Sychar; ; ACT 2:41 3,000 people on the day of Pentecost; ; ACT 8:12 Samaritans; ; ACT 9:35 people of Lydda and Sharon; ; ACT 15:3 Gentile converts

Examples of individual converts

LUK 15:18 the prodigal son; ; LUK 19:8–19:9 Zacchaeus; ; ACT 8:35–8:38 the Ethiopian eunuch; ACT 9:3–9:18 parallels ACT 22:6–22:11, ACT 26:12–26:18; ACT 10:44–10:48 Cornelius; ; ACT 13:12 the proconsul, Sergius Paulus; ; ACT 16:14–16:15 Lydia; ; ACT 16:29–16:34 the Philippian jailer

The prophetic vision of the future conversion of Israel and the nations

Israel's turning back to God

HOS 3:5 See also JER 50:4–50:5; HOS 11:10–11:11; ROM 11:26

The conversion of the nations

JER 16:19 See also PSA 22:27–22:28; ISA 2:3 parallel MIC 4:2; ZEC 14:16

title: 6632: conviction
content:
The proving of a person guilty of sin or a crime. Scripture stresses that God has declared the guilt, together with its consequences, of sinful humanity. The term can also refer to a state of being convinced; i.e., the holding of a strong belief, especially a conscious awareness of one's guilt and the atoning death of Jesus Christ as the means of remission of that guilt.

Conviction is proving a person guilty of sin or a crime

Proving someone guilty of sin

JHN 8:46 See also JHN 16:8–16:9 NIV footnote at verse 8; 1CO 14:24; JAS 2:9; JUD 1:15

Proving someone guilty of a crime

DEU 19:15 See also EXO 22:9; LEV 17:3–17:4NUM 35:30; DEU 17:6 2SA 14:13; PSA 5:10, PSA 109:7; PRO 24:25LUK 23:4, LUK 23:14, LUK 23:22; JHN 18:38ACT 24:13, ACT 25:7

God's conviction of sinful humanity

He declares them to be guilty

ROM 3:19–3:20 See also PSA 5:9, PSA 10:7, PSA 14:1–14:3 parallel PSA 53:1–53:3; PSA 140:3; ECC 7:20; ISA 59:7–59:8; ROM 3:10–3:18

He also declares the consequences of their guilt

GEN 2:17, GEN 3:17–3:19; ; ROM 1:32, ROM 5:12, ROM 5:16–5:19; ; 1CO 15:22

Conviction as holding a strong belief

ROM 14:5 See also GEN 45:28; MAT 17:20; LUK 20:6 parallels MAT 21:26, MRK 11:32 The people were convinced that John was a prophet; LUK 16:31ACT 17:4, ACT 19:26, ACT 28:24 ACT 26:9, ACT 26:26; ROM 2:19, ROM 4:21, ROM 8:38–8:39 Paul's conviction that it is impossible to be separated from God's love; ROM 14:14 Paul's personal convictions about food regulations; ROM 15:14; 2CO 5:14; PHP 1:25; 1TH 1:5; 2TI 1:5 Paul is convinced by Timothy's faith; 2TI 1:12, 2TI 3:14

Conviction as the conscious awareness of one's guilt

Examples of conviction not resulting in repentance

EXO 9:27 See also EXO 10:16; NUM 14:40, NUM 22:34; DEU 1:41; JDG 10:101SA 15:24, 1SA 24:17, 1SA 26:21 MAT 27:3–27:4 Judas Iscariot; ACT 24:25 Governor Felix

Examples of conviction leading to repentance

ACT 2:37 2SA 12:13; ; PSA 51:3–51:4; MAT 26:75 parallels MRK 14:72, LUK 22:62 Peter; LUK 15:17–15:18, LUK 15:21; ACT 16:30 the Philippian jailer; ; ROM 7:9

resource.label: 3296: Holy Spirit, work in the world

title: 6634: deliverance
content:
God frequently rescues people from danger, suffering and sin, often through human agency.

The source of deliverance

Deliverance comes from God

PSA 3:8 See also EXO 3:7–3:8; PSA 18:2; DAN 3:16–3:17; JON 2:9

There is no deliverance by other gods

2KI 18:33 parallel ISA 36:18

There is no escape from God

ISA 43:13

Deliverance may be declined

MAT 26:53–26:54; ; JHN 18:36; ; HEB 11:35

Different kinds of deliverance

From danger

1SA 17:37 See also LUK 8:23–8:24; 2CO 1:9–1:10; 2TI 4:17

From illness

PSA 103:3; ; ISA 38:16

From trouble

PSA 34:17, PSA 34:19, PSA 54:7

From slavery

EXO 20:2

From enemies

JER 38:10–38:13; ; DAN 6:19–6:22; ; LUK 1:74; ; 2TH 3:2

From Satan

LUK 13:16, LUK 22:31–22:32

From the fear of death

HEB 2:14–2:15

From all fears

PSA 34:4; ; REV 1:17

From sin

GAL 1:4 See also PSA 32:7; 2TH 2:13; REV 1:5

From the coming wrath

LUK 3:7; ; 1TH 1:10, 1TH 5:9

God promises deliverance

God's people are to pray for deliverance

MAT 6:13 See also 2KI 19:19 parallel ISA 37:20; PSA 59:1–59:2

Direct intervention by God

EXO 14:13; ; 2KI 7:6

Deliverance by angels

ACT 12:7

Deliverance by human agency

GEN 37:17–37:22; ; JDG 2:16; ; ACT 23:16–23:24

Abraham

GEN 14:14–14:16

Moses

ACT 7:35–7:36 See also PSA 77:20

Joshua

EXO 17:8–17:13

The judges

JDG 2:16 See also JDG 3:9, JDG 3:15, JDG 3:31, JDG 4:1–4:10, JDG 4:23, JDG 6:12, JDG 6:16, JDG 10:1, JDG 11:32, JDG 13:2–13:5, JDG 13:24–13:25

Samuel

1SA 7:3–7:13

Saul

1SA 11:9–11:13

Jonathan

1SA 14:45

David

2SA 8:1, 2SA 8:14

Jesus Christ

LUK 4:18–4:19 See also ISA 61:1–61:2; 1TI 2:5; HEB 2:14–2:15

Gifted Christians

Praise

Delight

Thanksgiving
