Trust

PSA 40:1–40:3

Obedience

PSA 56:12–56:13; ; LUK 8:38–8:39; ; JHN 5:14, JHN 8:10–8:11

title: 6636: drawing near to God
content:
Under the old covenant, coming near to God was closely linked with the sacrificial system, but under the new covenant Jesus Christ's sacrifice has given believers free access into God's presence.

Drawing near to God under the old covenant

Access to God was restricted

EXO 24:1–24:2 See also EXO 19:20–19:24, EXO 20:18–20:19; LEV 21:21; DEU 5:22–5:27; PSA 65:4

Priests drew near to God in order to minister to him

EZK 44:15 See also LEV 10:1–10:3; EZK 40:46, EZK 42:13, EZK 43:19, EZK 45:4

The people drew near to God to offer worship

LEV 15:13–15:15 See also LEV 9:1–9:5; PSA 96:8, PSA 100:2–100:4; MIC 6:6–6:8

Drawing near to God under the new covenant

HEB 10:19–10:22 See also 1CO 8:8; HEB 7:11–7:19, HEB 10:1–10:10

God prompts his people to draw near to him

ISA 55:1–55:3 See also ISA 55:6–55:7; JER 3:21–4:2

Some refuse to draw near to God

ZEP 3:2 See also JER 2:30–2:31

All people will ultimately draw near to God

Israel

JER 31:6 See also JER 30:21–30:22; HOS 3:4–3:5

All nations

ISA 66:22–66:23

God draws near to those who draw near to him

JAS 4:8 See also PSA 145:18; JER 3:22, JER 30:21–30:22; ZEC 1:3; MAL 3:7; HEB 7:19

title: 6638: election
content: Scripture affirms that God chooses a people as his own, not on account of their numerical strength or moral merits, but on account of his love for them. Election is on the basis of divine grace, not human merit.

title: 6639: election, to salvation
content:
God chooses to bring individuals to salvation through faith in Jesus Christ.

Election is part of God's eternal decree

Election is from eternity

EPH 1:4 See also 2TH 2:13; 2TI 1:9

Election is God's sovereign prerogative

ROM 9:15–9:24, ROM 11:1–11:6 See also EXO 33:19; ISA 65:1; JER 18:1–18:12; JHN 15:16, JHN 17:6; ROM 9:10–9:13; EPH 2:10

God's election places individuals within the covenant of grace

NEH 9:7–9:8 See also GEN 15:7–15:8, GEN 15:18–15:21, GEN 18:19; GAL 3:29

Election is not on the basis of merit

1CO 1:26–1:31 See also DEU 7:7–7:8, DEU 9:4–9:6; JAS 2:5

God's election of his people is the foundation of his saving action

Election does not suspend God's use of the means of salvation

2TH 2:13–2:14 See also MAT 1:21; EPH 2:8–2:10; JAS 1:18; 1PE 1:2

Election works in tandem with the call of the gospel

MAT 22:14 See also ROM 8:29–8:30

Election is evidenced through a positive response to the gospel

1TH 1:4–1:5 See also JHN 6:37–6:40

Election is a motive for praise

ROM 11:28–11:36; EPH 1:3–1:14 See also 1TH 1:2–1:4

Election is a source of practical comfort

ROM 8:31–8:39 See also JHN 10:27–10:29, JHN 17:2

Election is an incentive for righteous behaviour

COL 3:12–3:14 See also JHN 15:16–15:17; PHP 2:12–2:13; 2TH 2:13–2:15; 2PE 1:3–1:11

Election is a stimulus to the preaching of the gospel

2TI 2:10

title: 6640: election, privileges of
content:
God chose a people to enjoy a unique relationship with him which entailed the privilege of belonging to him, sharing in all his inheritance, serving him, praising him and proclaiming him.

God elected Israel out of all the nations of the world

DEU 7:6 See also DEU 14:1–14:2; 1KI 3:8; 1CH 16:13; PSA 105:6, PSA 105:43, PSA 135:4; ISA 41:8–41:9; EZK 20:5–20:6

The election of the nation is anticipated in the election of the patriarchs

DEU 4:37–4:38 See also DEU 10:15; ACT 13:17–13:19

Election was not based on merit

DEU 7:7–7:8 See also DEU 9:4–9:6; EZK 16:1–16:14

God conferred privileges on the people he elected

Election meant that God dwelled in their midst

2CH 6:6 See also 1KI 14:21 parallel 2CH 12:13; 2KI 21:7 parallel 2CH 33:7; 2CH 6:34–6:35, 2CH 7:12, 2CH 7:16; NEH 1:9; PSA 132:13

Election meant that God showed divine favour

PSA 33:12 See also PSA 65:4, PSA 106:4–106:5; ISA 14:1, ISA 43:1, ISA 43:20, ISA 44:1–44:5, ISA 45:4; ZEC 1:17

God gave responsiblilities to the people he elected

Election calls for obedient and holy living

LEV 20:26 See also EXO 19:5–19:6

Election means increased accountability

AMO 3:2 See also 2KI 23:26

God's choice of Israel does not automatically imply election to salvation

ROM 9:6–9:8 See also ROM 11:7–11:8

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 6641: election, responsibilities of
content:
God's election lays responsibilities upon those who have been chosen. Election to salvation has the natural consequence of election to service.

Election to leadership

JER 1:5 See also JDG 13:2–13:5; PSA 105:26, PSA 106:23

Election to religious service

DEU 21:5 See also DEU 18:3–18:5; 1SA 2:27–2:28; 1CH 15:2; 2CH 29:11

Election to build the house of God

Election to build the tabernacle

EXO 31:2–31:5 See also EXO 35:30–35:33, EXO 31:6–31:11, EXO 35:34–36:2

Election to build the temple

1CH 28:6 See also 1CH 28:10, 1CH 29:1

Election of foreign kings as instruments of discipline

ISA 45:1 ISA 44:28, ISA 48:14–48:15; ISA 7:17, ISA 7:20; JER 25:9, JER 27:6, JER 43:10

Election to kingship

1CH 28:4 1SA 16:1–16:13; ; PSA 78:70–78:71, PSA 89:3–89:4; 1SA 10:20–10:24 Saul; ; 1CH 28:5–28:7 Solomon

Election of the Messiah

The OT predicts the coming of a ``chosen one''

ISA 42:1–42:7 See also ISA 9:6–9:7, ISA 49:5–49:7

The NT identifies Jesus Christ as God's chosen Messiah

LUK 9:35 parallel MAT 3:16–3:17 See also MAT 12:15–12:21; ISA 42:1–42:4; 1PE 1:18–1:21, 1PE 2:4–2:6

Election to apostleship

LUK 6:13–6:16 parallel MRK 3:14–3:19 See also JHN 6:70, JHN 13:18, JHN 15:16, JHN 15:19; ACT 1:2, ACT 1:21–1:26, ACT 9:15–9:16, ACT 22:14–22:15; ROM 1:1; 1CO 1:1

title: 6644: eternal life
content: The state of being in a permanent living relationship with God, through Jesus Christ, begun in this life and consummated at the resurrection of believers.

title: 6645: eternal life, nature of
content:
Triumph over death and infinitely extended life. It also means a unique quality of life, knowing God in this life and sharing the life of Christ. Its source is God himself.

Eternal life is knowing God and enjoying his eternal blessings

JHN 17:3; PSA 16:11 See also PSA 21:4–21:6; EPH 1:3–1:14

Eternal glory is the believer's inheritance

2CO 4:17 See also DAN 12:3; 2TI 2:10; 1PE 5:4, 1PE 5:10

Contrasted with eternal judgment and rejection

MAT 25:46 Interpreters differ over whether “eternal punishment” implies an experience that continues for ever, or annihilation which is eternal in the sense of being irreversible. See also DAN 12:2; MAT 25:46; JHN 3:16; 2TH 1:8–1:9

The work of helping others come to eternal life

JHN 4:35–4:36

Eternal life as endless life

The guarantee of the resurrection

1CO 15:20–15:22 “firstfruits” because the resurrection of Jesus Christ is seen as the beginning of a harvest. See also PSA 49:15; ISA 26:19; 1TH 4:14

Death will be vanquished

REV 21:4 See also ISA 25:8; 1CO 15:26, 1CO 15:50–15:57

Believers will live for ever

JHN 11:25–11:26 See also LUK 20:34–20:36; JHN 6:51, JHN 6:54, JHN 6:58, JHN 8:51; 1JN 2:17

Eternal life as a quality of life

JHN 10:10 See also JHN 4:14

One of the blessings associated with the kingdom of God

MAT 19:29 parallels MRK 10:30, LUK 18:30; ; LUK 18:18

Life with Christ

JHN 6:67–6:69, JHN 15:5; ; COL 3:4; ; 1TH 5:10

Eternal life as experiencing Jesus Christ's life

GAL 2:20 See also 2CO 4:10–4:11; ROM 6:4, ROM 6:8, ROM 8:10–8:11

God is the source and giver of eternal life

God the Father

JHN 17:3 See also DEU 5:26; PSA 36:9, PSA 42:2, PSA 56:13; MAT 22:32 parallel MRK 12:27; LUK 20:38; 1TI 6:13

God the Son

JHN 14:6 See also JHN 1:4, JHN 11:25–11:26, JHN 17:3; 1JN 1:1–1:2, 1JN 5:20

God the Holy Spirit

JHN 6:63 See also 2CO 3:6; GAL 6:8

title: 6646: eternal life, gift of
content:
Eternal life cannot be earned or merited but must be received as a gift from God.

Fruitless attempts to earn eternal life

JHN 5:39–5:40 It is possible to study the mere letter of Scripture while ignoring coming to its focus, Jesus Christ; GAL 3:11–3:12 See also ROM 3:20, ROM 3:28

Jesus Christ is the source of eternal life

1JN 5:11–5:12 See also JHN 11:25–11:26, JHN 14:6; ROM 5:21; HEB 5:9, HEB 9:12; 2TI 1:1, 2TI 1:10; 1JN 5:20

Eternal life is a gift

ROM 6:23 See also JHN 6:27, JHN 10:28; ROM 2:7, ROM 5:15–5:17; EPH 2:8–2:9; 1PE 3:7; 2PE 1:3

Received by faith

JHN 3:36 Faith in Jesus Christ, God's son, is the only way to gain eternal life. See also JHN 3:16, JHN 5:24, JHN 6:40, JHN 20:31; 1TI 1:16

The need for personal commitment

JHN 12:25–12:26 See also MAT 7:14; MRK 8:37–8:38; ACT 11:18 Repentance is the first step of commitment; ROM 6:8–6:11

title: 6647: eternal life, experience of
content:
Eternal life is a present possession, to to be experienced here and now, but it also offers rich and joyful future prospects.

Eternal life as a present possession

JHN 5:24 See also ROM 6:22; GAL 2:20; EPH 2:4–2:5; COL 2:13, COL 3:1–3:4; 1TI 6:12

The Holy Spirit as a sign of new life

ROM 8:9; ; 1CO 3:16, 1CO 6:19; ; EPH 1:13–1:14; ; 2TI 1:14

Eternal life as a future hope

1PE 1:3–1:5

Present experience the basis for future hope

See also COL 1:27; 2TH 2:16–2:17

Eternal life contrasted with present suffering

ROM 8:18 See also MAT 5:11–5:12; LUK 18:28–18:30 parallels MAT 19:29, MRK 10:29–10:30; JAS 1:12

The life to come

1TI 4:8 See also ROM 2:7; 2CO 5:4; COL 1:5; JUD 1:21

Being with Christ for ever

2CO 5:8 See also LUK 23:43; JHN 14:2–14:3, JHN 17:24; 1TH 4:15–4:17

Assurance of eternal life

The keeping power of God

JHN 10:28–10:29 See also ROM 8:38–8:39; 1PE 1:3–1:5

The believer's confidence in God

JHN 17:2–17:3 See also PSA 73:26; JOB 19:25; 1JN 5:11–5:13

The sure and certain hope

TIT 1:2, TIT 3:5–3:7; ; 2PE 1:10–1:11

title: 6648: expiation
content:
The removal of the guilt of sin in God's sight by the offering of a vicarious sacrifice which, in its death, bears the sin of the individual or nation concerned.

Expiation is necessary because sin is punishable by God

EXO 32:30 See also NUM 16:46–16:48; DEU 21:8; PSA 79:8–79:9

Expiation and the sacrificial system

Unintentional sins expiated by the sin offering

LEV 4:1–5:6; ; NUM 15:22–15:29

Unintentional sins requiring restitution expiated by the guilt offering

LEV 5:14–6:7, LEV 19:20–19:22

The nation's sins expiated on the annual Day of Atonement

EXO 30:10 See also LEV 16:3–16:34; HEB 9:7

Blood as a symbol of substitutionary sacrifice

LEV 17:11 See also EZK 45:19–45:20; HEB 9:22, HEB 13:11

The rite of expiation administered by the priests

LEV 9:7 See also LEV 7:7, LEV 9:15, LEV 10:17–10:18; NUM 6:11

Expiation and special provision for the poor

LEV 12:7–12:8 See also LEV 5:7–5:13, LEV 14:21–14:32

The sin offering as a constant reminder of the need for expiation

Sin offerings for occasions of dedication

EXO 29:31–29:33 See also LEV 8:14–8:15; NUM 8:12; 2CH 29:21–29:24; EZR 6:17

Sin offerings for occasions of purification

LEV 12:6–12:7, LEV 14:19–14:20, LEV 14:49–14:53, LEV 15:13–15:15, LEV 15:28–15:30; ; NUM 19:1–19:13

Sin offerings as part of the regular cycle of worship

NUM 28:15, NUM 28:22, NUM 28:30, NUM 29:5, NUM 29:11, NUM 29:16

The death of Jesus Christ as the fulfilment and replacement of the old sacrificial system

The death of Jesus Christ as a substitutionary, expiatory sacrifice

1JN 2:2 See also 1CO 15:3; GAL 1:4; HEB 1:3; 1PE 3:18

God's provision of Jesus Christ as an atoning sacrice

ROM 3:25 See also ISA 53:10; ROM 8:3; 1JN 4:10

The blood of Jesus Christ as an atonement for sin

HEB 9:13–9:14 See also MAT 26:28; EPH 1:7; 1JN 1:7; REV 1:5

The sacrifice of Jesus Christ as final and unique

HEB 7:27 See also ROM 6:10; HEB 9:25–10:14

title: 6650: finding
content:
Discovering something or someone that is being looked for. The whole of Scripture points to God seeking out lost human beings and finding them. The delight of people at finding things (such as lost coins or wayward sons) is seen as a parable of the joy of God over repentant sinners.

Finding by searching

People do not always find what they are searching for

PRO 20:6 See also GEN 27:36–27:40; 2SA 17:20

God is sometimes found without being sought

ROM 10:20 See also ISA 65:1; ROM 9:30–9:31

Promises that God can be found

DEU 4:29; MAT 7:7–7:8 parallel LUK 11:9–11:10 See also JER 29:13–29:14

People's joy in finding God

Finding the kingdom

MAT 13:44 See also MAT 13:45–13:46

Finding the infant Jesus

MAT 2:9–2:11 See also LUK 2:10–2:12, LUK 2:16–2:18

Finding the Messiah

JHN 1:41 See also MRK 1:36; JHN 1:45

Finding the risen Christ

JHN 20:20 See also MAT 28:1–28:9; LUK 24:40–24:43

Results of finding God

Finding truth

JHN 8:31–8:32 See also PSA 25:4–25:5; JHN 16:13; 1TI 2:3–2:4

Finding rest and security

PSA 36:7 See also PSA 91:4; MAT 11:28–11:29

Finding mercy

PRO 28:13 See also PSA 6:9; MAT 5:7

Finding satisfaction

ECC 2:24–2:25

Finding success

1SA 18:14 See also GEN 39:23

Finding life

PRO 8:35 See also MAT 7:13–7:14, MAT 10:39

God's joy in finding repentant sinners

LUK 15:3–15:7 See also LUK 15:8–15:32

Other aspects of finding in life

Finding things

DEU 14:21; ; MAT 17:24–17:27

Finding people

GEN 41:38; ; ACT 19:1; ; 2TI 1:16–1:17

Finding a wife

GEN 24:1–24:4; ; PRO 18:22

Finding wisdom

PRO 3:13, PRO 24:14; ; JAS 1:5

Finding God's will

NUM 9:8; ; JHN 7:17; ; ROM 12:2

Sin finds people out

NUM 32:23; ; 2SA 12:1–12:14; ; ACT 5:1–5:11

title: 6652: forgiveness
content: The freeing of a person from guilt and its consequences, including punishment; usually as an act of favour, compassion or love, with the aim of restoring a broken personal relationship. Forgiveness can involve both the remission of punishment and the cancellation of debts.

title: 6653: forgiveness, divine
content:
God forgives the sins of believers on the basis of the once for all sacrifice offered by Jesus Christ on the cross. Believers' sins are no longer held against them, on account of the atoning death of Jesus Christ.

God's nature and forgiveness

EXO 34:5–34:7 See also NUM 14:17–14:20; NEH 9:16–9:17; PSA 103:1–103:18; ISA 43:25; MIC 7:18–7:20; 1JN 1:8–1:9

God's promise of forgiveness

JER 31:31–31:34 See also 2CH 7:14; ISA 55:6–55:7; HEB 8:8–8:12

People's need of forgiveness

1JN 1:8–1:10 See also PSA 51:1–51:5; ISA 6:1–6:5; ROM 3:9, ROM 3:23

The means of forgiveness

Under the old covenant

HEB 9:22 See also LEV 4:27–4:31, LEV 5:17–5:18

Under the new covenant

MAT 26:27–26:28 See also JHN 1:29; EPH 1:7–1:8; COL 2:13–2:15

The assurance of forgiveness

1JN 1:8–1:9 See also PSA 51:7, PSA 103:8–103:12, PSA 130:3–130:4; PRO 28:13; ISA 1:18; ACT 2:38; JAS 5:13–5:16; 1JN 2:1–2:2

title: 6654: forgiveness, Jesus Christ's ministry of
content:
A central feature of Jesus Christ's ministry was his declaration that believers' sins were forgiven through their faith in him.

Jesus Christ's ministry of forgiveness was foretold

MAT 1:20–1:21; JHN 1:29

Jesus Christ's exercise of forgiveness

LUK 23:33–23:34 See also JHN 8:3–8:11

Jesus Christ has authority on account of his divinity to forgive sins

MAT 9:1–9:8 parallels MRK 2:1–2:12, LUK 5:17–5:26 Jesus Christ's authority to forgive is authenticated by his healing of the paralytic.

People's offence at Jesus Christ's exercise of forgiveness

MRK 2:5–2:7 parallels MAT 9:2–9:3, LUK 5:20–5:21

Parables of forgiveness

See also MAT 18:23–18:35 the unmerciful servant; LUK 7:36–7:50, LUK 15:11–15:32 the lost son

The church's ministry of forgiveness in Jesus Christ's name

JHN 20:21–20:23 See also ACT 2:38, ACT 13:38, ACT 26:15–26:18

title: 6655: forgiveness, application of
content:
God's forgiveness of believers' sins leads them to pray for his forgiveness of others and to be forgiving in their dealings with other people.

Prayers for forgiveness on behalf of others

For God's own people

EXO 32:30–32:32 See also NEH 1:4–1:11; DAN 9:4–9:19; AMO 7:1–7:6

For other human beings

GEN 18:20–18:33

For one's persecutors

LUK 23:33–23:34 See also MAT 5:43–5:44; ACT 7:59–7:60

Prayers for forgiveness for oneself

LUK 11:4 parallel MAT 6:12 See also NEH 9:1–9:3; PSA 51:1–51:17

Examples of forgiveness given

PSA 32:1–32:5 See also ISA 6:1–6:7; JHN 8:3–8:11

The call to exercise forgiveness

As a principle of life

LUK 6:37 See also MAT 5:38–5:48 parallel LUK 6:27–6:36

Within the church

COL 3:12–3:13 See also 2CO 2:5–2:11; EPH 4:32; 1PE 3:8–3:9

Forgiving enemies

PRO 24:17, PRO 25:21–25:22; ; MAT 5:44; ; ROM 12:20

Being forgiven is dependent on forgiving others

MRK 11:25–11:26 NIV footnote at verse 26. See also MAT 6:12 parallel LUK 11:4; MAT 6:14–6:15, MAT 18:21–18:35; LUK 6:37

Forgiveness is to be without limits

LUK 17:3–17:4 See also MAT 18:21–18:22

Examples of human forgiveness

ACT 7:59–7:60 See also GEN 50:15–50:21; 2SA 16:5–16:11

title: 6658: freedom
content: The state of liberty that results from not being oppressed or in bondage. Scripture stresses that human beings lack freedom on account of sin, but that faith in Jesus Christ brings freedom from the power of sin and the law.

title: 6659: freedom, acts of in OT
content:
The OT provides many examples of God delivering his people from bondage, of which the greatest is the exodus. These acts of deliverance foreshadow and prepare the way for the redeeming work of God through Jesus Christ, revealed in the NT.

God's people regain their freedom

The exodus from Egypt as an act of deliverance

content:

title: 6674: imputation
content:
The crediting by God to believers with righteousness on account of Jesus Christ. Paul argues that Abraham did nothing which earned him the status of being righteous in the sight of God. Rather, Abraham believed the promise of God, and for that reason was granted the status of being righteous before God. Likewise, all who trust in Jesus Christ have righteousness imputed to them--that is, reckoned as if it was theirs. Imputation should not be confused with impartation. Believers are not made right ethically (impartation), but put right relationally (imputation). What God changes is not the character of believers but their legal standing before him. From this new position, believers are called to co-operate with the Holy Spirit in sanctification so that their character increasingly reflects their new standing.

Righteousness imputed through faith not works

The example of Abraham

ROM 4:1–4:3 See also GEN 15:1–15:6; ROM 4:9–4:22; GAL 3:6–3:9

JAS 2:20–2:24 James does not contradict, but complements Paul's teaching by showing that the person who has been credited with righteousness will always express this in works.

title: 6676: justification
content: The acquittal, or declaration of being righteous, before God as judge. It is a central aspect of Paul's understanding of what God achieved for believers through the death and resurrection of Jesus Christ.

ACT 7:53 See also JOB 5:1, JOB 16:19–16:21, JOB 33:23; DAN 4:13, DAN 4:23

Jesus Christ is the only true mediator

1TI 2:5

Jesus Christ as greater than all OT mediators

JHN 1:17 See also HEB 1:3, HEB 3:1–3:6, HEB 7:26–7:28, HEB 8:6, HEB 9:23–9:24

Mediators between people

2SA 14:1–14:33 Joab; ; MAT 5:9 Christians; ; ACT 9:26–9:27 Barnabas; 1CO 1:10–1:12; ; PHP 4:2–4:3; ; PHM 1:17–1:21

title: 6686: mercy
content: A quality of compassion, especially as expressed in God's forgiveness of human sin. Scripture stresses God's forbearance towards sinners. In his mercy, God shields sinners from what they deserve and gives gifts that they do not deserve.

title: 6687: mercy, nature of God's
content:
A central aspect of God's character, expressed in his covenant relationships with undeserving people.

God is merciful

DAN 9:9; EPH 2:4 See also 2SA 24:14; NEH 9:31; PSA 5:7, PSA 25:6; JER 3:12; LUK 1:78; 1TI 1:2; JAS 5:11; 1PE 1:3; 2JN 1:3; JUD 1:2

God's mercy is expressed in his faithfulness to his covenant with his people

MIC 7:18–7:20 See also DEU 4:31, DEU 7:9; 1KI 8:23–8:24; NEH 1:5, NEH 9:30–9:31; PSA 143:1; ISA 63:9; LUK 1:54–1:55, LUK 1:69–1:75

God's faithfulness and mercy are never-ending

PSA 103:17 See also PSA 25:6, PSA 106:1, PSA 119:132

God's mercy and grace are seen in Jesus Christ

JHN 1:14 See also ROM 3:22–3:24, ROM 5:15; EPH 1:7; HEB 4:16; JUD 1:21

God's mercy is not confined to the Jewish nation

ROM 3:29–3:30 See also ISA 49:6; JON 4:2; ROM 10:12, ROM 11:28–11:32, ROM 15:8–15:12; GAL 3:14; EPH 3:6–3:8; COL 1:27

God chooses when and where to exercise his mercy

ROM 9:15–9:16 See also EXO 33:19; DEU 7:7–7:8; PSA 33:12; JHN 15:16; 1CO 1:27–1:29

God's responds in mercy to those who call to him

PSA 6:9 There are many appeals to God's mercy in the book of Psalms. See also 1KI 8:28; 2CH 6:19; JOB 9:15; DAN 9:18

title: 6688: mercy, demonstration of God's
content:
God demonstrates his mercy in his various dealings with his people.

God as a parent

PSA 103:13–103:14; EPH 1:4–1:6 See also ISA 49:15, ISA 63:7–63:8, ISA 66:13; JER 31:20; HOS 11:1–11:4; MAL 3:17; 2CO 1:3

God's mercy and compassion to those in distress

ISA 49:13 See also EXO 2:23–2:24; PSA 91:14–91:16, PSA 111:2–111:9, PSA 113:7–113:9, PSA 142:1–142:3; ISA 63:9; JON 2:2; 2CO 4:1; HEB 4:16

In judgment, God's mercy is just and true

DEU 32:36 See also PSA 143:1; ISA 11:3–11:5, ISA 30:18, ISA 54:7–54:8, ISA 60:10; HAB 3:2; REV 19:11

God may choose to show no mercy

PSA 59:5 See also JOS 11:20; JER 21:7; JAS 2:13

God's mercy shown in his actions

The act of salvation

EPH 2:4–2:5 See also EXO 15:13; JDG 2:18 In the OT, salvation was seen in physical, as much as in spiritual, terms; PSA 13:5, PSA 28:6–28:8, PSA 31:21–31:22PSA 40:10–40:11, PSA 57:1, PSA 69:16, PSA 86:15–86:16 PSA 98:2–98:3, PSA 116:4–116:6; NEH 9:27; 1PE 1:3, 1PE 2:10; 2PE 3:15

His forgiveness

PSA 51:1–51:2 See also NUM 14:18–14:19; PSA 25:6–25:7, PSA 79:8, PSA 130:1–130:4; ISA 55:7; HOS 14:1–14:4; MIC 7:18–7:19; 1TI 1:15–1:16; 1JN 1:9

His blessing

DEU 13:17–13:18 Moses concerning idolatry. See also DEU 7:12–7:13, DEU 30:3

title: 6689: mercy, of Jesus Christ
content:
Jesus Christ displays the same attitude of grace and mercy towards men and women as his Father does.

Jesus Christ is merciful

LUK 4:16–4:21 See also ISA 61:1–61:2JHN 15:12–15:13 See also 2CO 8:9, 2CO 13:14; EPH 4:7; PHP 2:1; 1TI 1:16; 2TI 1:18, 2TI 2:1; JUD 1:21

Jesus Christ's concern for people in need

MAT 9:36 parallel MRK 6:34 See also MAT 14:14, MAT 15:32 parallel MRK 8:2; MAT 23:37 parallel LUK 13:34

Jesus Christ's response to appeals for mercy

MAT 20:30–20:34 See also MAT 8:2–8:3 parallels MRK 1:39–1:40, LUK 5:12–5:13; MAT 8:5–8:13, MAT 9:18–9:30, MAT 15:22, MAT 17:14–17:18; JHN 11:6; 2CO 12:8–12:9

Jesus Christ's mercy in response to judgment

JHN 12:47

Jesus Christ as high priest

HEB 2:17–2:18 See also HEB 4:15

Jesus Christ's mercy is shown in his actions

In salvation

EPH 5:2 See also LUK 19:10; GAL 2:20; EPH 5:25; 1TI 1:14–1:16; TIT 3:4–3:7

In forgiveness

LUK 23:34 See also MRK 2:10

In blessing

MRK 10:13–10:16 parallel MAT 19:13–19:15

resource.label: 2027: Jesus Christ, grace and mercy of

title: 6690: mercy, response to God's
content:
God's merciful dealings with believers should move them to show similar acts of mercy to others, as well as to rejoice in his graciousness and proclaim it to others.

The proper response to God's mercy

A self-giving faith

ROM 12:1 See also PSA 13:5, PSA 52:8; EPH 2:8; 1TI 1:16; HEB 4:16; 1PE 5:5; JAS 4:6

A merciful attitude towards others

LUK 6:36 See also MIC 6:8; ZEC 7:9; MAT 5:7, MAT 9:13; LUK 10:27–10:37; JAS 2:12–2:13

A forgiving attitude towards others

MAT 6:12 parallel LUK 11:4 See also MAT 6:14, MAT 18:21–18:35; LUK 6:37, LUK 17:3–17:4; 2CO 2:7; EPH 4:32; COL 3:13

Mercy is to be a characteristic of daily living

HEB 13:1 See also MAT 25:37–25:40; ROM 12:9–12:21; JUD 1:23

By God's mercy, the believer can persevere

2CO 4:1 See also 2TH 3:3–3:5

Sharing God's grace and mercy with others

2CO 8:1 See also 2CO 5:14–5:20

title: 6691: mercy, human
content:
Believers are urged to show the same qualities of mercy and compassion towards one another as God demonstrates to them. A lack of mercy is regarded as characteristic of godless people.

The need for human mercy

PRO 18:23 See also DEU 28:49–28:50; 2SA 12:6; 1KI 3:26; JOB 19:21; PRO 21:10; ISA 47:6, ISA 49:15; ZEC 7:9–7:11

Believers should be merciful

MIC 6:8; MAT 5:7; LUK 6:36 See also ZEC 7:9; JAS 2:12–2:13; 1PE 3:9; JUD 1:22

Lack of mercy characterises the godless

ISA 13:18 See also JER 6:23, JER 21:7, JER 50:42; AMO 1:11–1:12; HAB 1:13–1:17; MAT 23:23

Lack of mercy on Israel's part was sometimes obedience to God

DEU 7:2 Concerning the peoples they were to displace in the promised land. See also DEU 13:6–13:9, DEU 19:11–19:13, DEU 19:21, DEU 28:53–28:54; JOS 6:17–6:19; 1SA 15:18–15:19

Mercy as a greeting and a blessing

As a greeting

1TI 1:2 See also 2TI 1:2; 2JN 1:3; JUD 1:2

As a blessing

GAL 6:16 See also 2TI 1:16–1:18; JUD 1:21

title: 6694: mystery
content:
Normally, God's plan of salvation which, though once unknown to human beings, is now revealed by God.

God's character and purpose cannot be discerned by human reason

JOB 11:7 See also JOB 38:1–42:6

God reveals his plans through dreams, interpreted by his servant

DAN 2:28 Nebuchadnezzar's dreams require the interpretation that God discloses to Daniel before they can be understood as a revelation of divine purposes for Babylon and the surrounding nations. See also DAN 2:17–2:49, DAN 4:9

God's mysteries are revealed to a man inspired by his Spirit

DAN 4:18 See also AMO 3:7

The mystery of God's kingdom

MAT 13:11 parallels MRK 4:11, LUK 8:10 God's plan to establish his kingdom is revealed only to those chosen to receive it. To those “outside” it is hidden in parables.

Mystery as God's plan of salvation

God's plan, now revealed, has been hidden for long ages

EPH 3:9 The use of “mystery” in this sense is found particularly in Paul's letters. See also ROM 16:25; 1CO 2:7; COL 1:26

God's plan is fulfilled in Jesus Christ

EPH 1:9 See also EPH 3:4; COL 2:2; 1TI 3:16 The “mystery of godliness” is the revealed secret that produces godliness in people.

God's plan through Jesus Christ is for the salvation of Gentiles as well as Jews

EPH 3:6 See also ROM 11:25; COL 1:27

Ultimately God's plan is for the transformation of the whole creation

EPH 1:9–1:10 See also 1CO 15:51–15:53; REV 10:7, REV 17:5–17:8

God's plan is revealed by his Spirit so that his servants may publicly proclaim it

EPH 3:8–3:9 See also EPH 3:4–3:6, EPH 6:19; COL 1:25–1:27, COL 4:3; REV 10:7

Mystery as other aspects of God's purposes, spiritually discerned

2TH 2:7 Paul's reference to “the secret power (literally “mystery”) of lawlessness” suggests that we know some things about evil only as God reveals them. See also 1CO 13:2, 1CO 14:2

Mystery as an allegorical understanding of a biblical text, revealed to Paul by God

EPH 5:31–5:32 Paul sees Ge 2:24 as symbolising the unity between Jesus Christ and his church; the unity of husband and wife is a human reflection of that relationship.

title: 6696: necessity
content:
That which makes certain actions or events occur. Scripture relates it to particular events which must happen to bring about God's plan of salvation and also to the behaviour that believers are to adopt as a result of their commitment to Jesus Christ.

The necessity of Jesus Christ's death and resurrection

LUK 24:25–24:27 See also MAT 16:21 parallels MRK 8:31, LUK 9:22; LUK 17:25, LUK 24:44–24:47; JHN 3:14–3:15, JHN 20:9

The necessity of Jesus Christ's obedience

LUK 4:42–4:44 See also MAT 3:14–3:15; LUK 2:49, LUK 13:31–13:33; JHN 4:4 The necessity lay in Jesus Christ's mission not in geography; JHN 9:4

The necessity of the events leading to the end times

REV 1:1 See also MRK 13:7–13:8 parallels MAT 24:6–24:8, LUK 21:9–21:11; MRK 13:10 parallel MAT 24:14; 1CO 15:50–15:55; 2CO 5:10; REV 4:1, REV 20:1–20:3, REV 22:6

Necessity and the law

GAL 5:3 See also JHN 19:7; HEB 5:3, HEB 9:16, HEB 9:22–9:23

Necessity in the life of the Christian

The necessity of conversion

JHN 3:5–3:7 See also MAT 18:2–18:3; JHN 3:3; ACT 4:12, ACT 17:30

The necessity of godly behaviour

1JN 2:6 See also ROM 15:1–15:2; 1TI 3:14–3:15, 1TI 4:7–4:8; 2TI 2:22–2:24; TIT 2:11–2:14

Necessity in the life of Paul

The necessity of Paul's calling

ACT 9:15–9:16 See also ACT 9:6

The necessity of Paul's preaching of the gospel

ACT 13:46, ACT 19:21, ACT 23:11, ACT 27:21–27:25, ACT 28:19; ; ROM 1:14; ; 1CO 9:16–9:18

The paradoxes of necessity

Freedom and necessity

ROM 6:18 See also 1CO 9:19–9:23; 2CO 9:7; PHM 1:14

Responsibility and necessity

MAT 18:7 parallel LUK 17:1 See also LUK 22:22 parallels MAT 26:24, MRK 14:21; ACT 2:23

title: 6698: newness
content:
Through Jesus Christ, God has made possible a new relationship between himself and sinful humanity. This involves the renewal of human nature and eventually a renewal of creation itself.

The newness of the gospel

The teaching of Jesus Christ was acclaimed as something new

MRK 1:27

The kingdom of God is like new cloth

LUK 5:36 parallels MAT 9:16, MRK 2:21

The kingdom of God is like new wine

LUK 5:37–5:38 parallels MAT 9:17, MRK 2:22

A new covenant has been established between God and his people

JER 31:31; HEB 9:15 See also 1CO 11:25; 2CO 3:6; HEB 8:1–8:13, HEB 10:20, HEB 12:24

The newness brought to believers through redemption

Believers are called by a new name

ISA 62:2 See also REV 2:17

Believers are born anew

1PE 1:3 See also JHN 3:1–3:16

A new life is made possible for believers

ACT 5:20 See also ROM 6:4; COL 3:10

Believers are a new creation

2CO 5:17 See also GAL 6:15; EPH 4:23–4:24

Believers are given a new commandment

JHN 13:34–13:35 See also 1JN 2:7–2:8

The newness of creation to come through God's redeeming action

REV 21:1–21:2 See also ISA 43:19, ISA 65:17, ISA 66:22; 2PE 3:13; REV 3:12, REV 21:5

title: 6700: peace
content: The state of harmony that is available to believers through having a right relationship with God and others and is especially associated with the presence of the Holy Spirit.

title: 6701: peace, human search for
content:
Scripture teaches that peace is only found in God, but people try to find it elsewhere. A person's desire for peace varies according to his or her circumstances.

The places where people search for peace

People search for peace in others

GEN 5:28–5:29 See also SNG 8:10

People search for peace in material possessions

ECC 4:8 See also JOB 21:7–21:13; DAN 4:4; MAT 19:16–19:22; LUK 12:16–12:19

People search for peace in God

PSA 4:8 See also JOB 22:21; PRO 19:23; ISA 26:3; LUK 7:37–7:50

The dangers in searching for peace

It can lead to greed

ECC 4:6 See also MAT 23:25

It can lead to suffering and bondage

GEN 42:1–42:17; ; JOS 9:3–9:27; ; MRK 5:25–5:26

It can lead to destruction

1TH 5:3 See also 2KI 20:12–20:19; LUK 16:19–16:26

People's search for peace varies according to their circumstances

The desire can increase in times of suffering and difficulties

EXO 2:23 See also JER 14:19, JER 47:2–47:6; LAM 3:7–3:24; EZK 7:23–7:27; MRK 5:24–5:34 parallels MAT 9:18–9:22, LUK 8:42–8:48

The desire can diminish in times of comfort

EXO 8:12–8:15 See also JOB 12:5; HOS 10:1; AMO 6:1

title: 6702: peace, human destruction of
content:
Because of human sinfulness, God's provision of peace is always under threat. Scripture shows that this breaking of peace has implications for the whole of creation.

Causes of the destruction of peace

Sin and self-centredness

ISA 57:21 See also GEN 11:4–11:9; ISA 59:7–59:8; LUK 19:41–19:44

Idolatry

ZEC 10:2 See also EXO 32:7–32:10; 1TI 6:9–6:10

Fear and anxiety

JHN 20:19 See also JOS 7:3–7:5; PRO 29:25; JER 30:10, JER 46:27; MAT 6:31–6:34 parallel LUK 12:29–12:30

The sins of ancestors

LEV 26:36–26:42 See also GEN 3:16–3:19; 2KI 23:26–23:27; JER 31:29; EZK 18:2

Friends

JER 20:10 See also LAM 1:1–1:2, LAM 1:19

Enemies

PSA 35:19–35:20 See also 2KI 9:14–9:28; LAM 1:16

The consequences of the human destruction of peace

Humanity suffers

DEU 28:53–28:57; ; 1KI 2:5–2:6; ; 2KI 6:24–6:29, 2KI 25:2–25:3; ; EZK 13:10–13:16; ; REV 6:3–6:8

Nations suffer

JER 49:31–49:32 See also EZK 38:14–38:23

The land suffers

JER 25:37 See also EXO 8:12–8:14; LEV 26:27–26:35; ISA 1:7, ISA 36:16–36:20

The whole creation suffers

REV 6:3–6:4 See also GEN 6:5–6:7; ISA 24:1–24:5; ROM 8:22; REV 8:7–8:13

title: 6703: peace, divine in OT
content:
Providing peace for his creation is a characteristic of God. In the OT peace came through adherence to God's will as expressed in his spoken word, covenants and law. The Hebrew word “shalom” means “peace in all its fulness, in every aspect of life”.

Peace provided for God's people

Through obedience to God's spoken word

GEN 2:16–2:17 See also 2KI 5:13–5:19; JOB 22:21–22:22; PSA 85:8; ISA 54:13; DAN 10:15–10:19

Through obedience to God's covenants

DEU 29:9 See also DEU 30:15–30:16; MAL 2:1–2:6

Through obedience to God's laws

PSA 119:165 See also LEV 26:3–26:12; DEU 28:1–28:7; 2CH 14:2–14:7; PSA 37:34–37:38; PRO 3:13–3:17; ISA 48:18

Peace expressed in signs

GEN 9:13–9:16 See also EXO 13:21–13:22, EXO 40:36–40:38; JDG 6:22–6:24

Peace provided for God's nation

Through provision of the land

PSA 37:11 See also 1SA 7:13–7:14; 1CH 4:40; PSA 37:3

Through provision of a holy city

ISA 33:20–33:24 See also PSA 122:6–122:8; ISA 66:10–66:13; JER 33:6–33:9; ZEC 1:16–1:17

Through provision of a house of God

HAG 2:9 See also 1CH 22:6–22:10; EZK 37:24–37:28

Through provision of the law

ISA 48:18 See also DEU 6:1–6:3, DEU 30:15–30:16

The benefits of peace

Peace and God's presence

ISA 41:10 See also NUM 6:22–6:26; JOS 1:9; PSA 23:1–23:4, PSA 29:11; JER 30:10–30:11

Peace in captivity

JER 29:7 See also EXO 1:6–1:12; JER 31:1–31:2

Peace in suffering

PSA 119:50 See also ISA 61:1–61:4; JER 34:1–34:5

Peace in poverty

ISA 14:30 See also 1SA 2:6–2:9; PSA 68:5–68:10; PRO 17:1

Peace with enemies

PRO 16:7 See also 1CH 22:7–22:9; JER 29:4–29:7

title: 6704: peace, divine in NT
content:
God's ultimate provision of peace is discovered in the person and work of Jesus Christ. It is only through Christ that peace with God can be achieved and maintained.

Provision of peace through the Father

Peace in a believer's relationship with the Father

ROM 5:1 See also ROM 8:1, ROM 8:31–8:39; 1CO 1:2–1:3

Peace through the Father's provision for the believer

1TI 6:17 See also MAT 6:25–6:34 parallel LUK 12:22–12:31; MAT 7:7–7:11 parallel LUK 11:9–11:13

Provision of peace through Jesus Christ

Through Jesus Christ's coming

LUK 2:10–2:14 ISA 9:6–9:7; ; MIC 5:2–5:5; ; ZEC 9:9–9:10; LUK 2:25–2:32; ; EPH 2:17

Through Jesus Christ's teaching

JHN 16:33 See also JHN 14:23–14:27, JHN 15:3

Through Jesus Christ's ministry

ACT 10:36 See also MRK 4:35–4:41 parallels MAT 8:23–8:27, LUK 8:22–8:25; LUK 4:33–4:35 parallel MRK 1:23–1:25; LUK 4:38–4:41 parallels MAT 8:14–8:17, MRK 1:29–1:34

Through Jesus Christ's death

COL 1:19–1:20 See also ISA 53:5; MAT 26:26–26:28 parallels MRK 14:22–14:24, LUK 22:19–22:20; GAL 6:14–6:16; EPH 2:13–2:17

Through Jesus Christ's resurrection

LUK 24:36 See also MRK 16:4–16:6 parallel LUK 24:1–24:8; JHN 20:19–20:21, JHN 20:26–20:29; 2CO 4:14; HEB 13:20–13:21

Through Jesus Christ's ascension

ROM 8:34 See also LUK 24:51–24:53; ACT 2:33–2:39

Provision of peace through the Holy Spirit

Through the Holy Spirit's inner witness

2CO 1:21–1:22 See also ROM 8:14–8:17; GAL 4:6–4:7

Through the Holy Spirit's presence

ACT 9:31 See also JHN 14:16–14:18; GAL 5:16–5:18; REV 22:17

Peace as the Holy Spirit's fruit and gift

GAL 5:22 See also ROM 14:17

title: 6705: peace, believers' experience of
content:
Peace is the birthright of every believer in all circumstances. It is found only in God and is maintained through having a close relationship with him.

Peace for believers in differing situations

In times of sickness, pressure and hardships

PSA 41:1–41:3; MAT 11:28 See also JOB 1:13–1:22, JOB 2:7–2:10; ACT 16:22–16:25; 2CO 12:7–12:10; 2TI 4:16–4:18

In times of death and grief

JHN 14:1–14:3 See also JOB 19:25–19:26; 2KI 22:18–22:20; ISA 57:1–57:2; 1TH 4:13–4:18

The effects of peace for believers

Forgiveness

ACT 7:60 See also ROM 12:17–12:19

Encouragement

2CO 1:3–1:6 See also PHP 4:11–4:13

Health and healing

PRO 14:30 See also ISA 57:18–57:19

Security

PRO 1:33 See also ACT 27:21–27:26; ROM 8:28, ROM 8:35–8:39

Hope

ROM 15:13 See also ROM 5:1–5:5

How believers maintain peace

Through remaining in Christ

JHN 15:4–15:7 See also JHN 16:33; ROM 5:1–5:5

Through living by the Holy Spirit

ROM 8:6 See also ROM 14:17–14:19; GAL 5:22

Through obedience to God's word

JOS 1:8–1:9 See also PSA 119:165–119:167

Through prayer and meditation

PHP 4:6–4:9 See also PSA 1:1–1:3; ISA 26:3; 1TI 2:1–2:2

Final peace in death for believers

ISA 57:2; REV 14:13 See also 2KI 22:19–22:20; LUK 2:29

Hope of future peace for believers

Peace in heaven

1PE 1:4 See also JHN 14:1–14:3; 1TH 4:13–4:14; REV 7:9–7:17

Peace in God's new creation

REV 21:1–21:4 See also ISA 11:6–11:9; ROM 8:18–8:23; REV 22:3–22:5

title: 6706: peace, and Holy Spirit
content: See 3215: Holy Spirit, and peace

title: 6708: predestination
content:
God's foreordination of all events and circumstances for the good of his people and the glory of his name.

Predestination depends on God's sovereignty

He is the Creator of all things

JER 32:17–32:19 See also GEN 1:1; JOB 38:1–38:4; ISA 44:24–44:28, ISA 45:12–45:13, ISA 48:12–48:14

He rules over nature and history

PRO 16:4 See also GEN 18:10–18:14; PSA 67:4; PRO 16:9, PRO 16:33, PRO 21:1; MAT 8:23–8:27 parallels MRK 4:35–4:41, LUK 8:22–8:25; MAT 10:29–10:30; ACT 17:26

His will is perfect

GEN 18:25 See also EXO 33:19; DEU 32:4; JOB 8:3; PSA 119:137; DAN 4:37

God's predestined purposes cannot be thwarted

Human beings cannot stand against his will

ROM 9:19–9:21 See also PSA 2:1–2:4; PRO 19:21, PRO 21:30; ISA 14:24–14:27, ISA 46:10–46:12; DAN 4:35

False gods are impotent before him

PSA 115:3–115:8 See also ISA 44:8–44:20, ISA 45:20, ISA 48:14

He brings good out of evil

GEN 50:20 See also GEN 15:13–15:16, GEN 45:4–45:8

Predestination undergirds biblical prophecy

It explains the prophets' confidence

1KI 22:17–22:28 See also 1PE 1:10–1:12

It is demonstrated in the fulfilment of prophecy

1KI 22:29–22:38 See also MAT 1:22, MAT 2:15, MAT 2:23, MAT 4:14, MAT 8:17, MAT 12:17–12:19; ACT 2:17–2:25, ACT 3:22–3:25, ACT 13:27–13:30, ACT 15:15–15:18

The life and ministry of Jesus Christ was predestined

In that it was predicted by the prophets

ISA 9:6–9:7 See also ISA 11:1–11:10, ISA 52:13–53:12; JER 23:5–23:6; ACT 3:18; GAL 4:4–4:5

In his sufferings and death

ACT 2:23 See also MAT 16:21; LUK 18:31–18:32, LUK 22:22, LUK 24:25–24:27, LUK 24:44–24:45; JHN 13:1; ACT 4:27–4:30

In the outcome it achieved

GEN 3:15

God's purpose in predestination is to bless his people

In the path their lives follow

ROM 8:28 See also PSA 139:14–139:16; JER 29:11; MAT 10:29–10:31 parallel LUK 12:6–12:7

In their salvation

ROM 8:29–8:30 See also GEN 12:1–12:3; MAT 11:25–11:27; MRK 4:11; JHN 6:37–6:44; ACT 13:48; 1TH 5:9

In their assurance of salvation

ROM 8:31–8:39 See also JHN 10:27–10:29, JHN 17:2; EPH 1:3–1:14 Predestination is basic to Christian certainty.

Regarding final perseverance

PHP 1:6 See also PSA 138:8; JHN 6:37–6:40; PHP 2:12–2:13; JAS 5:11

Regarding God's call to righteousness

EPH 2:10 See also JER 1:4–1:5; ACT 22:10; GAL 1:15–1:17

Regarding future inheritance

JHN 14:2–14:3 See also MAT 25:34; ROM 8:30

God predestines judgment

ISA 65:11–65:12 See also GEN 6:17 the flood; EXO 7:13, EXO 9:13–9:18; JOS 11:20; 2KI 19:25–19:26; ISA 14:24–14:27 judgment on Assyria; ISA 19:12–19:14, ISA 23:9 judgment on Tyre; JER 49:20, JER 50:45 judgment on Babylon; 1PE 2:6–2:8; ISA 8:14–8:15

Predestination does not set aside human responsibility

PHP 2:12–2:13 See also JHN 6:37, JHN 6:40; ACT 13:48

title: 6710: privileges
content:
Spiritual and material benefits conferred upon people by God. They are given, not because of human merit, but because of God's graciousness, and therefore carry responsibilities with them.

Privileges as the gift of God, not the result of human merit

1CO 4:7 See also LUK 1:26–1:38; EPH 2:8–2:9; PHP 3:4–3:9

The privileged position of humanity in creation

PSA 8:3–8:6 See also GEN 1:26–1:28

The privileged position of Israel

ROM 9:4–9:5 See also DEU 7:7–7:9; 2SA 7:22–7:24; ROM 11:1–11:12

The privileged position of the believer

The privilege of forgiveness and new life

EPH 1:3–1:8 See also 2CO 5:17–5:21; EPH 2:3–2:9; COL 1:13–1:14

The privilege of access to God

EPH 2:18 See also ROM 5:1–5:2; HEB 10:19–10:22, HEB 12:18–12:24

The privilege of knowing God

JHN 17:3 See also JHN 12:44–12:46, JHN 14:6–14:7

The privilege of being God's children

GAL 4:4–4:7 See also ROM 8:14–8:17; GAL 3:26–3:29; HEB 12:5–12:11

The privilege of becoming God's people

1PE 2:9–2:10 See also EPH 2:19–2:22

The privilege of receiving divine insights

LUK 10:23–10:24 See also MAT 13:10–13:17 parallels MRK 4:10–4:12, LUK 8:9–8:10; JHN 15:15; 1CO 2:6–2:12; 1PE 1:10–1:12

The privilege of being called to serve God

JER 1:5 See also 2SA 7:18–7:21; JHN 15:15–15:16; 1CO 15:8–15:10

The privilege of giving

1CH 29:14–29:16 See also ACT 20:33–20:35; 2CO 8:1–8:15, 2CO 9:6–9:11

Privileges bring responsibilities

1CO 4:1–4:2 See also MAT 24:45–24:51 parallel LUK 12:42–12:48; 1CO 6:19–6:20; HEB 13:17

Privileges can be abused

1SA 2:12–2:17 Eli's sons; ; 2SA 11:1–11:4 King David; ; 1KI 21:1–21:16 King Ahab; ; ACT 16:35–16:39 magistrates in Philippi; ; ACT 23:1–23:3 Ananias, the high priest

Privileges can be withdrawn

MAT 21:33–21:43 parallels MRK 12:1–12:9, LUK 20:9–20:16 parable of the tenants; ; MAT 25:14–25:30 parable of the talents; ; LUK 19:12–19:27 parable of the ten minas

title: 6712: propitiation
content:
The satisfaction of the righteous demands of God in relation to human sin and its punishment through the sacrificial death of Jesus Christ upon the cross, by which the penalty of sin is cancelled and the anger of God averted. [The NIV is distinctive at this point, in that it generally translates this term by “atonement” and related words.].

The need for propitiation: God's anger against sin

PSA 7:11; ROM 2:5 See also EXO 32:11–32:14; NUM 32:8–32:15; DEU 6:14–6:15; 2KI 23:26; PSA 78:38; ISA 30:27–30:31; DAN 9:16–9:19; HOS 11:8–11:9; MAT 25:41–25:46; JHN 3:36; ROM 1:18; EPH 5:6

The provision of propitiation: Jesus Christ the atoning sacrifice

The promise in the OT

ISA 53:5–53:6 See also ISA 53:10–53:12

The fulfilment in the NT

ROM 3:21–3:26 See also ROM 5:9–5:10; COL 1:21–1:22; HEB 2:17, HEB 9:11–9:14; 1JN 2:2

The motivation for propitiation: God's love

1JN 4:10 See also PSA 85:2–85:3, PSA 103:8–103:12; MIC 7:18–7:19; ROM 5:6–5:8; 2CO 5:19

title: 6714: ransom
content:
The price paid for redemption, that is, for setting something or someone free from some form of obligation or captivity.

Ransom paid for what has been devoted to the Lord

EXO 13:12–13:13, EXO 30:11–30:16, EXO 34:20; ; LEV 27:1–27:33; ; NUM 3:40–3:51, NUM 18:14–18:17

Ransom of a person's life instead of punishment

EXO 21:28–21:32

Ransom from poverty and misfortune, including widowhood

See also LEV 25:25–25:28, LEV 25:47–25:55; RUT 4:1–4:13; PRO 13:8

Limitations on ransom payments

NUM 35:31–35:32 See also LEV 27:29

Paying a ransom to God

PSA 49:7–49:8 See also EXO 30:12–30:16; JOB 33:24

Promises of redemption for the Lord's ransomed people

ISA 51:11 parallel ISA 35:10 See also ISA 40:2, ISA 43:3–43:5; JER 31:11; HOS 13:14

The death of Jesus Christ seen as a ransom

MRK 10:45 parallel MAT 20:28 See also ACT 20:28; 1TI 2:5–2:6; TIT 2:14; HEB 9:15; 2PE 2:1

The priceless value of the ransom of Jesus Christ

1CO 6:19–6:20 See also 1CO 7:23; 1PE 1:18–1:19; REV 5:9

title: 6716: reconciliation
content: The restoration of fellowship between God and humanity and the resulting restoration of human relationships. The NT affirms that the reconciliation of the world to God is only possible on the basis of the work of Jesus Christ.

resource.label: 6717: reconciliation, of the world to God

title: 6717: reconciliation, of the world to God
content:
On account of sin, people are alienated from God and cut off from fellowship with him. Through Jesus Christ, God reconciles the world to himself, breaking down the barriers of hostility and estrangement.

A broken relationship through sin brings alienation from God

ISA 59:2 See also GEN 3:23–3:24 the expulsion from Eden; GEN 4:13–4:14 Cain's alienation from God; ISA 48:22, ISA 64:7; JER 33:5; LUK 18:13 the tax collector's prayer for mercy; ROM 5:10, ROM 8:7; EPH 2:1–2:3, EPH 2:12, EPH 4:18; COL 1:21; JAS 4:4

God takes the initiative in bringing about reconciliation

2CO 5:18–5:19 See also ROM 5:6–5:8; GAL 4:4–4:5; EPH 2:4–2:5; 1JN 4:10

The means of reconciliation is the death of Jesus Christ

ROM 5:6 See also 2CO 5:18–5:19, 2CO 5:21; EPH 2:13, EPH 2:16; COL 1:20

The results of reconciliation are both personal and universal

Peace with God

ROM 5:1 See also ACT 10:36–10:46; EPH 2:14–2:19; COL 1:21–1:22

Access to God

ROM 5:2 See also EPH 2:18, EPH 3:12; HEB 10:19–10:22

Adoption as God's children

ROM 8:15–8:16; ; GAL 3:26, GAL 4:4–4:6; ; 1JN 3:1–3:2

Peacemaking throughout the universe

COL 1:20 See also ROM 11:15; EPH 1:7–1:10, EPH 1:22–1:23

Believers are to be the ambassadors of reconciliation

2CO 5:18–5:20

title: 6718: reconciliation, between believers
content:
True reconciliation between people is only possible after they have been reconciled to God through Jesus Christ. Believers are urged to settle differences among themselves in brotherly love.

The cause of the breakdown in relationships is sin

GEN 27:41 GEN 37:4–37:5, GEN 37:18–37:20; 1SA 15:12–15:14 Samuel and Saul; ; 2SA 14:28 Absalom and David; ; ACT 15:37–15:40 Paul and Barnabas; ; GAL 2:11 Paul opposes Peter

Believers should be reconciled to one another

MAT 5:23–5:24 See also MAT 5:9, MAT 5:25 parallel LUK 12:58; MAT 5:44, MAT 18:15–18:17, MAT 18:21–18:35; JHN 17:20–17:23; ROM 12:18–12:21; 2TH 3:14–3:15

The death of Jesus Christ should bring believers together in peace

EPH 2:14–2:22

The church should display reconciliation

COL 3:12–3:15 See also ROM 12:18–12:21; EPH 4:32; 2TH 3:14–3:15

Examples of reconciliation between people

GEN 33:4 See also GEN 45:1–45:5 Joseph and his brothers; JOS 22:10–22:34 Israel, Reuben and Gad; LUK 23:12 Herod and Pilate; JHN 21:15–21:17 Jesus Christ and Peter; ACT 9:26–9:28 Paul and the apostles; 1CO 7:11 a wife and her husband

title: 6720: redemption
content: The buying back or release of an object or person. In Scripture redemption refers to God's ransoming of believers only through the death of Jesus Christ upon the cross and to all the benefits that this brings.

title: 6721: redemption, in everyday life
content:
The purchase of a person's freedom or the buying back of an object from the possession of another. Scripture provides illustrations of these everyday meanings of the word.

The OT redemption of property, animals and individuals

Redemption of property

LEV 25:24–25:28 See also LEV 27:15–27:20; RUT 4:1–4:6; JER 32:8

Redemption of animals

EXO 13:13 See also EXO 34:20; LEV 27:13, LEV 27:27; NUM 18:14–18:17

Redemption of individuals

EXO 30:12–30:16 See also EXO 13:12–13:13, EXO 21:8, EXO 21:28–21:32, EXO 34:19–34:20; LEV 25:47–25:55; NUM 3:44–3:51

The redemption of the nation of Israel

EXO 6:6 See also DEU 9:26; 2SA 7:23–7:24 parallel 1CH 17:21–17:22; NEH 1:10; PSA 77:15, PSA 78:35, PSA 106:10; ISA 43:1–43:3; MIC 6:4

Redemption as release from sin

PSA 130:8 See also ISA 40:2

The role of the redeemer

In helping close relatives regain property or freedom

LEV 25:25 See also LEV 25:47–25:49, LEV 27:15–27:20; RUT 2:20, RUT 3:9, RUT 4:1–4:8

In avenging death

NUM 35:12, NUM 35:19–35:21

title: 6722: redemption, in OT
content:
The act of God by which he delivered his people from bondage. The exodus of Israel from Egypt and the later deliverance of Jerusalem from exile in Babylon are seen as definitive examples of God's redeeming acts.

God is the Redeemer of his people

God redeems his people from captivity out of love

DEU 7:8 See also EXO 6:6–6:8, EXO 15:13; PSA 130:7–130:8; ISA 43:14, ISA 47:1–47:4, ISA 63:16; JER 15:21, JER 31:11, JER 50:34

His redemption guarantees security

ISA 43:1–43:4 See also ISA 44:21–44:28, ISA 48:20, ISA 49:7, ISA 49:22–49:26, ISA 52:9–52:12

He redeems from death

PSA 49:15 See also JOB 5:20, JOB 19:25–19:26; HOS 13:14

His redemption is associated with his holiness

ISA 41:14 See also ISA 43:14, ISA 47:4, ISA 48:17, ISA 49:7, ISA 54:5

He redeems by his mighty arm

LUK 1:51 See also DEU 11:2; PSA 89:10, PSA 77:15; ISA 62:8

title: 6723: redemption, in NT
content:
The culmination of the OT work of redemption is seen in the cross of Jesus Christ, by which believers are liberated from bondage to sin.

Redemption is achieved through Jesus Christ

EPH 1:7

Jesus Christ's teaching on redemption

MRK 10:45 parallel MAT 20:28 See also MAT 26:26–26:28 parallels MRK 14:22–14:25, LUK 22:20–22:22

Apostolic teaching on redemption

Jesus Christ is the redemption of believers

1CO 1:30 See also 1TI 2:6; TIT 2:14

Redemption comes through the shedding of the blood of Jesus Christ

GAL 3:13 See also ACT 20:28; ROM 3:25; 1CO 6:20, 1CO 7:23, 1CO 11:23–11:25; 1PE 1:18–1:19; HEB 9:12; REV 1:5–1:6, REV 5:9

The results of redemption

Forgiveness of sin

COL 1:13–1:14

Justification and freedom from the law

GAL 3:8–3:10 See also ROM 3:23–3:25; GAL 4:5

Inclusion in the covenant

GAL 3:14 See also HEB 9:15

Freedom to live a new life

GAL 4:4–4:7 See also 1CO 6:19–6:20, 1CO 7:22–7:24; COL 1:13; TIT 2:14; HEB 9:14; REV 1:5–1:6, REV 5:9–5:10

Creation and believers await final redemption

ROM 8:19–8:23 See also LUK 21:28; ACT 3:21; EPH 1:14, EPH 4:30

resource.label: 9414: heaven, as community of the redeemed

title: 6724: redeemer, God as
content: See 1315: God, as redeemer

title: 6725: redeemer, Jesus Christ as
content: See 2321: Jesus Christ, as redeemer

title: 6728: regeneration
content:
The radical renewal of a person's inner being by the work of God's Spirit.

The need for regeneration

JHN 3:3 See also EPH 2:1, EPH 2:5; COL 2:13

Regeneration is a work of God

It originates in God the Father

JHN 1:12–1:13 Regeneration cannot be controlled by human actions or rituals; it is an act of God's sovereign will.

It is made possible by the resurrection of Jesus Christ

1PE 1:3 See also EPH 2:4–2:5

It occurs through the hearing of the Christian gospel

JAS 1:18 See also 1PE 1:23–1:25 The “word of God” is the good news about Jesus Christ.

It is effected by God's Spirit

JHN 3:5–3:8 See also JHN 6:63; TIT 3:5

Regeneration is given to those who believe in Jesus Christ

1JN 5:1

Baptism is the sign of regeneration

JHN 3:5 Water here is seen by some, though not all, interpreters as a reference to baptism, administered by the church to new converts as a sign of their new birth and their entry into the Christian community. See also ACT 2:38–2:39; EPH 5:25–5:26; TIT 3:5

The results of regeneration

Entry into God's kingdom

JHN 3:5

A new holiness of life

1JN 3:9 See also 1JN 5:18; 1PE 2:1–2:2

Love for other people

1JN 4:7 See also 1JN 5:2

Victory over the world's sinful pattern of life

1JN 5:4

title: 6730: reinstatement
content:
Restoration to a previous state or position. It is demonstrated in human relationships and also by God in his love and grace restoring fallen humanity and lifting up his people to their former place within his service.

The basis of reinstatement

God does not remember sin

See also PSA 103:12; ISA 38:17, ISA 43:25; EZK 20:44

God's unfailing love

PSA 89:28; ; LAM 3:22; ; HOS 11:8

God's reinstatement of fallen humanity

Humanity's unique calling

HEB 2:6–2:8 See also PSA 8:4–8:6; GEN 1:26–1:27; ISA 43:7

Humanity's fall and reinstatement

ROM 3:23–3:24; 2TH 2:14 See also ROM 5:15–5:19, ROM 8:17; EPH 1:12; HEB 2:10; 1PE 5:1

God's reinstatement of Israel

The promise of reinstatement

MIC 4:8 See also ISA 1:26, ISA 60:10; HAG 2:9; ZEC 1:17, ZEC 10:6–10:8

Reinstatement as God's people

HOS 2:23 See also ROM 9:25; 1PE 2:10

Reinstatement as God's bride

ISA 54:5–54:8 See also JER 3:12–3:14; HOS 2:14–2:20

Reinstatement in the land

JER 33:10–33:16 See also 2CH 30:9; JER 31:16–31:17; HOS 11:10–11:11

God's reinstatement of his servants

JDG 16:22 Samson's hair begins to grow again, indicating his renewed call to serve God; ; JER 15:19 God will continue to speak through a repentant Jeremiah; ; DAN 4:34–4:36 Nebuchadnezzar's sanity and kingdom is restored; ; JON 3:1–3:2 God renews Jonah's commission; ; JHN 21:15–21:17 Jesus Christ reinstates Peter after his denial.

Further examples of reinstatement

GEN 40:13 Pharaoh's cupbearer reinstated; ; 1SA 19:6–19:7 David reinstated by Saul; ; 2SA 9:7 Mephibosheth reinstated by David; ; 2SA 14:33 Absalom reinstated by David; ; DAN 3:30 Nebuchadnezzar reinstates Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego; ; HOS 3:2–3:3 Gomer reinstated as Hosea's wife; ; LUK 15:22–15:24 the prodigal reinstated by his father; ; PHM 1:10 Paul asks Philemon to reinstate his slave Onesimus; ; PHM 1:24 John Mark, formerly rejected, has here been reinstated as Paul's companion.

title: 6732: repentance
content: A change of mind leading to a change of action. It involves a sincere turning from sin to serve God and includes sorrow for, and confession of, sin and where possible restitution. At points, Scripture refers to God changing his plans in response to events.

title: 6733: repentance, nature of
content:
Scripture stresses the necessity of repentance from sin if individuals and communities are to have full fellowship with God. It also uses the term to refer to God's relenting of sending judgment on his people, usually in response to human repentance.

Repentance is a requirement for fellowship with God

2KI 17:13; 1TH 1:9 See also PSA 34:14; ISA 55:7; ACT 14:15; JAS 4:7–4:10

Repentance involves turning from sin

Sorrow for sin

PSA 51:17; 2CO 7:8–7:10 See also JOB 42:6; PSA 34:18; ISA 57:15, ISA 66:2; JOL 2:12–2:13; LUK 18:13

Confession of sin

LUK 15:17–15:19 See also LEV 5:5; PSA 51:1–51:3; PRO 28:13; HOS 14:1–14:2

Forsaking specific sins

EZR 10:10–10:11; ; EZK 14:6; ; ACT 15:19–15:20

Making appropriate restitution

NUM 5:6–5:7; ; LUK 19:8

Repentance involves turning to God

Faith in God

ISA 30:15 See also LUK 22:32; ACT 11:21, ACT 20:21, ACT 26:18

Obedience

EZK 18:21–18:23 See also MAL 3:7–3:10

Repentance demonstrated by actions

ACT 26:20 See also ISA 1:16–1:17; DAN 4:27; MAT 3:8 parallel LUK 3:8; LUK 3:10–3:14

Repentance must be sincere

JER 3:10, JER 24:7 See also 1KI 8:46–8:50 parallel 2CH 6:36–6:39; PSA 78:34–78:37; HOS 6:1–6:4

The repentance of God

JER 26:3 See also EXO 32:14; PSA 106:45; HOS 11:8; JOL 2:13; AMO 7:1–7:6

title: 6734: repentance, importance of
content:
Repentance is of central importance because sin brings God's judgment and fellowship with God is only possible through full and sincere repentance. God, through his servants, calls people to repent as the only way to escape the judgment and receive the forgiveness and restoration which he offers.

The call to repentance

LUK 5:32; JAS 5:19–5:20 See also JER 25:4–25:6; EZK 33:7–33:9; MRK 1:4 parallel LUK 3:3; LUK 24:47; 2TI 2:24–2:26

Repentance opens the way for blessing

It is the only way to escape God's judgment

EZK 18:30–18:32 See also JOB 36:12; JER 18:7–18:8, JER 26:3; HOS 11:5; JON 3:10; LUK 3:8–3:9; REV 2:5

It prepares the way for God's kingdom

MAT 4:17 parallel MRK 1:14–1:15 See also MAT 3:2

It brings forgiveness and restoration

2CH 7:13–7:14; ISA 55:7 See also DEU 30:1–30:10; NEH 1:8–1:9; JOB 22:23–22:25, JOB 36:10–36:11; ISA 44:22; ACT 2:38–2:39, ACT 3:19, ACT 5:31, ACT 11:18

God desires that all people should repent

He wants everyone to be saved

EZK 18:23

His patience with the unrepentant

2PE 3:9 See also ISA 65:2; ROM 2:4; REV 2:21

His discipline encourages repentance

JER 31:18–31:20 See also ISA 10:20–10:21, ISA 19:22; HOS 2:6–2:7, HOS 6:1

Taking God's opportunity for repentance

ISA 55:6; ACT 17:30–17:31 See also HEB 3:13–3:15, HEB 4:7; PSA 95:7–95:8

Refusing God's opportunity for repentance

Examples of those who refuse to repent

JER 35:15 See also JER 5:3; MAT 11:20, MAT 21:32; REV 9:20–9:21, REV 16:9–16:11

God confirms those who refuse to repent in their hardness of heart

MAT 13:14–13:15 parallels MRK 4:11–4:12, LUK 8:9–8:10; ; ACT 28:25–28:27; ; ISA 6:10

Repentance may not remove the effects of human sin

NUM 14:39–14:45 The Israelites' repentance could not prevent the wandering that resulted from their refusal to enter the promised land; ; 1SA 15:24–15:26 Saul's repentance is too late to escape God's judgment on his kingship; ; 2SA 12:13–12:14 Despite David's repentance the son of his adulterous relationship with Bathsheba still died; ; HEB 12:16–12:17 Esau's repentance could not bring back the birthright he had sold to Jacob.

title: 6735: repentance, examples of
content:
Scripture provides examples to illustrate the importance of repentance for individuals and communities.

The repentance of individuals

The call to personal repentance

ACT 2:38 See also 2TI 2:19

Examples of individual repentance

NUM 22:31–22:35 Balaam; ; 2SA 24:10 parallel 1CH 21:8 David, after taking a census; ; 1KI 21:27–21:29 Ahab; ; 2KI 22:19 Josiah; ; JOB 42:6 Job; ; PSA 51:1–51:17 David, following his adultery with Bathsheba; ; LUK 15:21 the prodigal son; ; LUK 18:13 the tax collector in the temple; ; MAT 26:75 parallels MRK 14:72, LUK 22:61–22:62 Peter's sorrow after denying Jesus Christ; ; JHN 21:15–21:17 Peter's confession and reinstatement; ; ACT 8:22–8:24 Simon the sorcerer's repentance may not have been genuine.

Corporate repentance

Examples of corporate repentance

JER 18:7–18:8; MAT 3:1–3:6 parallels MRK 1:1–1:6, LUK 3:1–3:6 See also NUM 21:7 Israel in the desert; JDG 10:15–10:16 Israel in the time of the judges; 1SA 7:3–7:4 Israel, after asking for a king; ISA 19:22 Egypt turns to God in repentance; ACT 9:32–9:35 the people of Lydda and Sharon

Leaders encouraged corporate repentance

EZR 10:1 See also 2KI 23:1–23:7 Josiah leads Judah in repentance; 2CH 15:8–15:15 Asa leads Judah in repentance; 2CH 30:6–30:9 Hezekiah leads Israel and Judah in repentance; EZR 10:10–10:12 Following the exile, Ezra leads the Israelites in repentance; JON 3:6–3:8 The king of Nineveh leads the people in repentance.

Corporate repentance within the church

REV 2:4–2:5 See also 2CO 7:9–7:11; REV 2:14–2:16, REV 2:20–2:22, REV 3:3, REV 3:19–3:20

Symbols of repentance

1KI 21:27; JON 3:5 See also 1SA 7:6; EZR 8:21; NEH 9:1; JER 36:9; JOL 1:13–1:14, JOL 2:12

title: 6736: repentance, of God
content: See 1120: God, repentance of

title: 6738: rescue
content:
Deliverance from people or forces that are overwhelming. It implies a liberator with strength and wisdom to ensure true freedom. God's physical rescue of the Israelites points to the spiritual deliverance obtained by Jesus Christ.

When rescue is the only answer

PSA 35:10 See also DEU 22:27, DEU 28:29; 1SA 12:21; ISA 42:22; DAN 8:4, DAN 8:7; HOS 5:14; MIC 5:8; ROM 7:24

God's power to rescue in times of need

ISA 41:10; ; 1CO 10:13; ; 2CO 12:9–12:10; ; HEB 13:6

God's people cry to him for rescue

JDG 10:15 See also 1SA 7:8; PSA 22:11, PSA 35:17, PSA 43:1, PSA 69:13–69:14, PSA 71:2, PSA 107:19, PSA 140:1, PSA 142:6, PSA 143:9, PSA 144:7; PRO 24:11; ROM 15:31 Paul's need for protection

God's physical rescue of his people

From their enemies

EXO 3:8 See also JDG 6:14EXO 18:10; 1SA 11:13; 2SA 22:17–22:20 parallel PSA 18:16–18:19; 2KI 13:5; 1CH 11:14; 2CH 20:15, 2CH 32:22; NEH 9:27; EST 4:14; JER 20:13 2CH 20:29 Israel's enemies recognise God's rescue of his people.

From trouble

DAN 6:27 See also GEN 45:7; JOB 5:19; PSA 32:7, PSA 91:14–91:15, PSA 126:1; PRO 11:8; JOL 2:32; JON 2:10; 2PE 2:9

From oppression

PSA 72:12–72:14, PSA 82:3–82:4

God's spiritual rescue of believers

From the guilt, penalty and power of sin

ISA 61:1 See also LUK 4:18; JER 29:11–29:14; ROM 8:21; 1TH 1:10; JUD 1:24

From the dominion of Satan

COL 1:13 See also MAT 6:13; LUK 1:74, LUK 9:1, LUK 11:21–11:22, LUK 13:16

From spiritual attack

2TI 4:18

From fear and the fear of death

HEB 2:14–2:15 See also PSA 34:4; 1CO 15:54–15:57; 2CO 1:10; REV 2:10

Rescuers sent by God

ACT 7:34 Moses; JDG 3:9, JDG 3:15, JDG 10:1

Examples of people rescued

ACT 7:10 Joseph; ; JER 38:6–38:13 Jeremiah; DAN 3:17, DAN 3:28, DAN 6:21–6:23, DAN 6:27; ACT 12:11 Peter; ACT 23:27, ACT 26:17; ; 2TI 3:11

title: 6740: returning to God
content:
Scripture declares that all humanity has turned away from God, and must return to him. The term “repentance” is often used to refer to this process of returning to God and the inward and outward changes that it demands of people. Scripture also indicates that believers can wander away from God; they too must return to him.

All people have turned away from God

ISA 53:6, ISA 64:6 See also JOB 25:4; JER 17:9; JHN 3:19; ROM 3:23

Wayward people must return to God

ISA 44:22; HOS 14:1; ZEC 1:3 See also 2CH 30:9; NEH 1:9; JOB 22:23; JER 24:7; LAM 3:40; HOS 6:1, HOS 12:6; JOL 2:12

Means of returning to God

Realisation of estrangement from God

LAM 3:40 See also 1SA 24:5; 2SA 24:10; 2CH 6:29–6:31; ROM 3:20

Acknowledgment of sin

PSA 51:3 See also EXO 9:27; NUM 14:39–14:40, NUM 22:34; JOS 7:20; 1SA 15:24–15:25; MAT 27:3–27:4

Sorrow for sin

PSA 38:18 See also PSA 51:1–51:2; 2CO 7:9–7:10

A sense of guilt for sin

2SA 24:10 See also EZR 9:5–9:6; PSA 38:1–38:4, PSA 38:17–38:18, PSA 51:3; ACT 2:37–2:38

Repentance

ISA 1:27; ACT 2:37–2:38 See also ISA 59:20

A parable of returning to God

LUK 15:11–15:24

Examples of people returning to God

Israelites: JDG 10:15; ; 2CH 15:4; 2SA 12:13–12:14 David; ; JON 3:5–3:10 the people of Nineveh

resource.label: 6717: reconciliation, of the world to God

title: 6742: sackcloth and ashes
content:
Sackcloth is a coarse, black cloth made from goat's hair that was worn together with the burnt ashes of wood as a sign of mourning for personal and national disaster, as a sign of repentance and at times of prayer for deliverance.

Sackcloth and ashes worn as a sign of mourning for the dead

By Jacob for Joseph

GEN 37:34

By David, Joab and the people for Abner

See also 2SA 3:31

Sackcloth and ashes worn as a sign of mourning for personal or national disaster

JOB 16:15, JOB 42:6 See also EST 4:1; LAM 2:10; JOL 1:8

Sackcloth and ashes worn as a sign of repentance for sin

By Ahab

1KI 21:27

By David and the elders

See also 1CH 21:16

By the Israelites

NEH 9:1

By the Ninevites and their king

See also JON 3:5–3:9 Even the animals were to wear sackcloth.

By others

ISA 15:3; ; JER 49:3; ; EZK 27:31; ; MAT 11:21 parallel LUK 10:13

Sackcloth and ashes worn at times of prayer for deliverance

By Hezekiah and his companions

2KI 19:1–19:2

By Daniel

DAN 9:3

Sackcloth was worn in many different ways

See also 1KI 20:31; 2KI 6:30; ISA 3:24, ISA 58:5; JOL 1:13

Sackcloth used figuratively

ISA 50:3

title: 6744: sanctification
content: The process of becoming consecrated to God, which is an integral aspect of being a member of the people of God. This process of being made holy through the work of the Holy Spirit ultimately rests upon the sacrificial death of Jesus Christ, which the OT anticipates and foreshadows.

title: 6745: sanctification, nature and basis of
content:
The process of renewal and consecration by which believers are made holy through the work of the Holy Spirit. Sanctification is the consequence of justification and is dependent upon a person being in a right relationship with God.

Sanctification is grounded in the holiness of God

God is holy

EZK 39:7 See also LEV 22:32; JOS 24:19; PSA 30:4; HOS 11:9; ISA 6:3; REV 6:10

God demands that his people should reflect his holiness

LEV 19:2 See also LEV 11:44–11:45, LEV 20:7–20:8; HEB 2:11; 1PE 1:15–1:16

Sanctification is the will of God for his people

1TH 4:3 See also EPH 1:4, EPH 2:10; 2TH 2:13; 1PE 1:1–1:2

The basis for sanctification

God's election of his people

1CO 1:2; ; EPH 1:4–1:11; ; 1TH 5:9

The atoning death of Jesus Christ

HEB 13:12 See also ROM 6:11, ROM 7:4, ROM 8:2; 1CO 1:30, 1CO 6:11; EPH 5:25–5:27; HEB 10:10–10:14; 1PE 2:5

The grace of God

LUK 1:69–1:75; ; PHP 2:13; ; 2TI 1:9; ; HEB 12:10

The work of the Holy Spirit

ROM 15:16; ; 2TH 2:13; ; 1PE 1:2

The word of God

JHN 17:17; ; EPH 5:25–5:26; ; 2TI 3:16

The need for sanctification

The universal sinfulness of humanity

ISA 64:6 See also JOB 15:14–15:15; PSA 51:5; ROM 5:12–5:19; EPH 2:3

Enslavement to evil can only be broken through the death of Jesus Christ

JHN 8:34–8:36 See also ROM 6:16–6:18, ROM 8:5–8:7; EPH 4:17–4:24

The need for renewal and growth

2PE 3:18 See also ROM 12:1–12:2; COL 1:10; 1TH 4:3–4:6; HEB 6:1–6:3

The nature of sanctification

A process which has already been initiated

1CO 1:2, 1CO 6:11

A process of growth in holiness

ROM 12:1–12:3; ; 2CO 3:18; ; EPH 4:15; ; 1TH 4:3–4:7; ; HEB 12:14; ; 1PE 2:1–2:3; ; 2PE 3:18

Consecration to God

EXO 32:29; ; 1CH 29:5; ; PRO 23:26; ; ROM 12:1

title: 6746: sanctification, means and results of
content:
Sanctification results from the renewing work of the Holy Spirit and leads to the renewal of believers and their being equipped for ministry in the world.

The means of sanctification

The work of the Holy Spirit

1CO 6:11 See also ROM 8:9–8:11, ROM 15:15–15:16; 1CO 12:13; 2CO 1:21–1:22; EPH 1:13–1:14; 2TH 2:13; TIT 3:4–3:7; 1PE 1:1–1:2

Meditation on the Scriptures

1PE 2:2–2:3 See also DEU 11:18; PSA 119:12–119:18, PSA 119:48, PSA 143:5–143:6; JHN 17:17; COL 3:16; JAS 1:25

The active pursuit of holiness and righteousness

1TI 6:11–6:12 See also 2CO 7:1; GAL 5:24; EPH 4:1; 1TH 5:22; 1PE 2:9–2:12; 3JN 1:11

Obedience and self-denial

ROM 6:19–6:22, ROM 8:5–8:14; ; GAL 2:20, GAL 5:16–5:24; ; 1PE 2:11

Prayer

PSA 145:18 See also MAT 7:7–7:8; ACT 4:31; 1TI 4:4; JAS 5:16; JUD 1:20

Confession of sin

1JN 1:9 See also NEH 1:6–1:9; PSA 32:5, PSA 40:11–40:12; PRO 28:13; ISA 64:5–64:7; JER 14:20–14:22; LAM 3:40

Obstacles to sanctification

A lack of faith

MAT 5:13; ; JHN 15:6; ; 2CO 12:20–12:21; ; 1TI 1:18–1:19

Rebellion against God

EZK 18:24 See also DEU 32:15–32:18; JOB 34:26; ISA 65:11–65:12; GAL 1:6–1:7, GAL 5:7–5:9; HEB 12:15; REV 2:4–2:5

Satanic temptation

1PE 5:8–5:9 See also ACT 5:3; 2CO 2:8–2:11; JAS 4:7

Self-indulgence and greed

LUK 12:15 See also LUK 21:34; ROM 13:13; 2CO 12:21; EPH 4:19

Yielding to sinful desires

1PE 1:14 See also MRK 4:18–4:19; 1CO 10:6–10:8; 1PE 2:11; 2PE 2:14–2:18; 1JN 2:16–2:17

The results of sanctification

Good works

2CO 9:8 See also EPH 2:10; COL 1:10, COL 3:15–3:17; 2TH 2:16–2:17; HEB 10:24–10:25; JAS 2:14–2:26

Becoming like Jesus Christ

1PE 2:21 See also JHN 13:15; ROM 8:28–8:30; 1CO 11:1; 2CO 3:18; GAL 3:27; 1JN 3:2–3:3

Becoming like God

MAT 5:48; ; EPH 5:1–5:2; ; COL 1:21–1:22

Perfection

MAT 5:48 See also 2CO 13:11; COL 1:28

Blamelessness in the sight of God

2PE 3:14 See also EPH 1:4; COL 1:21–1:22; 1TH 5:23

Being able to see God

HEB 12:14

title: 6747: sanctification, and Holy Spirit
content: See 3233: Holy Spirit, and sanctification

title: 6750: sin-bearer
content:
A person or animal that acts in God's sight in a substitutionary capacity, to whom are transferred the sins of others, together with the corresponding penalty for those sins.

The principle of accountability necessitates a sin-bearer

All are held responsible for their own sin and its consequences

LEV 5:17 See also LEV 5:1, LEV 20:16–20:21, LEV 24:15–24:16; EZK 18:19–18:20

Individuals and nations come under God's judgment for their sin

EZK 4:4–4:6 Ezekiel's behaviour foreshadowed God's judgment on the sin of Israel and Judah. See also EZK 16:58, EZK 18:4; MAT 11:21–11:24 parallel LUK 10:12–10:15; ROM 1:18–1:32, ROM 6:23

God's gracious provision of a sin-bearer in the OT

God takes away the sin of those who repent

ISA 44:22 See also 2SA 12:13; PSA 103:12; ZEC 3:4

The sacrifice of an animal to remove sin

LEV 5:5–5:6 See also LEV 4:1–4:35, LEV 5:14–6:7

The Day of Atonement and the removal of sin

LEV 16:8–16:10 The sacrificial goat expiated the sins of the people in the sight of God; the driving away of the scapegoat into the desert symbolised the removal of the sin of the community. See also LEV 16:15–16:19 the sin offering; LEV 16:20–16:22 the scapegoat

The function of the priests in the sin-bearing process

The priests were responsible for the sanctuary

NUM 18:1–18:7

Strict regulations governed the performance of their service

LEV 22:9 See also EXO 28:42–28:43, EXO 30:17–30:21; LEV 16:3–16:4

The priests bore the sin of the people in a representative capacity

EXO 28:36–28:38 See also LEV 10:17

Jesus Christ as sin-bearer

Jesus Christ bore the sins of the world on the cross

HEB 9:28 See also ISA 53:4–53:6, ISA 53:10–53:12; JHN 1:29; COL 2:14; HEB 10:11–10:14; 1PE 2:24; 1JN 3:5

Jesus Christ's intercessory role as high priest

1JN 2:1 See also ROM 8:34; HEB 7:25

title: 6752: substitution
content:
Jesus Christ died as the substitute for sinful humanity, taking humanity's place on the cross.

OT images of substitution

Examples of substitution

GEN 22:13 See also NUM 3:12–3:13, NUM 3:41, NUM 3:45, NUM 8:18; 1SA 17:9; 1KI 20:42; LAM 5:7; EZK 4:4

Substitutes atoning for sin or acting as sin bearers

LEV 1:4 See also EXO 28:38; LEV 16:21–16:22, LEV 22:19–22:20

Anticipations of the substitutionary work of Jesus Christ

ISA 53:4–53:6 NT writers have interpreted the servant as Jesus Christ: see Mt 8:17 and 1Pe 2:23–25. See also PSA 69:9

The substitutionary work of Jesus Christ

Jesus Christ died for believers

MRK 10:45; ROM 5:6, ROM 5:8; 1TH 5:10 See also JHN 10:11, JHN 11:50; ROM 14:15; 2CO 5:14; GAL 2:20, GAL 3:13; 1TI 2:6; TIT 2:14; HEB 2:9; 1PE 2:21; 1JN 3:16

Jesus Christ died for sin

1PE 2:24, 1PE 3:18 See also 2CO 5:21; HEB 9:28

Jesus Christ's substitution as an example

2CO 5:15 See also 1PE 2:21

Jesus Christ's substitution as an act of love

JHN 15:13 See also EXO 32:32; ROM 9:3

title: 6754: union with Christ
content: The sharing of believers in the life of Jesus Christ by faith, allowing them to share in all the benefits and riches that result from his person and work.

title: 6755: union with Christ, nature of
content:
The nature of the union of Jesus Christ with believers is explained using a number of central images. The NT stresses the reality, closeness and considerable benefits of this union.

A covenantal relationship

OT background

ISA 54:5–54:8 The marriage covenant is used to illustrate the intimacy of the relationship between God and his people. See also JER 3:14, JER 31:32; HOS 2:7, HOS 2:16

In the NT

EPH 5:31–5:32

A relationship deepened through the incarnation

JHN 1:14 See also ROM 8:3; 2CO 5:21; GAL 4:4; PHP 2:7–2:8; 1TI 3:16; HEB 2:14–2:18

A relationship personally entered through faith

JHN 3:16 See also JHN 1:12, JHN 2:11, JHN 7:38; ACT 16:31; ROM 3:22, ROM 8:1, ROM 10:9–10:10; 2CO 5:17; GAL 2:16

A relationship enriched through the sacraments

Baptism

ROM 6:3–6:5; GAL 3:27 See also MAT 28:19; ROM 6:8; COL 2:11–2:12

The Lord's Supper

MAT 26:26–26:28 parallels MRK 14:22–14:24, LUK 22:17–22:20; ; 1CO 10:16, 1CO 11:23–11:29

A relationship that affects every aspect of life

1CO 6:17 See also JHN 15:1–15:8, JHN 17:20–17:26; ROM 8:9–8:11; 1CO 6:19–6:20; GAL 2:20

It is a mysterious relationship

COL 1:27 See also EPH 5:32

title: 6756: union with Christ, significance of
content:
The union between Jesus Christ and believers is the basis for all exhortation to faithful, holy living, as well as to sharing in all his benefits.

For the believer

Identification with Jesus Christ

MAT 10:40 See also MAT 18:5, MAT 25:40; EPH 1:1; PHP 1:1

The experience of being in Christ

ROM 8:10; 2CO 5:17 See also JHN 14:20; 2CO 13:5; GAL 2:20; EPH 3:14–3:19; COL 1:27

Conformity to Jesus Christ

COL 3:1–3:4 See also MAT 16:24 parallels MRK 8:34, LUK 9:23; ROM 6:5; 1CO 6:15–6:17; 2CO 4:10; EPH 2:6; PHP 3:10–3:11; 1PE 4:13; 1JN 2:6, 1JN 2:28, 1JN 3:24, 1JN 5:20

Righteousness in Christ

ROM 4:23–4:25; 2CO 5:21 See also ROM 1:17; 1CO 1:30

Blessing in Christ

EPH 1:3–1:14

For the church

In persecution

ACT 9:3–9:5 See also ACT 22:6–22:8, ACT 26:14

In service

1CO 12:12 See also ROM 12:4–12:8; 1CO 12:27; EPH 1:22–1:23, EPH 5:28–5:30; COL 3:15
